AI-First since December 2023.
Position: Data Analyst (A/B Testing & Experimentation)
Total Annual Package: Up to AED 348,000 (Tax-Free) + comprehensive benefits
• Performance bonus: up to 10% of base, paid monthly
Location: Dubai, UAE (On-site) with relocation assistance and visa support
About the Role:

If you spend your days running A/B tests, analyzing conversion funnels, and digging into why users drop off before checkout — and you write your own SQL and Python to get those answers — this role is built for you. General analysts and program managers without hands-on technical and experimentation skills won't be a fit.

You'll own Puffy's experimentation program from hypothesis to business recommendation. You pull data from Shopify, GA4, Google Ads, and BigQuery yourself. You design the test, run the statistical analysis, and tell the business what to change and why. Every recommendation you deliver has to be clear enough that marketing, UX, and development can act on it the same week.
The Career Accelerator:

Puffy runs a high volume of experiments across a $1B+ e-commerce platform. You'll be involved in more A/B tests, conversion analyses, and data pipeline builds in your first year than most analysts see in three. You'll work across departments — marketing, UX, development, operations — which means broader context and faster pattern recognition. The pace is demanding, and it rewards people who are genuinely curious and want to sharpen their craft quickly.
What Is In It For You:
This isn't just another analytics role. This is your chance to become a true industry architect.

1. Own the Full Experimentation Cycle — Not Just the Dashboard. Most data analysts inherit someone else's reporting setup and maintain it. Here, you own hypothesis generation, test design, statistical analysis, and the business recommendation that follows. You build your own pipelines across Shopify, GA4, Google Ads, and BigQuery — no waiting in a queue for data, no presenting metrics without a point of view. You still report to the manager, but the experimentation output is yours to drive. Within 12 months, you'll have more hands-on testing reps than analysts accumulate in three years elsewhere.
2. Join the Elite Organization and Partner with the 1%:
🏆 Top 5 Mattress Brand in the USA with Over $1 Billion USD in Sales
🌍 Great Place to Work Certified
💡 Work with Forbes Council Member CEO Gain firsthand exposure to strategic decisions and high-velocity execution that drives a global brand.

3. Experience a World-Class Lifestyle. Dubai offers a tax-free salary, world-class infrastructure, year-round sunshine, and a global lifestyle that most professionals only experience later in their careers. Your entire compensation package lands in your account — no deductions, no surprises.

4. Become the Person Who Knows Why Things Convert. In most companies, analysts report numbers. Here, you're the person who tells the business what to change and why. When a test wins or loses, your analysis is the reason leadership moves. You'll build the instinct to separate signal from noise across segments, devices, and traffic sources — and the communication skills to make non-technical stakeholders act on what you find.
What You'll Need:
3+ years in data analysis, experimentation, or CRO within e-commerce, D2C, or any high-traffic transactional website (fintech, marketplaces, SaaS, banking)
Strong SQL — complex joins, window functions, CTEs across large datasets without relying on pre-built dashboards
Working Python (or R) for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and automation
Proven A/B testing experience — hypothesis design through statistical analysis to business recommendation
column2: solid grasp of sample sizing, significance thresholds, and common pitfalls

column1: Hands-on GA4 experience — event-based tracking, custom explorations, and the ability to tie GA4 data to other sources
Experience integrating ad platform data (Google Ads, Meta) with site analytics to see the full acquisition-to-conversion funnel
Ability to independently connect to and pull from multiple data sources (APIs, warehouses, flat files) without engineering support
Sharp analytical communication — complex findings distilled into concise recommendations for non-technical stakeholders
Proactive and curious — you spot testing opportunities and chase root causes before anyone asks
Good-to-Have:
Experience with BigQuery or AWS data warehousing, including scheduled queries and data modeling
Familiarity with session replay and heatmap tools (FullStory, Hotjar) for qualitative behavioral analysis
Background in Looker, Tableau, or similar dashboarding tools
The Puffy DNA:
We're restless, perpetually hungry, and fast-paced, driven by an unwavering belief that we can outperform any competitor — regardless of their size.
Be the Owner: We don't hire employees
column2: we welcome owners. You're accountable for results, not activity.

column1: Execute with Urgency: We thrive in high-stakes environments by making thoughtful decisions quickly and acting decisively.
Demand Excellence: We solve the hard problems that others can't, pushing the boundaries of our craft.
Be AI-First: Daily multi-turn AI conversations challenge your assumptions.
Go All-In: When the mission demands it, we rally as one team to cross the finish line.
✨ Your Total Compensation & Benefits:
💰 Compensation:
Monthly performance bonuses (up to 10%)
Tax-free income
Annual return flight allowance to your home country
Parking allowance (up to AED 7,800 annually)
🏥 Health & Protection:
Premium worldwide health coverage (upto AED 1Million)
Life insurance & Personal Accident Insurance (24x monthly salary)
🌴 Time Off:
22 working days annual leave + UAE public holidays
🌍 Relocation Support (International Hires):
UAE work visa sponsorship
International flights & airport transfers
14-day 4-star hotel accommodation
💼 Work Environment:
AI First organization with access to AI-native tool stack
Premium office environment in Dubai Internet City
Learning & development opportunities
International team collaboration (14+ nationalities)
🏆 Rewards & Recognition:
Eligibility for selection to the Leaders Club — an elite recognition program featuring premium wellness memberships, exclusive networking with senior leadership, and annual leadership retreats
Eligibility for nomination as 'We Are' Champion of the Month — monthly recognition with gift vouchers and company-wide celebration for exceptional contributions
✅ Other Benefits:
End of Service Gratuity per UAE law
The alternative is another year where your potential is capped and your best hypotheses die in a backlog no one reads.

4430558033,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-at-netent-4430558033?position=15&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=d4ko0G7%2Fa9Edvnf47D305g%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst,NetEnt,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Company Description

Evolution RNG is the dedicated slots division of Evolution, a world-leading innovator in online casino solutions. We bring together the industry’s most creative and successful slot brands under one powerhouse umbrella: Nolimit City, NetEnt, Red Tiger, Big Time Gaming, Sneaky Slots and Tap-A-Roo. Our mission is to build scalable, high-impact entertainment for players and operators worldwide while pushing the boundaries of slot mechanics and player engagement. By balancing creative product development with operational excellence, we deliver top-tier gaming content across the globe.
Job Description

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join the Evolution RNG Division in Stockholm. This role will operate at divisional level and be responsible for shaping, delivering and governing key data capabilities that support decision-making, operational effectiveness and risk management across the RNG organization. The successful candidate will be a booster for teams across Commercial, Product, Fraud, Customer Support, Engineering and Operations. This person will be expected to bring structure, ownership and forward-looking thinking to critical data assets, reporting, monitoring routines and governance practices across RNG.
Key Responsibilities
Business Intelligence & Data Analytics
Deliver data-driven insights and analysis to support commercial performance, product development, operational efficiency, and fraud prevention across the RNG division.
Develop, maintain, and enhance reporting solutions and Tableau dashboards, ensuring data accuracy, accessibility, and effective governance.
Translate business requirements into scalable datasets, reporting frameworks, and process improvements that support informed decision-making.
Lead and contribute to data-related initiatives and projects, collaborating with stakeholders across the organization to deliver business value.
Develop and improve fraud monitoring capabilities, identifying anomalies, player exploits, defects, and suspicious behavior across RNG products.
Drive continuous improvements in data quality, governance, monitoring processes, and operational routines.
Fraud Monitoring
Further develop fraud monitoring toolset and data framework across all RNG game providers.
Drive the further advancement of fraud detection capabilities, ensuring that monitoring approaches remain effective as game complexity evolves.
Work towards improving the scope, accuracy and effectiveness of the fraud detection toolset and related monitoring processes.
Help improve monitoring practices, operational routines and data structures that enable earlier detection of anomalies, player exploits, defects and suspicious behaviour.
Qualifications
Several years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, analytics engineering or a comparable senior data role.
Strong SQL capability and proven experience building analytical datasets, reporting layers and decision-support solutions.
Demonstrated experience working with BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Superset or similar platforms, including ownership of reporting assets and user enablement.
Ability to operate credibly with senior stakeholders and to balance hands-on delivery with broader ownership and prioritization.
Experience working with data governance, data quality improvement and cross-functional collaboration in complex environments.
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail and an ability to work effectively in ambiguous or evolving problem spaces.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and spoken, with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to technical and non-technical audiences.
A proactive, structured and outcome-focused mindset with the ability to independently drive work forward.
Nice to Have
Experience with AWS technologies such as Athena
Experience with data management, data catalogues or operational data tools such as no-code database platforms.
Exposure to fraud analytics, anomaly detection, game monitoring or risk-related analytical domains.
Experience with Python or other scripting languages for automation and advanced analysis.
Experience with version control and development best practices such as Git and CI/CD workflows.
Background in gaming, iGaming or other digitally native, data-rich environments.
Additional Information
Our benefits are
Competitive pension, health and medical insurance
30 paid vacation days
Fitness benefit (Friskvårdsbidrag)
Professional growth
Sport activities, parties, team buildings
This is a 100% on-site position. Based in Stockholm, Sweden.

  • Several years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, analytics engineering or a comparable senior data role.
  • Strong SQL capability and proven experience building analytical datasets, reporting layers and decision-support solutions.
  • Demonstrated experience working with BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Superset or similar platforms, including ownership of reporting assets and user enablement.
  • Ability to operate credibly with senior stakeholders and to balance hands-on delivery with broader ownership and prioritization.
  • Experience working with data governance, data quality improvement and cross-functional collaboration in complex environments.
  • Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail and an ability to work effectively in ambiguous or evolving problem spaces.
  • Excellent communication skills in English, both written and spoken, with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to technical and non-technical audiences.
  • A proactive, structured and outcome-focused mindset with the ability to independently drive work forward.


Nice to Have

  • Experience with AWS technologies such as Athena
  • Experience with data management, data catalogues or operational data tools such as no-code database platforms.
  • Exposure to fraud analytics, anomaly detection, game monitoring or risk-related analytical domains.
  • Experience with Python or other scripting languages for automation and advanced analysis.
  • Experience with version control and development best practices such as Git and CI/CD workflows.
  • Background in gaming, iGaming or other digitally native, data-rich environments.



Additional Information

Our benefits are

  • Competitive pension, health and medical insurance
  • 30 paid vacation days
  • Fitness benefit (Friskvårdsbidrag)
  • Professional growth
  • Sport activities, parties, team buildings



This is a 100% on-site position. Based in Stockholm, Sweden.

We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.

Finance, Risk, and Compliance are undergoing a major transformation—driven by business and regulatory changes as well as significant shifts in architectural design. We are therefore looking for a Risk Data Modeller with experience in data modelling to develop an integrated data architecture that meets regulatory requirements and business needs. You will be a vital part of our community driving the Finance, Risk, and Compliance agenda in the bank. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, this opportunity is for you!
About the role:

As a Risk Data Modeller in our team, you will take responsibility for the data architecture within the risk area. Your work will involve a high degree of complexity, both from a business and data perspective as well as from a people and process perspective. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders and architects to:
Capture and interpret business and regulatory requirements driven by our major initiatives.

Develop an integrated data architecture for Risk that supports all risk types, ensuring data is consistent, integrated, traceable, and fit for regulatory and business use, while connecting to the domain‑wide data architecture.

Develop business information and logical data models that align with business needs and integrate with business and application architectures, supporting metadata capture, consistency, lineage, and traceability across the data landscape.
Support the definition of business terms, and ensure they are correctly represented, consistently used, and traceable across models, data flows, and solutions.
Ensure compliance with BCBS 239, Basel/CRR, IFRS, and other applicable regulatory requirements, and alignment with the domain architecture.
Collaborate with stakeholders from the risk organisation, data management professionals, and architects across Finance, Risk, and Compliance.
To thrive in this role, we believe you have:
Strong professional background in risk management within a banking or financial services (e.g., credit, market, counterparty, or liquidity risk).

Competence in developing ontologies, business information models, and logical data models (including normalised and dimensional models), as well as data definitions, data mapping specifications, and data flows.
Good understanding of data warehouse architectures and methodologies, and familiarity with the Data Mesh framework and data products.
Knowledge of regulatory and data management requirements in Finance and Risk areas, and how data architecture enables compliance and governance.
Experience with BCBS 239 (Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting, RDARR) would be an advantage.
Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills.
Ability to collaborate effectively with a positive, solution‑oriented mindset.
Excellent communication and documentation skills to convey complex ideas effectively to both business and IT professionals.
Fluency in English.
What we offer:
A key role in one of SEB’s most challenging areas
Innovative working environment with highly motivated colleagues
Agile and modern ways of working
Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
Attractive compensation and access to SEB staff banking with exclusive benefits
Do you want to be part of SEB?

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial to our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than July 6th.

Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.

4429959067,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/trainee-commercial-operations-insights-at-nornorm-4429959067?position=18&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=1FSWoOt4ftW6n395j%2FYjdQ%3D%3D,Trainee - Commercial Operations & Insights,NORNORM,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Purpose of the Role

NORNORM is scaling across Europe, and our commercial and delivery teams work from one shared set of data. This is a development role for someone early in their career who wants to grow into real ownership. You'll start by learning and supporting the commercial systems and performance dashboards the teams across four regions rely on, and over time take on more of running them yourself. It's an analytical, hands-on role with a clear path from supported work to genuine ownership. It will suit someone who enjoys making systems run cleanly and turning data into decisions the business acts on.
Job description

You'll run, and over time own, the day-to-day of our core commercial systems and produce the KPIs and dashboards our regional teams and leadership review on an ongoing basis. We work with AI tools as part of how we operate, and we're looking for someone keen to build on that. As you ramp, you'll take on more independent ownership and support the build of new capabilities as the function grows, working closely with commercial, delivery and supply teams across Europe.
Key responsibilities:
Run and maintain our core commercial systems day to day: configuration, data quality, automation and integrations, and the fixes that keep them working for the teams who depend on them.
Build and maintain the KPIs and dashboards in our analytics platform that the regional teams and leadership review.
Produce and progressively automate the performance reporting across all four regions.
Support new builds as the function grows
column2: within sales, retention, delivery, operations, and self–serve analytics.

column1: Success criteria (2026):
You've got up to speed quickly on our systems and ways of working, and you're using new tools, including AI, to do the work better over time.
Our commercial systems run reliably and the teams trust the data in them.
The regional teams can steer on data they trust, available how and when they need it. As much of this as possible runs self-serve and automated, rather than produced by hand each month.
The dashboards and metrics the teams need get delivered and actually adopted.
What we're looking for:
This is a graduate trainee position, we expect a bachelor's degree in business, economics, or a related field.
An open, curious mind and real enthusiasm for working with AI and new tools. You need to be the kind of person who wants to try new systems, learn fast, and find better ways to work.
Strong analytical skills, comfortable working with data and building dashboards or reports people can rely on.
Self-directed, organized and reliable, with a genuine ownership mindset.
Fluent English
column2: able to work from Stockholm.

column1: A plus, but not required:
Experience administering commercial or CRM systems (configuration, automation, integrations).
SQL or hands-on experience with a BI tool.
Experience in a fast-scaling or international company.
What we offer you:
A 6-month traineeship where we mentor and help you grow as a professional, with the aim to hire on a full-time basis based on the performance success criteria.
If you are driven, determined and want to grow within an exciting scaleup, this role is for you. Come and join us!

4408014168,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/insights-and-measurement-lead-amazon-ads-at-amazon-4408014168?position=19&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=jmjTX79S277lH59Y8Jad%2BA%3D%3D,"Insights and Measurement Lead, Amazon Ads",Amazon,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Description

Amazon Ads is the digital advertising arm of Amazon and one of our fastest growing businesses. We operate at the intersection of commerce, entertainment and advertising, offering unique advertising products, enterprise ad tech and a rich array of unique creative, format and measurement solutions. We offer advertisers premium brand placements on our owned and operated properties, including Prime Video ads, Twitch, and a rich slate of retail media solutions.

As an Insights and Measurement Lead, you will work as a senior individual contributor to accelerate our advertising business growth in the Nordics and Benelux regions. With Prime Video's launch in Sweden and Netherlands, you'll have a pivotal role in shaping the streaming TV and video landscape in the region. You will serve as a strategic advisor, implementing analytics solutions to drive business transformation. You will lead Ad measurement discussions, create actionable insights, and present clear recommendations to executive stakeholders.
Key job responsibilities
Leverage Amazon's proprietary data, third-party research, and ad measurement solutions to generate actionable insights
Own senior-level measurement and insights customer relationships, partnering across strategic learning objectives that influence business decision-making
Collaborate cross-functionally to influence sales and marketing initiatives across multiple business functions and geographies
Advocate for data-driven decision-making and guide advertisers to implement recommended solutions
A day in the life

On a daily basis, the Insights and Measurement Lead performs hands-on data extraction and analysis using SQL and Python, leveraging Amazon measurement solutions to build insights and track success metrics for advertisers' businesses and industries. The role requires developing data-driven recommendations for executive client leadership and communicating those insights in a clear and compelling manner.
About The Team

The Analytics & Insights (A&I) organization leverages Amazon's data to develop actionable insights for advertisers, enhancing marketers' decision-making and measurably improving their success both on and off Amazon.
Basic Qualifications
7+ years of external customer facing analytics role.
Experience in customer segmentation, customer behavior analysis, data science modeling.
Working experience with data analysis tools SQL, Python/R.
Experience in advertising measurement methodologies, including incrementality, marketing mix modeling, and media attribution.
Preferred Qualifications
Excellent Swedish and English communication skills.
Experience with advertising publishers and marketing/creative agencies
Advanced degree (MBA, Master's) in Economics, Marketing, Statistics, Advertising, or Business
column2: or equivalent Digital media planning experience

4426392349,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-at-epidemic-sound-4426392349?position=20&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=DFqRtCeOWx%2B2i4Hn%2BcmE4A%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst,Epidemic Sound,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Join our global force of 400+ innovators, blending the latest in tech with the greatest in soundtracking — from our Stockholm HQ to offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris, Oslo, and Seoul. We're an industry leader with a startup mentality. We take what we do seriously, but we don't take ourselves too seriously. Come create with us, and let the world feel your work.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll join an embedded, AI-native analytics team that sits inside the commercial and product domains driving our growth. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the CPO and product teams as we evolve from a music-library product into a broader creative-workflow platform, defining what success looks like for surfaces that don't exist yet and proving it with data.
What you'll do
Partner with the CPO and product teams to define success metrics, surface growth opportunities, and shape product strategy with evidence.

Experienced experimentalist. Fluent in A/B testing and causal inference.

4430944062,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/junior-data-engineer-at-playground-tech-4430944062?position=21&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=kD%2BOY1csTXab6P%2FYTrhw8A%3D%3D,Junior Data Engineer,Playground Tech,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Playground Data
Stockholm
Hybrid
Junior Data Engineer
The Playgorund Pitch

Some data problems are neat and tidy. Ours usually aren’t. They are messy, evolving, and tied to real decisions. We work closely with our clients to understand what actually needs to be solved and build from there.

At Playground Data, we build modern data platforms for analytics, AI and machine learning. We are a collaborative team that values solid engineering and shared growth. Our engineers take ownership and work closely with clients to deliver high-quality solutions.
What You'll Actually Do

You'll join our client engagements as part of a team, working on data platforms that matter to the business. Many of our projects are greenfield builds or major platform transformations, so there's plenty to learn from people who've done it before. This role suits someone early in their data engineering career who wants to build real things and grow fast.
In Practice, You'll
Build and maintain data pipelines that ingest, model, and structure data for analytics, AI and ML workloads.
Work alongside senior engineers and pick up how modern data platforms are designed and operated.
Take growing ownership of your own tasks as you find your feet, with support whenever you need it.
Contribute to how we work internally, share ideas, and help improve our data engineering frameworks and ways of working.
You'll never sit idle, there's always something meaningful to build, learn, or untangle.
Who are you?

You're early in your data engineering journey, maybe fresh out of school or with a first project or two behind you, and you already know that data doesn't magically organize itself. Python and SQL are tools you're comfortable with and keen to get sharper at, and you've had your first taste of a modern data stack (AWS, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake etc.).
You Would Probably Describe Yourself As Someone Who
Wants to understand data engineering in practice: You're eager to learn how data flows, scales, and holds up in production for analytics and AI and ML workloads.
Enjoys building from scratch: Greenfield work doesn't scare you, it motivates you.
Stays curious when things are unclear: You don't panic when requirements are fuzzy and the system diagram needs three whiteboards, you ask questions and dig in.
Wants to grow into a consultant role: You're up for customer dialogue and learning how to guide clients, with senior colleagues alongside you.
As a person, you're humble, genuinely curious about technology, and motivated, while knowing that asking for help is a strength, not a weakness. Friendly, curious, and playful.
You've graduated from KTH, Stockholms Tekniska Institut, Nackademin or a similar program in a relevant field.
Language requirement

You need to be fluent in Swedish and able to use it confidently in a professional environment. Why? Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements.
Let's Play!
Applying to us is a breeze!
Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
Answer the choice-questions (no ""please-rewrite-your-resume"" nonsense, promise)
Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us
Note
Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support. For this role applicants must have a swedish or EU-citizenship.
Welcome to Playground!

At Playground, you work on real, complex assignments for well-established and ambitious clients, not bench projects or short-lived experiments. Our teams are trusted with production systems, platforms, and data that actually matter. Here growth comes from ownership, curiosity, and doing the work properly. You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time. Playground brings together specialized domains under one umbrella, with a shared culture and way of working, and easy access to broad technical expertise when it helps move the work forward.

4433397875,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/junior-data-engineer-at-cgi-4433397875?position=24&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=sUcEQLSRfq4yaJ2U6nn%2BzA%3D%3D,Junior Data Engineer,CGI,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Beskrivning av tjänsten

Du kommer att ingå i ett team som arbetar med dataplattformar, analys och AI baserade lösningar i komplexa miljöer. Du bidrar till att skapa strukturerande, tillförlitlig och användbar data som stödjer verksamhetens analys, planering och beslutsfattande.

AI är ett naturligt inslag i lösningarna, exempelvis inom analys, prediktion och automatisering. Arbetet sker i nära samarbete med både verksamhet och tekniska team och syftar till att omsätta data till konkret värde för våra kunder.
Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer huvudsakligen att finnas inom:
Data management och informationsmodeller
Data engineering och ETL/ELT flöden
Business Intelligence och analys
AI och maskininlärningsbaserade lösningar
Molnbaserade dataplattformar
Datakvalitet, governance och grundläggande datastyrning
(Exempel på tekniker och plattformar kan vara Azure, Snowflake, Databricks, Power BI, dbt, Python)
Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
Vi söker dig som har 2–4 års erfarenhet inom dataanalys, Data engineering eller Business Intelligence, samt en teknisk och analytisk grund att bygga vidare på.
Vi ser gärna att du har en relevant utbildning inom området.
Du är nyfiken på AI och moderna dataplattformar, vill förstå helheten och har ett driv att utvecklas. Vi ser att du har erfarenhet eller grundläggande kunskap inom flera av följande områden:
SQL och arbete med datapipelines (ETL/ELT)
Molnbaserade dataplattformar (t.ex. Azure, Snowflake, Databricks)
Analys och visualiseringsverktyg (t.ex. Power BI)
Programmering eller scripting, exempelvis Python
Grundläggande förståelse för AI, data science eller maskininlärning
Analytiskt arbetssätt och förmåga att koppla data till verksamhetsbehov
Som person är du strukturerad, samarbetsinriktad och gillar att arbeta i team där teknik och verksamhet möts.
Start: September 2026
Urval sker löpande och tjänsten kan tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag
Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.
Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.

Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.

Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.

Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

  • Grundläggande förståelse för AI, data science eller maskininlärning
  • Analytiskt arbetssätt och förmåga att koppla data till verksamhetsbehov

Som person är du strukturerad, samarbetsinriktad och gillar att arbeta i team där teknik och verksamhet möts.

  • Start: September 2026
  • Urval sker löpande och tjänsten kan tillsättas innan sista ansökningsdag

Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.

Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.

Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.

Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.

Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.

Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

4429962831,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-angry-birds-2-at-rovio-entertainment-4429962831?position=25&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=35ZT6K8LEd4VlPn3Di%2Fvig%3D%3D,"Senior Data Analyst, Angry Birds 2",Rovio Entertainment,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Rovio is part of the SEGA family and world famous for our flagship IP Angry Birds - want to know more about Rovio as an employer? Click here.

Data is at the heart of everything we do at Rovio. It enables us to continually improve our games and provide incredible experiences for the millions of users who play our games every day. All of this data is only valuable if it can be used to guide our day to day operations and the mission of the Data and Analytics craft is to turn that raw data into actionable insights.

We are now looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our flagship Angry Birds 2 game in the Stockholm studio. Coming into this role, you will build an understanding of the game and different business levers and use that knowledge to build new game features, optimising existing ones, structure vague problem statements in collaboration with key stakeholders. You will be working as a part of a live ops pod, supporting it as a dedicated team member.

The team is located in our studio in the centre of Stockholm and works in a hybrid mode (3 days per week onsite). Currently not based in Sweden? No problem at all, we will help you with the relocation and work permit if needed!
You will have impact and fun at work by:
Working with the team members of your pod, participating in the end to end process of new feature development from planning, research and testing to launch and post launch evaluation
Bringing an objective perspective and recommendations to all phases of decision making, leveraging your technical skills to mine raw data for valuable information
Constantly designing and running in-game A/B test experiments, helping the team to find options leading to better game monetization performance and higher audience satisfaction
Creating dashboards and reports to help the game team understand your findings
Proactively working with data exploration and looking for opportunities to improve gameplay experience
Working closely with peer Data Analysts, Product Managers, Designers and Producers to continuously improve how we are interpreting game metrics and making decisions
Coaching and sharing best practices between colleagues in the data craft and whole game team
Experience and skills we are looking for:
High proficiency in SQL
Proficiency in scripting languages such as Python or R
Solid understanding of statistics (hypothesis testing, regression, analysing bias)
Experience in product analysis and experiments design including A/B testing
Excellent presentation and collaboration skills with proven ability to present data and provide actionable recommendations to senior stakeholders
Prior experience as an analyst in mobile games or apps with large audiences
Passion for playing games and understanding what makes a successful feature
You'll work in a hybrid setup with two to three days at the office per week. Kindly send us your CV in English. We look forward to speaking to you!
We welcome applicants to use the name they want to be addressed by in the application form, regardless of whether it is their legal name.

At Rovio, we are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities or conditions in our working environment and job application procedures. We are committed to continuously improving workplace accessibility. If you have any questions before applying, please write to us at recruitment@rovio.com.

4432265692,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-sveriges-riksbank-4432265692?position=26&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=Fu7%2BSE3%2FV6bNGaYHVR7hAA%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Sveriges riksbank,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Brinner du för att arbeta med data och dataprodukter och vill samtidigt arbeta i samhällets tjänst för en stark och säker ekonomi? Då är denna tjänst för dig! Vi söker dig som vill använda din tekniska kompetens för att fortsätta utveckla hur Riksbanken arbetar med data och analys med fokus på tillgångsförvaltning och betalningar.
VI ERBJUDER DIG

Du får möjlighet att vidareutveckla din tekniska kompetens samtidigt som du fördjupar din kunskap inom Riksbankens verksamhetsområden så att vi utvecklar lösningar som är enkla att ta till sig och som gör nytta i verksamheten. Du möter utmaningar och blir en viktig del i vår förändringsresa - från att utveckla AI och automatisera dataflöden till att bygga robusta IT-lösningar, förstärka cybersäkerhet och bygga framtidens betalningssystem. Här löser du komplexa problem och utvecklas i en prestigelös miljö som präglas av samarbete, nyfikenhet, lärande och mod.
DET HÄR GÖR DU HOS OSS

Riksbankens Data Och Statistik Över Tillgångsförvaltning Och Betalningar Består Till Stor Del Av Mikro- Och Transaktionsdata. Betalningsstatistiken Från Finansiella Institut Är i En Uppbyggnadsfas Och Det Innebär Att Du Är Med Och Utformar Dataflöden Från Grunden. Du Arbetar i Ett Agilt Team Med Helhetsansvar För Sin Data Och Sina Dataprodukter. Konkret Innebär Rollen Att Du
ansvarar för design och utveckling av dataflöden för bearbetning av stora och detaljerade datamängder
skriver automatiska tester för att kvalitetssäkra din kod och underhåller CI/CD-pipelines
utvecklar API:er för att tillgängliggöra data
använder marimo notebooks för att visualisera och tillgängliggöra resultat
bidrar till att utveckla hur Riksbanken använder AI i sin verksamhet
DITT TEAM

Dina närmaste teammedlemmar utgörs av två av Riksbankens datateam där olika kompetenser arbetar sida vid sida och lär av varandra. Datateamen är en del av Riksbankens tvärfunktionella verksamhetsområde Data och Statistik och består av ett femtital medarbetare. Tillsammans med datateamen för du en nära dialog med relevanta policyavdelningar för att leverera data och dataprodukter som är användarvänliga, robusta och värdeskapande. Organisatoriskt kommer du att tillhöra avdelningen för marknader. Här kan du läsa mer om Riksbankens organisation.
MER OM DIG
Vi Ser Att Du Har
en relevant akademisk examen (exempelvis inom data science, matematik, programmering eller liknande)
erfarenhet av att utveckla dataflöden i orkestreringsverktyg som Dagster eller liknande (Airflow, dbt, etc.)
minst 2 års erfarenhet av att koda i Python eller motsvarande programmeringsspråk
erfarenhet av att hantera och bearbeta stora datamängder
goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både skriftligt och muntligt.
Utöver ovan grundkrav är det meriterande om du även har:
erfarenhet av containermiljöer som Podman eller Kubernetes
erfarenhet av att arbeta med datalake-lagring så som S3 och LakeFS
erfarenhet av att arbeta enligt DevOps-principer med fokus på automatisering och CI/CD
erfarenhet från finans- eller fintechbranschen.
erfarenhet av att arbeta agilt
Vi söker dig som är driven, ansvarsfull, kvalitetsmedveten, har gott omdöme och är bra på att samarbeta. Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
MER OM TJÄNSTEN
Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse
Anställningstyp: Tillsvidareanställning, provanställning kan komma att tillämpas
Placering: Kontor i centrala Stockholm
Arbetstid: Förtroendearbetstid
Möjlighet att jobba distans: 1 eller 2 dagar i veckan
Förmåner: Generösa semestervillkor, löneväxling till pension, subventionerad lunch, friskvårdsbidrag, subventionerad massage och tillgång till gym och ledarledda träningspass.

Tjänsten kan komma att placeras i säkerhetsklass och en säkerhetsprövning innefattande registerkontroll, säkerhetsprövningssamtal och särskild personutredning görs då för slutkandidaten. För vissa tjänster ställs krav på svenskt medborgarskap.
ATT JOBBA PÅ RIKSBANKEN

Riksbanken är en myndighet under riksdagen som arbetar på medborgarnas uppdrag för en stark och säker ekonomi. Som landets centralbank har vi en nyckelroll för individer, hushåll och företag i Sverige och i över 350 år har vi utforskat nya vägar för att vara i framkant – från kopparplåtmynt till AI. Oavsett inom vilket område din kompetens finns – är det med stolthet vi tar oss an våra uppdrag. Tillsammans arbetar vi för en låg och stabil inflation, bidrar till ett effektivt och stabilt finansiellt system och säkerställer att betalningar fungerar smidigt, även i kriser. Vi ger också ut Sveriges sedlar och mynt. Läs mer om hur det är att arbeta på Riksbanken här .
ANSÖK

Vi vill ha din ansökan med CV senast den 17 augusti. Vi vill att du i denna rekrytering besvarar ett antal urvalsfrågor. På så vis kan du enkelt söka tjänsten och vi ges möjligheten att utvärdera våra kandidater på lika villkor. Arbetsprov kan komma att ingå som en del av rekryteringsprocessen. Vi genomför också bakgrundskontroller på slutkandidaterna, vilket innebär att vi kontrollerar examensbevis eller annat dokument som styrker din utbildning.
Du kan läsa mer om hur det fungerar när du söker jobb på Riksbanken här . Vi ser fram emot att få kontakt med dig!

4425624402,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-research-assistant-part-time-at-yougov-4425624402?position=27&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=H9gboBWkY4mRb4j1Ljzkaw%3D%3D,Student Research Assistant (Part-Time),YouGov,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","YouGov is an international online research data and analytics technology group. Our innovative solutions help the world’s most recognised brands, media owners and agencies to plan, activate and track their marketing activities better. With operations in the UK, the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, India and Asia Pacific, we have one of the world's largest research networks. At the core of our platform is an ever-growing source of connected consumer data that has developed daily over our 20 years of operation. We call it living data. All of our products and services draw upon this detailed understanding of our 20+ million registered panel members to deliver accurate, actionable consumer insights. Job description You will be supporting fast-turnaround research projects on many different topics, which is a part of YouGov’s Client Service offering.

As Student Research Assistant, you will support Research Executives on various tasks. The role relies on excellent communication skills. You’ll be working closely with other YouGov teams as well as directly with clients to design and deliver research which is in line with the brief.
Your primary responsibility will be:
Proof-reading of client-based questionnaires.
Checking and adjustment of questionnaire scripting instructions.
Scripting of surveys in YouGov’s own self-service tool.
Checking of questionnaire test links before launch.
Data output checking and delivery for clients.
Supporting on custom report preparations.
These are the essentials you’ll need to get an interview:
Swedish in writing/speaking and excellent written/spoken English skills (corporate language).

Problem-solving, analytical and numerical skills.

Join our global team to help us achieve our social mission: to make millions of people’s opinions heard for the benefit of our local and international communities.

Understanding diversity of opinion requires diversity of background. Although our global panel of millions of people worldwide powers our research, our biggest asset is our people. If our research is to be truly representative of what the world thinks, we need people from all walks of life to be part of the team to bring their perspective to the work we do.

We are driven by a set of shared values. We are fast, fearless, and innovative. We work diligently to get it right. We are guided by accuracy, ethics, and proven methodologies. We respect and trust each other, bringing these values into everything that we do.

Nexer Insight, partner till marknadsledande molnplattformar, där innovation inom data och AI står i fokus, söker nu en Data Engineer som vill bli en del av vårt team. Hos oss får du arbeta i en engagerad och kunnig miljö tillsammans med spännande kunder, i varierande och utmanande uppdrag – samtidigt som du erbjuds goda möjligheter till utveckling och förmåner.
Kanske är du vår nästa “Data Ninja”

Hos Nexer Insight blir du en viktig del av ett bolag som präglas av entreprenörskap och nytänkande. Hos Nexer Insight, partner med databricks, arbetar vi med den senaste tekniken från Microsoft. Här får du möjlighet att pusha gränserna inom data och molnteknik, experimentera med nya verktyg och omsätta cutting-edge tech till lösningar som skapar verkligt affärsvärde. Här möts data engineering och data science – och du är med och driver lösningar från data till affärsnytta.
I rollen som Data Engineer kommer du att:
Designa, bygga och vidareutveckla skalbara och högpresterande datalösningar
Arbeta end-to-end med moderna dataplattformar, datamodeller och pipelines
Samarbeta nära kunder och omsätta affärsbehov till smarta tekniska lösningar
Säkerställa robust arkitektur med fokus på datakvalitet, tillförlitlighet och prestanda
Jobba i framkant med den senaste tekniken inom Data och Analytics
Arbeta med datainhämtning, transformation och lagring
Vara med och driva våra kunders resa mot datadrivna arbetssätt genom innovativa molnlösningar
Vi tror att du:
Har stark erfarenhet av att arbeta med molnplattformar som Azure, AWS eller GCP
Har flera års hands-on erfarenhet av ETL och dataintegration, exempelvis med Azure Data Factory, Databricks/Fabric, DBT eller Snowflake
Är van vid att arbeta med moderna dataplattformar som Data Lakes eller Lakehouse-arkitektur
Har mycket goda kunskaper i SQL och trivs med att jobba nära data
Har erfarenhet av informationsmodellering och dimensionell modellering
Har god förståelse för DevOps-principer, versionshantering i Git och CI/CD-flöden
Kommunicerar obehindrat på både svenska och engelska
Det är meriterande om du även har erfarenhet av:
Modernisering av dataplattformar
Tabular-modeller och DAX
Data Vault-modellering
Att sätta upp infrastruktur i Azure, AWS eller GCP

4428836058,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/forskningsassistent-at-stockholm-university-4428836058?position=31&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=8NnbQqwTjw4D9%2FZbhoEPcw%3D%3D,Forskningsassistent,Stockholm University,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap. Med över 200 anställda och 4 500 studenter är Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap (DSV) en stark och dynamisk forsknings- och utbildningsmiljö. Ämnet data- och systemvetenskap rör sig mellan teknik, humaniora, samhällsvetenskap och beteendevetenskap, med stor relevans för hur vi ska leva i dag och i framtiden. Vår forskning handlar om hur IT ska utformas för att vara till nytta för människor, organisationer och hela samhällen.
Mer information om oss finns på: Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap.
Projektbeskrivning

Forskningsprojektet ”Sektorkoppling och verksamhetsforskning för miljömässig hållbarhet (SCORES): Effekter på klimatutsläpp, energipriser och ekonomisk nytta som en följd av Nordens energiomställning” kommer att ta itu med energiomställningen i Sverige innebär ett avsteg från fossila bränslen samt en ökad elektrifiering av industrin, där kopplingen mellan elproduktion och konsumtion (sektorkoppling) blir av större betydelse när icke-reglerbar förnybar energi (VRE) som vind- och solkraft får en större proportion. Då VRE är intermittent och platsspecifik krävs ett mer flexibelt energisystem, med reglerbar produktion som komplement till exempel i form av vattenkraft. Här kan flexibla producenter ha större förmåga för att påverka elpriser, vilket kräver mekanismer för att mildra välfärdsförluster. På samma sätt kommer överföringsplaneringen att behöva anpassas för att dämpa producenternas marknadsstyrka. I projektet ska spelteoretiska modeller tillämpas för att identifiera hur producenternas marknadsmakt påverkas av sektorskoppling och ta fram metoder att förutse ekonomiska snedvridningar på elmarknaden. Dessa insikter kommer att stödja policyutveckling för en välfärdshöjande övergång till ett hållbart energisystem.
Arbetsuppgifter

En tvärvetenskaplig ansats som omfattar ekonomiska, tekniska och samhällsvetenskapliga perspektiv utgör ramen för analys. En forskningsassistent i projektet kommer att undersöka incitament för marknadsmakt inom energilagring. Exempel på forskningsuppgifter är:
Uppdatera och konsolidera en databas över de nordiska värme- och kraftsektorernas attribut.
Använda Nash-Cournot-modeller för att undersöka strategiskt beteende inom energilagring.
Utveckla både öppna och slutna modeller som syftar till analytiska lösningar med kompletterande numeriska exempel.
Vi välkomnar sökande från olika metodologiska bakgrunder.
Kvalifikationer
En forskningsassistent anställs i huvudsak för forskning och ska ha avlagt examen på masternivå eller har en utländsk examen som bedöms motsvara masternivå.
Vi söker en kandidat med följande kvalifikationer:
En meritlista som visar utmärkt modelleringsarbete relaterat till energisystem eller miljöpolitik publicerat i vetenskapliga tidskrifter.
Metodologisk verktygslåda relaterad till tillämpad matematik, miljöekonomi, spelteori eller operationsanalys.
Färdigheter i analytiska lösningsmetoder för spelteoretiska problem och beräkningsmässig implementering av optimeringsmodeller i GAMS eller liknande plattformar.
Domänkännedom om kraftsektorn och energipolitik.
Vilja att arbeta kollegialt som del av ett tvärvetenskapligt team och bevis på tidigare samarbete med forskare från olika discipliner.
Utmärkt engelska i tal och skrift (svenska är inte ett krav).

Vi letar efter en kandidat som kan arbeta inom ett mycket tvärvetenskapligt team och agendasättande forskningsprojekt. Du är ansvarsfull, lyhörd och en bra lagspelare. Du är proaktiv, tar ansvar för dina uppgifter och har ett strukturerat arbetssätt. Du är van att prioritera och kan hålla en överblick över bredare mål samtidigt som du levererar på konkreta uppgifter.
Om anställningen
Anställningen avser heltid och är tidsbegränsad till fyra månader. Stockholms universitet tillämpar individuell lönesättning. Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.
Vi erbjuder

Hos oss får du dynamiken i samspelet mellan högre utbildning och forskning som gör Stockholms universitet till en spännande och kreativ miljö. Du arbetar i en internationell miljö och får förmånliga villkor.
Stockholms universitet värnar om att vara en arbetsplats som är fri från diskriminering och ger lika rättigheter och möjligheter för alla.
Kontakt
Ytterligare information lämnas av professor Afzal S. Siddiqui, tfn 08-16 13 85, asiddiq@dsv.su.se.
Ansökan
Du söker anställningen via Stockholms universitets rekryteringssystem. Du som sökande ansvarar för att ansökan är komplett och att den är universitetet tillhanda senast sista ansökningsdag.
Anvisningar för sökande finns på webbsidan: att söka en anställning.
Stockholms universitet bidrar till det hållbara demokratiska samhällets utveckling genom kunskap, upplysning och sanningssökande.

  • Färdigheter i analytiska lösningsmetoder för spelteoretiska problem och beräkningsmässig implementering av optimeringsmodeller i GAMS eller liknande plattformar.
  • Domänkännedom om kraftsektorn och energipolitik.
  • Vilja att arbeta kollegialt som del av ett tvärvetenskapligt team och bevis på tidigare samarbete med forskare från olika discipliner.
  • Utmärkt engelska i tal och skrift (svenska är inte ett krav).

Vi letar efter en kandidat som kan arbeta inom ett mycket tvärvetenskapligt team och agendasättande forskningsprojekt. Du är ansvarsfull, lyhörd och en bra lagspelare. Du är proaktiv, tar ansvar för dina uppgifter och har ett strukturerat arbetssätt. Du är van att prioritera och kan hålla en överblick över bredare mål samtidigt som du levererar på konkreta uppgifter.

Om anställningen

Anställningen avser heltid och är tidsbegränsad till fyra månader. Stockholms universitet tillämpar individuell lönesättning. Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.

Vi erbjuder

Hos oss får du dynamiken i samspelet mellan högre utbildning och forskning som gör Stockholms universitet till en spännande och kreativ miljö. Du arbetar i en internationell miljö och får förmånliga villkor.
column3:
Stockholms universitet värnar om att vara en arbetsplats som är fri från diskriminering och ger lika rättigheter och möjligheter för alla.

Kontakt

Ytterligare information lämnas av professor Afzal S. Siddiqui, tfn 08-16 13 85, asiddiq@dsv.su.se.

Ansökan

Du söker anställningen via Stockholms universitets rekryteringssystem. Du som sökande ansvarar för att ansökan är komplett och att den är universitetet tillhanda senast sista ansökningsdag.

Anvisningar för sökande finns på webbsidan: att söka en anställning.

Stockholms universitet bidrar till det hållbara demokratiska samhällets utveckling genom kunskap, upplysning och sanningssökande.

4431235493,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/postdoctoral-researcher-at-kth-royal-institute-of-technology-4431235493?position=32&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=Ial6XeB31YbzJMgLcd0InQ%3D%3D,Postdoctoral Researcher,KTH Royal Institute of Technology,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Several challenges remain before larger scale deployment of Autonomous vehicles (AVs). This postdoc project, part of a Swedish national research project, is investigating how DevOps practices can be adapted beyond software to support safety critical systems and specifically automated vehicles (AVs). The need to update AVs is specifically driven by the open environments in which AVs operate and evolving security threats. This requires field data to accompany classical verification methods, while ensuring that system updates and new behaviors do not jeopardize safety. The project addresses the gap between the tradition of DevOps, which is not addressing safety-critical systems, and the tradition of safety-critical systems, which does not address DevOps.

The postdoc position represents a unique opportunity to do research on trustworthy DevOps for AVs. The postdoc is expected to contribute to collaborative research (including with other academic researchers and our industrial partners). There will also be excellent opportunities for experimental evaluation and case studies, something we believe is important. You are welcome to contribute to relevant educational efforts, especially in formulating and supervising project-related MSc thesis projects and supervision of involved PhD students. Other teaching engagements are possible depending on your interests.

As a candidate, we believe that you have a strong basis in safety engineering, software engineering or closely related fields, ideally with experience in meta modeling and/or contract-based design. We are open to different profiles and directions for the position as outlined in this position paper: (http://tiny.cc/kg5nzz).
Read more about the position, what we offer and qualitifications - and apply here:
﻿https://www.kth.se/lediga-jobb/939747?l=en

4416145647,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-eqt-group-4416145647?position=33&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=YdYbNnS%2BBA1%2BcUbUUuhhbQ%3D%3D,Data Engineer,EQT Group,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Join EQT as a Data Engineer in the Fintech Value Stream, where you will help build and scale modern data capabilities that power financial operations, reporting, and decision-making across the organization. You will work with cloud technologies, scalable data models, and business-critical financial data in a collaborative global environment.
About The Fintech Value Stream Team

The Fintech Value Stream focuses on delivering technology and data solutions that support finance-related processes across EQT. The team partners closely with finance, engineering, analytics, and business stakeholders to create scalable platforms and reliable data products that improve operational efficiency, transparency, and insights across financial operations.
The team works across modern cloud technologies and data platforms to support reporting, forecasting, reconciliation, and analytics capabilities that enable informed business decisions.
About The Role

You will play an important role in designing, building, and operating EQT’s cloud-based financial data platform. You will work across ingestion, transformation, and data modeling to ensure financial data is reliable, accessible, and structured for analytics and reporting.
Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines across finance-related systems and platforms.
Ingest and integrate data from APIs, external platforms, enterprise systems, and cloud storage solutions.
Develop and maintain data models using dbt on Snowflake or similar cloud data platforms.
Structure and model data around key financial entities such as transactions, reporting structures, portfolios, and operational metrics.
Enable analytics and reporting through tools such as Power BI, Tableau, and AI-enabled analytics solutions.
Monitor, troubleshoot, and improve pipeline reliability, performance, and data quality.
Collaborate with stakeholders across finance, analytics, engineering, and business teams to understand requirements and deliver scalable solutions.
Contribute to modernization initiatives and continuous improvements across the data platform.
Support infrastructure and access management using tools such as GitHub, Terraform, and Snowflake.
About You

You are an experienced Data Engineer who enjoys solving complex data challenges and collaborating with cross-functional teams. You are comfortable working in a modern cloud environment and translating business needs into scalable technical solutions. You are curious about emerging technologies and motivated by building reliable data products that create business impact.
Essential Skills And Experience
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines in modern cloud environments.
Strong SQL skills and experience with data modeling for analytics and reporting use cases.
Experience with Snowflake, dbt, or similar cloud-based data technologies.
Experience integrating data from APIs, SaaS platforms, and enterprise systems.
Experience collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Familiarity with reporting and visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Experience with version control and infrastructure tools such as Git and Terraform.
Nice to have
Experience working with financial systems or financial reporting data.
Experience with GCP and Fivetran.
Understanding of private markets, investment operations, or fintech environments.
Experience supporting AI or advanced analytics use cases.
What We Offer

You will be part of a collaborative and forward-looking team where data and technology are central to how EQT creates value. We offer opportunities to work with modern technologies, contribute to impactful initiatives, and grow your skills in a global environment.

At EQT, you will work alongside curious and supportive colleagues who value collaboration, continuous learning, and different perspectives. You will have the opportunity to help shape scalable data capabilities that support business-critical financial processes across the organization.
Inclusion at EQT

Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing & engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact. Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness
column2: We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. By doing so, we unlock better collaboration, stronger innovation, and superior investment outcomes.

4429571850,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-rebtel-4429571850?position=39&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=MDQu6JIlk2v%2FAbkZtvBJVw%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Rebtel,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","What will you do?
As a Data Engineer at Rebtel, you will do more than build pipelines
column2: you'll help shape the future of our data platform and influence how data is used across the company. At Rebtel, data sits at the heart of how we build products, understand customers, optimize growth, and make decisions.

column1: This is a hands-on engineering role with significant ownership. You'll work across our modern data stack, build scalable data products, and help define the architecture that supports analytics, automation, and future AI initiatives.
If you're excited by clean architecture, modern data engineering practices, and solving meaningful business problems at scale
column2: if you care about data quality and believe trust in data is built through engineering discipline
column3: if you enjoy solving complex problems and turning ambiguity into practical solutions
column4: and if you're excited to experiment with new technologies and approaches, we'd love to talk.

column1: Areas Of Ownership
Design, build, and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines that power our Snowflake data platform
Integrate data from a wide range of business and product systems
Enable data activation across customer-facing and operational platforms, including CRM, customer support, and marketing tools
Develop robust monitoring, alerting, and observability practices to ensure platform reliability
Troubleshoot, optimize, and continuously improve data workflows and platform performance
Design and implement scalable ELT workflows using modern data engineering practices
Build well-structured dimensional models (facts and dimensions) that enable self-service analytics and reporting
Implement automated testing and quality controls throughout the data lifecycle
Ensure data is accurate, accessible, and trusted across the organization
Work closely with analysts, product managers, engineers, marketers, and business stakeholders to understand data needs and deliver impactful solutions
Help translate complex business challenges into scalable data products and datasets
Support decision-making by making data easier to discover, understand, and use
Evaluate emerging technologies, frameworks, and industry best practices
Run proof-of-concepts and contribute to strategic technology decisions
Help define the future architecture of Rebtel's data platform
Continuously improve engineering standards, tooling, and developer experience
Requirements
You are an excellent communicator and collaborator. We work in English, but you will hear many languages in our Stockholm office
3+ years of experience in Data Engineering or a similar role
Strong SQL skills and experience building production-grade data pipelines
Experience with modern ELT methodologies and data warehousing concepts
Experience integrating data from multiple systems and sources
Experience with dbt, Python, AWS, or similar cloud-based data platforms
Strong understanding of data modeling and analytics engineering best practices
Ability to operate independently and take ownership of technical solutions
Experience working in fast-growing technology or digital product companies is a plus
It's a bonus if you've been exposured to customer analytics, marketing analytics, experimentation, or machine learning workflows
Experience with BI tools such as Looker, Tableau, or Power BI is a plus
It's a plus with a degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
Why Rebtel?

Rebtel has been connecting people across borders for nearly 20 years. Today, we’re profitable, growing, and at a pivotal moment in our journey. As we enter our next phase, we’re building an organisation designed for speed, ownership, and real impact, where every role contributes directly to shaping what comes next.

This is a place with global ambition and a strong foundation, where ideas move quickly and decisions matter. You won’t get lost in layers of process or slow-moving structures. Instead, you’ll find the space to take ownership, collaborate across teams, and make meaningful contributions from day one. Based in Stockholm, we bring together a diverse, international team united by a shared purpose: to simplify the way people connect worldwide.
Benefits

At Rebtel, you are the most important asset and we strive to provide a comprehensive package of benefits and perks that enhance your well-being and work experience. Here are some of the things you can expect from us:
Pension Plan
Health Checkups, Influenza shots and Private Medical Insurance
Dental Insurance
Occupational insurance
Wellness allowance (5,000 SEK)
Discount on gym memberships
Bonus program
Extra parental pay
30 days annual vacation
Monday breakfasts
Relocation Support, if you're joining us from afar, we'll assist you in making a smooth transition.

We are Rebtel. We come from all around the world to create products for anyone who has crossed a border. We believe in equal opportunity and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

  • Help translate complex business challenges into scalable data products and datasets
  • Support decision-making by making data easier to discover, understand, and use
  • Evaluate emerging technologies, frameworks, and industry best practices
  • Run proof-of-concepts and contribute to strategic technology decisions
  • Help define the future architecture of Rebtel's data platform
  • Continuously improve engineering standards, tooling, and developer experience

Requirements

  • Strong SQL skills and experience building production-grade data pipelines
  • Experience with modern ELT methodologies and data warehousing concepts
  • Experience integrating data from multiple systems and sources
  • Experience with dbt, Python, AWS, or similar cloud-based data platforms
  • Strong understanding of data modeling and analytics engineering best practices
  • Ability to operate independently and take ownership of technical solutions
  • Experience working in fast-growing technology or digital product companies is a plus

Why Rebtel?

Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to define solutions
Support implementation and continuous improvement initiatives
Ensure alignment between business needs and IT solutions
Contribute to data privacy and information governance activities
Work closely with global cross-functional teams within the organization
Required Qualifications
Academic degree in a relevant field
Extensive experience as a Business IT Analyst or similar role
Experience working within automotive projects
Previous experience working with our automotive client in Södertälje
Strong analytical and stakeholder management skills
Excellent communication and documentation abilities
Meritorious Qualifications
Experience within information governance, including legal, architecture, or information security perspectives
Experience working with Data Privacy initiatives
Knowledge of vehicle electrical systems and/or embedded systems
C/CE driver's license
Experience working in global and cross-functional environments
What We Offer

Collaboration with talented and experienced professionals
Competitive compensation and benefits package
Continuous learning and professional development opportunities
Flexible hybrid working model supporting work-life balance
25 days of annual leave

4419916133,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-flower-4419916133?position=41&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=HoA7%2B5ePx9teG6jC0o8%2BrA%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Flower,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","⚡️ What We Do

Flower is Flexible Power. We are a next-gen energy company leveraging AI and machine learning to make renewable energy stable and always available – even when the sun isn’t shining and the wind isn’t blowing.

Through smart optimization and trading of energy assets like wind and solar farms, battery systems, and EV chargers, we make renewable energy reliable and predictable, leading the charge towards the energy system of tomorrow.
🌟 Who We Are
Tech company at heart – purpose in our DNA. Flower consists of a diverse group of innovative individuals with a strong desire to improve the state of the world.

At Flower, we believe trust, collaboration and diversity are essential to not only create an inclusive work environment, but also drive career growth. By embracing varying perspectives, we allow creativity and progress to flourish.

To accelerate towards our goal of becoming the pioneering force powering the energy system of tomorrow, we are now looking for a passionate and skilled Senior Software Engineer to join our Asset Optimisation domain.
👩‍💻 About the role

As a Data Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in guiding the future of the Wholesale squad within the Asset Optimization domain. Our mission is to master short-term energy-intensive markets, including Intraday Continuous.

You will join a small, highly talented team at a moment of expansion and help us build robust data pipelines that will push the performance of our strategies to the next level, guarantee observability of our trading activities, and enable the discoverability of new opportunities. Your contribution will help the team scale with the company’s growth, and excel in an ever-changing energy landscape.
What You’ll Do
Contribute to the backbone of our data ecosystem: Design and operate robust, low-latency data pipelines that reliably feed our trading algorithms and analytics systems.

Model and monitor trading intelligence: Develop and maintain efficient data models, analytical dashboards, and monitoring frameworks that bring clarity to the team’s trading performance and system health.
Design for reliability: Automate data quality checks, fallbacks, and alerting systems that prevent outages, protect trading operations, and ensure compliance.
Accelerate backtesting and research: Work alongside extremely ambitious and talented colleagues to design data pipelines and models that enable high-fidelity backtesting and rapid hypothesis testing.
Drive technical excellence: Contribute to code quality, observability, and maintainability
column2: champion good engineering patterns and regulatory compliance in everything you build.

column1: Who You Are
Experienced data engineer: 4+ years of experience designing and operating production-grade data systems in Python and SQL, ideally in time-sensitive or high-availability environments.

Engineering leader: Strong programming skills (especially Python and SQL) and deep expertise in building robust data pipelines in cloud-native solutions. You are fluent in IaC practices and tools (Terraform, CloudFormation) and experienced in designing scalable, fault-tolerant systems.

Full-stack problem solver: Comfortable owning everything in the data lifecycle, from ingestion to insight, from analytics to operational readiness, with the mindset that no problem is “out of scope” if it advances the team’s mission.
Great communicator: Adept at explaining technical concepts to both technical peers and business stakeholders, helping align decisions across the organization.

Bonus points: You have working experience in or around trading desks, especially with limit orderbook data. You have working experience in the energy sector, understand market data structures, or have experience enabling algorithmic or live trading environments.
📍 Location
Our beautiful office is located in the heart of Södermalm, just a short walk from Slussen subway station. We encourage in-office collaboration but support a hybrid work model.
✉️ Apply
Our corporate language is English, as we have about 30 nationalities in the office. We therefore appreciate it if you could submit your CV in English.
Throughout the recruitment process you will meet with the Talent Partner, the Head of Wholesale, the Team and VP of Engineering.
We look forward to hearing from you!
We kindly but firmly decline any engagement in recruitment assistance for our hiring processes. This includes partnership offers or the sale of recruitment tools.

4430935295,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-playground-tech-4430935295?position=44&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=rACD1g3X73d%2FGnAKo3KHNg%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Playground Tech,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Playground Data
Stockholm
Hybrid
Data Engineer

Some data problems are neat and tidy. Ours usually aren’t. They are messy, evolving, and tied to real decisions. We work closely with our clients to understand what actually needs to be solved and build from there.

At Playground Data, we build modern data platforms for analytics, AI and machine learning. We are a collaborative team that values solid engineering and shared growth. Our engineers take ownership and work closely with clients to deliver high-quality solutions.
What you'll actually do

You’ll work closely with our clients on high-impact initiatives where data is critical to the business. Many of our engagements involve greenfield builds or major platform transformations. This role suits engineers who enjoy creating structure from complexity and taking end-to-end ownership.
In Practice, You’ll
Design and build modern data platforms from scratch.
Ingest, model, and structure data so it’s usable for analytics, AI and ML workloads.
Work close to production, with growing responsibility for operating, evolving, and improving platforms over time.

Contribute internally by improving how we work, helping build and manage our data engineering frameworks, shaping our technical approaches, being part of presales and supporting Playground’s continued growth.
You’ll never sit idle, there’s always something meaningful to build, improve, or untangle.
Who are you?

You’ve been building data platforms for a few years and know that data doesn’t magically organize itself. Python and SQL are part of your daily toolkit, and you’ve wrestled with at least one modern data stack (AWS, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake etc.) and lived to tell the tale.
You Would Probably Describe Yourself As Someone Who
Understands data engineering in practice: You know how data needs to flow, scale, and hold up in production for analytics and AI and ML workloads.
Enjoys building from scratch: Greenfield work doesn’t scare you, it motivates you.
Stays calm when things are unclear: You don’t panic when requirements are fuzzy and the system diagram needs three whiteboards.
Is comfortable in a consultant role: You handle customer dialogue well, can contribute in presales, and help guide clients through difficult decisions.
As a person, you’re humble, genuinely curious about technology, and motivated, while knowing that asking for help is a strength, not a weakness. Friendly, curious, and playful.
You must be based in the Stockholm area and have a valid work permit for Sweden (no visa sponsorship or relocation).
Buzzword bingo: AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake, DBT, Python, SQL, Terraform
Language requirement
You need to be fluent in Swedish and able to use it confidently in a professional environment. Swedish citizenship is required.

Why? Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements for both Swedish citizenship and full professional fluency in the language.
Let's Play!
Applying to us is a breeze!
Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
Answer the choice-questions (no ""please-rewrite-your-resume"" nonsense, promise)
Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us
Note
Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support.
For this role applicants must have a swedish citizenship
Welcome to Playground!

At Playground, you work on real, complex assignments for well-established and ambitious clients, not bench projects or short-lived experiments. Our teams are trusted with production systems, platforms, and data that actually matter. Here growth comes from ownership, curiosity, and doing the work properly. You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time.
Playground brings together specialized domains under one umbrella, with a shared culture and way of working, and easy access to broad technical expertise when it helps move the work forward.
