Data Engineer

Some data problems are neat and tidy. Ours usually aren’t. They are messy, evolving, and tied to real decisions. We work closely with our clients to understand what actually needs to be solved and build from there.

At Playground Data, we build modern data platforms for analytics, AI and machine learning. We are a collaborative team that values solid engineering and shared growth. Our engineers take ownership and work closely with clients to deliver high-quality solutions.

What you'll actually do

You’ll work closely with our clients on high-impact initiatives where data is critical to the business. Many of our engagements involve greenfield builds or major platform transformations. This role suits engineers who enjoy creating structure from complexity and taking end-to-end ownership.

In Practice, You’ll

  • Design and build modern data platforms from scratch.
  • Ingest, model, and structure data so it’s usable for analytics, AI and ML workloads.
  • Work close to production, with growing responsibility for operating, evolving, and improving platforms over time.
  • Contribute internally by improving how we work, helping build and manage our data engineering frameworks, shaping our technical approaches, being part of presales and supporting Playground’s continued growth.

You’ll never sit idle, there’s always something meaningful to build, improve, or untangle.

Who are you?

You’ve been building data platforms for a few years and know that data doesn’t magically organize itself. Python and SQL are part of your daily toolkit, and you’ve wrestled with at least one modern data stack (AWS, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake etc.) and lived to tell the tale.

You Would Probably Describe Yourself As Someone Who

Understands data engineering in practice: You know how data needs to flow, scale, and hold up in production for analytics and AI and ML workloads.

Enjoys building from scratch: Greenfield work doesn’t scare you, it motivates you.

Stays calm when things are unclear: You don’t panic when requirements are fuzzy and the system diagram needs three whiteboards.

Is comfortable in a consultant role: You handle customer dialogue well, can contribute in presales, and help guide clients through difficult decisions.

As a person, you’re humble, genuinely curious about technology, and motivated, while knowing that asking for help is a strength, not a weakness. Friendly, curious, and playful.

You must be based in the Stockholm area and have a valid work permit for Sweden (no visa sponsorship or relocation).

Buzzword bingo: AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake, DBT, Python, SQL, Terraform

Language requirement

You need to be fluent in Swedish and able to use it confidently in a professional environment. Swedish citizenship is required.

Why? Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements for both Swedish citizenship and full professional fluency in the language.

Let's Play!

Applying to us is a breeze!

  • Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
  • Answer the choice-questions (no ""please-rewrite-your-resume"" nonsense, promise)
  • Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us

Note

Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support.

4431832191,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-twoday-4431832191?position=45&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=7dPpOf71d%2Flk2jUpTHmlyA%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Twoday,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Var med och skapa en datadriven värld!

Hos oss på twoday arbetar över 100 konsulter inom Analytics och Business Intelligence, bara i Sverige och Norge. Totalt inom Data&AI är vi ca 700 specialister på olika tekniker och verktyg, som används för att bygga moderna och hållbara BI-plattformar. I en allt mer datadriven värld ser vi en tydlig övergång från att BI-lösningar används för rapportering och att titta i backspegeln, till att lösningarna blir kritiska verktyg i våra kunders dagliga verksamhet.

Möjligheterna att också koppla in maskininlärning och prediktion i analysverktygen ställer nya krav på både dataplattformen och kompetensen inom olika molnteknologier. Vi skapar insikt i företag och samhälle genom avancerade analyser och lösningar. Våra kvalitativa tjänster är högt eftertraktade och vi letar nu efter fler kompetenta medarbetare.
Dina Centrala Arbetsuppgifter Som Data Engineer
Använda modern och marknadsledande teknik för att lösa våra kunders analys- och rapporteringsbehov
Utveckling av funktionella och tekniska arkitekturer som stöder användningen av moderna molnplattformar
Utveckling av lösningar för dataflöde, big data och datalager
Utveckling av datavisualisering, rapporter och applikationer
Förberedelse och utveckling av avancerade analysmodeller som använder sig av det senaste inom maskininlärning och AI
Verifiering och kvalitetssäkring av lösningar
Vi Letar Efter Dig Som Har
Relevant teknisk utbildning på master- eller kandidatnivå.
Arbetslivserfarenhet (minst 5 år) inom Business Intelligence-området
Kunskap om teknik, arkitekturer och koncept för dataflöde, ETL och datalager. Tex erfarenhet av Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake eller Databricks.
Kunskap och erfarenhet inom någon datavisualiseringsteknik, exempelvis Power BI, Qlik Sense, Tableau eller liknande.
Stark analytisk förmåga och kan förbereda & presentera avancerade analyser.
Personliga egenskaper
Engagerad och nyfiken. Du är uppdaterad om tekniska trender och har en vilja att utveckla både dina egna kunskaper och din omgivning. Du brinner för att skapa värde och göra skillnad i dina uppdrag.
Systematisk och strukturerad. Du arbetar effektivt och kan bryta ner komplexa problem och hitta enkla lösningar.
God skriftlig och muntlig kommunikationsförmåga på både svenska och engelska.
Ansökan
Låter detta som en intressant möjlighet? Urval och intervjuer sker löpande så vänta inte med att skicka in din ansökan.
Om du har frågor angående tjänsten eller din ansökan är du välkommen att kontakta Talent Acquisition Business Partner Matilda Persson matilda.persson@twoday.com.
Vad erbjuder twoday?
Medarbetaren i centrum

twoday är en flexibel arbetsplats med varierande arbetsuppgifter och stort utrymme att utvecklas. Vårt främsta mål är att du som medarbetare kan arbeta i miljö som är trivsam, innovativ, och framförallt - rolig!

Hos oss hittar du ett väl sammansvetsat, flexibelt gäng som hjälper varandra och våra kunder att nå framgång varje dag. Med mer än 3000 anställda arbetar vi för att skapa en bättre morgondag, som ger mervärde för oss som människor och samhället som helhet. Vi tror att med rätt medarbetare och högteknologiska lösningar kan vi skapa en tydligare bild av var vi är just nu – och var vi kommer vara imorgon.
Mångfald & inkludering

twoday är en inkluderande arbetsgivare och eftersträvar mångfald. Vi uppmanar alla kvalificerade kandidater att söka, oavsett kön, könsidentitet, etnicitet, religion, funktionsvariation, sexuell läggning eller ålder.

Möter du inte varje krav? Studier har visat att kvinnor och andra underrepresenterade grupper är mindre benägna att ansöka till ett jobb om dom inte möter varje kvalifikation. På twoday är vi dedikerade att bygga en arbetsplats som genomsyras av mångfald och inkludering. Om du är intresserad av denna roll men din tidigare erfarenhet inte matchar till hundra procent mot arbetsbeskrivningen, så uppmanar vi dig att ansöka ändå.
Informationssäkerhet och bakgrundskontroller

twoday är en arbetsgivare som arbetar systematiskt med informationssäkerhet. Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kan vi komma att genomföra relevanta bakgrundskontroller, såsom referenstagning, ID-verifiering och i vissa fall utdrag ur belastningsregistret. Detta i enlighet med tillämpliga lagar och i proportion till den aktuella tjänstens informationsåtkomst och ansvar.
Vi följer gällande dataskyddsförordning (GDPR) och all behandling av personuppgifter sker konfidentiellt och med samtycke.

I en allt mer datadriven värld ser vi en tydlig övergång från att BI-lösningar används för rapportering och att titta i backspegeln, till att lösningarna blir kritiska verktyg i våra kunders dagliga verksamhet.

Möjligheterna att också koppla in maskininlärning och prediktion i analysverktygen ställer nya krav på både dataplattformen och kompetensen inom olika molnteknologier. Vi skapar insikt i företag och samhälle genom avancerade analyser och lösningar. Våra kvalitativa tjänster är högt eftertraktade och vi letar nu efter fler kompetenta medarbetare.

Dina Centrala Arbetsuppgifter Som Data Engineer

  • Använda modern och marknadsledande teknik för att lösa våra kunders analys- och rapporteringsbehov
  • Utveckling av funktionella och tekniska arkitekturer som stöder användningen av moderna molnplattformar
  • Utveckling av lösningar för dataflöde, big data och datalager
  • Utveckling av datavisualisering, rapporter och applikationer
  • Förberedelse och utveckling av avancerade analysmodeller som använder sig av det senaste inom maskininlärning och AI
  • Verifiering och kvalitetssäkring av lösningar

Vi Letar Efter Dig Som Har

  • Relevant teknisk utbildning på master- eller kandidatnivå.
  • Arbetslivserfarenhet (minst 5 år) inom Business Intelligence-området
  • Kunskap om teknik, arkitekturer och koncept för dataflöde, ETL och datalager. Tex erfarenhet av Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake eller Databricks.
  • Kunskap och erfarenhet inom någon datavisualiseringsteknik, exempelvis Power BI, Qlik Sense, Tableau eller liknande.
  • Stark analytisk förmåga och kan förbereda &
column3:
presentera avancerade analyser.

Personliga egenskaper

  • Engagerad och nyfiken. Du är uppdaterad om tekniska trender och har en vilja att utveckla både dina egna kunskaper och din omgivning. Du brinner för att skapa värde och göra skillnad i dina uppdrag.
  • Systematisk och strukturerad. Du arbetar effektivt och kan bryta ner komplexa problem och hitta enkla lösningar.
  • God skriftlig och muntlig kommunikationsförmåga på både svenska och engelska.

Ansökan

Låter detta som en intressant möjlighet? Urval och intervjuer sker löpande så vänta inte med att skicka in din ansökan.

Om du har frågor angående tjänsten eller din ansökan är du välkommen att kontakta Talent Acquisition Business Partner Matilda Persson matilda.persson@twoday.com.

Vad erbjuder twoday?

Medarbetaren i centrum

twoday är en flexibel arbetsplats med varierande arbetsuppgifter och stort utrymme att utvecklas. Vårt främsta mål är att du som medarbetare kan arbeta i miljö som är trivsam, innovativ, och framförallt - rolig!

Hos oss hittar du ett väl sammansvetsat, flexibelt gäng som hjälper varandra och våra kunder att nå framgång varje dag. Med mer än 3000 anställda arbetar vi för att skapa en bättre morgondag, som ger mervärde för oss som människor och samhället som helhet. Vi tror att med rätt medarbetare och högteknologiska lösningar kan vi skapa en tydligare bild av var vi är just nu – och var vi kommer vara imorgon.

Mångfald &
column4:
inkludering

twoday är en inkluderande arbetsgivare och eftersträvar mångfald. Vi uppmanar alla kvalificerade kandidater att söka, oavsett kön, könsidentitet, etnicitet, religion, funktionsvariation, sexuell läggning eller ålder.

Möter du inte varje krav? Studier har visat att kvinnor och andra underrepresenterade grupper är mindre benägna att ansöka till ett jobb om dom inte möter varje kvalifikation. På twoday är vi dedikerade att bygga en arbetsplats som genomsyras av mångfald och inkludering. Om du är intresserad av denna roll men din tidigare erfarenhet inte matchar till hundra procent mot arbetsbeskrivningen, så uppmanar vi dig att ansöka ändå.

Informationssäkerhet och bakgrundskontroller

twoday är en arbetsgivare som arbetar systematiskt med informationssäkerhet. Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kan vi komma att genomföra relevanta bakgrundskontroller, såsom referenstagning, ID-verifiering och i vissa fall utdrag ur belastningsregistret. Detta i enlighet med tillämpliga lagar och i proportion till den aktuella tjänstens informationsåtkomst och ansvar.

Vi följer gällande dataskyddsförordning (GDPR) och all behandling av personuppgifter sker konfidentiellt och med samtycke.

4426900526,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-ten10-ab-4426900526?position=46&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=uq%2FvLTmyw5yKdz%2BlG1SvVw%3D%3D,Data Engineer,ten10 AB,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","VVi söker Data Engineers

Vi rekryterar inte till en specifik roll, utan söker ständigt efter nya duktiga kollegor med en positiv inställning och en vilja att lära och dela med sig av sina kunskaper. Med den inställningen vet vi att du kan lyckas som konsult hos ten10. Därför lyssnar vi på dig och ser, utifrån din erfarenhet och kompetens, vilken typ av spännande uppdrag där du trivs och utvecklas bäst.
Hos ten10 blir du en del av en organisation som värdesätter den personliga relationen, där varje individ får vara med och påverka både sin egen och ten10s utveckling.

Vi tror på att bygga ett bolag av olika personer som bidrar på olika sätt till att vi alla trivs och lyckas. Utöver att göra skillnad i de uppdrag vi gemensamt väljer utifrån ditt intresse och din utveckling är du en viktig del av ett framåtlutat bolag där våra medarbetare alltid står i fokus.
Hos oss ser man…
…positivt på utmaningar
…positivt på samarbete men klarar också av att vara självgående när det behövs
…positivt på nya teknologier, data och komplexa problem
…positivt på kompetensutveckling och att ständigt bli bättre
…positivt på att vara med och påverka
Att vara en konsult hos ten10

Vi vill ständigt bli bättre och kunna hjälpa fler av våra kunder i deras utmaningar. Vi därför söker vi nu fler Data Engineers som vill vara med på en spännande resa, tillsammans med oss och våra kunder.

Som Data Engineer-konsult hos ten10 har du en nyckelroll i att demokratisera data ute hos våra kunder. Tillgänglig, tillförlitlig och välstrukturerad data är grunden för både Analytics, Machine Learning och AI-lösningar och där gör du verklig skillnad.
Våra uppdrag varierar, men gemensamt är att du arbetar nära både verksamhet, analytiker, data scientists och utvecklingsteam för att skapa hållbara dataplattformar och datadrivna lösningar.
I din roll som Data Engineer…
flytande i både Svenska och Engelska
…har du vilja och intresse av att lösa kundernas problem och se det som en intressant utmaning.
…har du minst tre års arbetslivserfarenhet inom rollen eller liknande roller
har du gärna erfarenhet eller ett stort intresse av Databricks
…är du skicklig i ett skriptspråk som Python.
…gillar du att bygga pipelines med (med ApacheSpark, Flink eller liknande).
…har du en djup förståelse för systemdesign, datastrukturer och algoritmer och kunskapen att tillämpa dem för att skapa pragmatiska lösningar.
…har du erfarenhet av molnplattformar som AWS, Azure, GCP eller liknande.
…har du erfarenhet av av big data-teknologier som Hadoop, Kafka, SQL, Spark m.m.
…är du noggrann men också lösningsorienterad
…trivs du att jobba i agila team och gärna också i en mer ledande roll.
…har du ett stort intresse för Data och Analytics området i allmänhet
Vad får man ut av att vara en ten10are

Hos ten10 är det viktigt att hitta en sund balans mellan privatliv och arbetsliv. Vi arbetar tillsammans för att hitta ett upplägg som fungerar i din vardag – både när det gäller uppdrag, arbetssätt och ersättning.
Vi erbjuder en av marknadens mest attraktiva lönemodeller, där du får tydlig kompensation för det värde du skapar.
Vi lägger också stor vikt vid gemenskap. Du får möjlighet att dela erfarenheter, idéer och perspektiv när vi ses på AWs, resor, julbord och andra gemensamma aktiviteter.
Vi pratar inte bara om att vi bryr oss om våra medarbetare – hela vår verksamhet bygger på dem. Vår ambition är att skapa rätt förutsättningar för just dig.

4374766866,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-kreditz-4374766866?position=47&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=P5ZholpRkkt3qX8OUSjf3Q%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Kreditz,"Danderyd, Stockholm County, Sweden","Kreditz is transforming how businesses assess credit and risk using real-time data and NextGen Credit Scoring solutions.

As one of Europe’s fastest-growing FinTech scaleups - we’ve earned a place on the Sifted 100: Nordics & Benelux list in both 2024 and 2025 and most recently, we were proud to receive a double win at the Credit Management & Compliance Awards 2025, being named both ""Fintech Rocket of the Year"" and ""Breakthrough of the Year"".
With strong growth across the Nordics and Europe, our clients range from leading banks and iGaming giants to fast-moving digital innovators.
About The Position
At Kreditz, data is not just a support function, it is the core of our product. We turn raw bank transactions into real-time insight that powers credit & risk decisions across Europe.

As we scale, we’re strengthening our data foundation and building a modern, reliable, and cost-efficient data platform. We’re now looking for a Data Engineer to join our Product team and take ownership of our Data Warehouse, pipelines, and reporting enablement across the business.
This is a hands-on role for someone who loves building scalable data infrastructure and wants to work close to both product and real business impact.
Your mission is to build and evolve a scalable, reliable, and high-performing data environment that supports both analytics and operational needs.
What Can Kreditz Offer
A once-in-a-lifetime growth journey, from startup to Global scaleup
A journey where everyone in the team plays a crucial role for our company’s success
Fun-loving, driven, and passionate colleagues who support each other’s success
A culture that celebrates progress, humility, and teamwork
We win together
24/7 access to gym, sauna and sports facilities at our office, plus additional wellness allowance
Competitive base salary
RESPONSIBILITES
Some Of The Main Responsibilities You Will Cover
Maintain & Enhance the DWH: Own the architecture of our Data Warehouse, ensuring it is scalable, cost-effective, and optimized for both analytical and operational needs.

Pipeline Automation: Design, build, and maintain robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest data from various sources into our AWS ecosystem. Work closely with our MLOPs Engineering team to assist the data pipelines for our AI / ML models
Stakeholder Enablement: Work closely with Product, Finance, and Commercial teams to automate internal reporting and create self-service data environments.
AWS Infrastructure: Leverage and manage AWS services (such as Glue, Redshift, Athena, Lambda, and S3) to ensure a high-performing data environment.
Data Quality & Governance: Implement monitoring and validation frameworks to ensure data integrity and lineage across all systems.
Collaborate: Partner with our DevOps and Backend teams to align infrastructure and ensure smooth data flow across the entire Kreditz platform.
WE ARE LOOKING FOR
We focus on practical engineering skill, strong foundations, and the ability to build systems that last.
In Addition To That We Believe You Have
BSc / MSc in a quantitative subject, i.e., Physics, Engineering, Mathematics, Computer Science or equivalent.
3+ years of experience in Data Engineering or a similar role focused on data infrastructure.
Expert-level SQL skills for complex transformations and performance tuning.
Proficiency in Python for building pipelines and automating tasks.
Strong hands-on experience with AWS (specifically services like Redshift/Snowflake, S3, Glue, and Lambda).
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts (e.g., Star Schema, Kimball) and modern data stack tools.
Experience with orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Dagster) and CI/CD workflows (i.e., GitHub Actions).
Ability to translate complex technical data structures into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders.
Meritorious Skills
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform).
Experience from FinTech, Open Banking, or working with highly regulated data.
Familiarity with BI tools (e.g., AWS QuickSight, PowerBI...etc)
WHY JOIN NOW?

At Kreditz, you’ll work with data that matters, powering real-time financial decisions across Europe. You’ll have real ownership, real impact, and the opportunity to shape the data foundation of a fast-growing FinTech scaleup.
APPOINTMENT OF ROLE
SCOPE
Full-time, permanent employment (tillsvidareanställning)
SALARY
Competitive salary
REPORTING
You will be reporting to the company’s CPO
WORKPLACE
Danderyd, Stockholm
RECRUITMENT PROCESS
Ongoing selection, we review candidates continuously.
Last day for applications 31st March
CRIMINAL RECORDS EXTRACT
As part of our recruitment process we will ask you to present a clean criminal records extract
CONTACT
Lorenzo Puccio, VP Strategic Operations, Lorenzo@kreditz.com
For more information about the role feel free to reach out to our CPO, Spilios Tzouras, spilios.tzouras@kreditz.com

Why Join Us?

At Tobii Dynavox, we empower people with disabilities to do what they once did or never thought possible. We call this mission Power to Be You. Our assistive communication technology helps our customers express themselves, connect with the world, and pursue independence, whether through everyday activities like ordering food or extraordinary feats like running a company. Working at Tobii Dynavox, you’ll become part of a team that spans the globe, with offices in the US, Sweden, France, the UK, China, and beyond.
To learn more about what we make possible, meet some of our customers or take a look at some of our solutions

d.

ts.

nce.
In this role you
will:Work to improve and streamline BI data warehouse maintenance, enhancements and daily operat
ions.Translate business needs into technical solutions through hands-on development around architecture, coding and integration to contribute to a scalable, and stable BI architec
ture.Build and optimize data models, semantic layers, and ETL pipel
ines.Be part of the BI data warehouse maintenance, enhancement and daily operat
ions.Help develop capabilities within Azure technologies such as AI, Fabric, Event Hubs, Stream Analytics and Synapse pipel

ines.Work across the full data value chain from integrating data sources using connectivity tools and APIs, to transforming and aligning data, and delivering insights via reports, dashboards, and analytical mo
dels.Contribute to the improvement of Power BI dashboards and analytics solutions using the Microsoft BI s
tack.Collaborate with colleagues to support global data initiatives and participate in projects focused on customer usage data and event-based analy
tics.
What We’re Looki

ng ForAs we continue to strengthen our data landscape, the need for event-driven analytics, and ability to handle larger and more complex data sets is growing. We are therefore looking for a Data & Analytics Engineer with a strong engineering mindset, interest in business and passion for
data.

If having a key hands-on role driving our organization forward to be more data driven, modern and scalable applying modern data techniques excites you and sounds like a challenge you’d thrive in, we’d love to hear fr
om you!
We’re looking for a detail-oriented and analytical profession
al with:Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Computer Science, Engineering (Industrial or Mechanical) or similar fields with skills in statistics and re
porting.Experience in BI roles such as BI Specialist, Analyst, Consultant, or Data E

ngineer.Coding skills in SQL, Python/PySpark with interest in business processes and curiosity to explore programming languages or platforms as our solutions develop. Experience with TimeXtender or similar orchestration tools is
a plus.Familiarity with traditional database management, Azure event-based technologies (Event Hubs, Stream Analytics, etc.) and Microsoft Fabric or modern lake house concepts is
a plus.Communication, collaboration and interpersonal skills to work closely with external and internal stakeholders across various levels and fu
nctions.Proficiency in both spoken and written communication in English. Proficiency in Swedish is
valued.
Our

Values: At Tobii Dynavox, our mission guides what we do, and our values guide us in how we do it. Across the organization, we are committed to being Collaborative, Considerate, Curious and Courageous. We build a trusting environment where every team member prioritizes our customers with empathy and insight. Bold ideas and learning lead to impactful solutions. Driven by curiosity, we continuously challenge the status quo to create meaningful, customer-focused solutions for our c
ustomers.

us unique:Purpose-Driven Work: Join a company that transforms lives by giving a voice to those with communication challenges. Every day, your work makes a meaningful and concre
te impact.“Yes, and...” Flexibility: Build a rewarding career AND enjoy time with loved ones. We offer flexible work options so you don’t have to choose between personal and professio
nal goals.Growth and Development: Whether you’re advancing your skills or growing your career, we invest in your future with training, learning opportunities, and internal gro
wth paths.Inclusive and Supportive Culture: Work in a collaborative, caring environment where diversity and individuality are valued. You’ll feel connected to both your team and our global
community.A Global Leader with Heart: Be part of an innovative, forward-thinking company that combines experience and cutting-edge solutions with a mission to cha
nge lives.

4385273090,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-dxc-technology-4385273090?position=55&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=%2FCRR3ZstPbaWf1u6anjpLg%3D%3D,Data Engineer,DXC Technology,"Solna, Stockholm County, Sweden","Job Description

Do you want to take part in developing and implementing products for our Clients in Sweden? These systems support critical business needs for our Clients and their customers and you will be performing a key role in bringing digital solutions to our Clients. Then we will have the right challenge for you

DXC Technology has been driving the digitalization of Sweden for more than 50 years and we are seeking top-candidates, who thrive to work with technologies and solutions that will impact on a national scale, and who take pride in delivering state-of-the-art services that benefit citizens, financial institutions and the public sector.
Essential Job Functions
In this role you will be covering the full life cycle of product realization, including but not limited to the following capabilities:
Product Management
Stakeholder Management
Product Concept Identification
Product Concept Development
Product Development
Dependency Management
Scope of work will be determined by the client's products planning process and will align with strategic objectives and department roadmap.
Product Development for both data products and data platform will align with the client's requirements and built with considerations to best practice architecture and engineering.
Basic Qualifications
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field or equivalent combination of education and experience
3+ years of relevant work experience
Proven experience in data engineering
Must be able to manage the following technologies:
Snowflake.
Python.
SQL.
Cloud development and data environments (Azure).
Continuous deployment.
CI/CD
Data Warehouse experience.
Data Analytical Skills.
Data Modelling.
DBT (Data Build Tool) & Airflow in collaboration
Data visualization using stream-lit
Infrastructure cost management optimization for azure and snowflake
Fluent in English and Swedish
Other Qualifications
Advanced degree in a relevant field a plus
Relevant certifications, such as Data Engineering on Google Cloud Platform or IBM Certified Data Engineer a plus
What We Offer

You’ll benefit from ongoing training and development opportunities, access to certifications, and a supportive team environment. Strong collaboration in the entire European team drives our joint success. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including pension plans, life and health insurance, as well as flexible benefits tailored to your needs.
If you are a results-driven professional excited about leveraging your experience, we invite you to pursue this opportunity with us at DXC Technology.

At DXC Technology, we believe strong connections and community are key to our success. Our work model prioritizes in-person collaboration while offering flexibility to support wellbeing, productivity, individual work styles, and life circumstances. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.

Recruitment fraud is a scheme in which fictitious job opportunities are offered to job seekers typically through online services, such as false websites, or through unsolicited emails claiming to be from the company. These emails may request recipients to provide personal information or to make payments as part of their illegitimate recruiting process. DXC does not make offers of employment via social media networks and DXC never asks for any money or payments from applicants at any point in the recruitment process, nor ask a job seeker to purchase IT or other equipment on our behalf. More information on employment scams is available here.

    What We Offer

    You’ll benefit from ongoing training and development opportunities, access to certifications, and a supportive team environment. Strong collaboration in the entire European team drives our joint success. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including pension plans, life and health insurance, as well as flexible benefits tailored to your needs.

    If you are a results-driven professional excited about leveraging your experience, we invite you to pursue this opportunity with us at DXC Technology.

    At DXC Technology, we believe strong connections and community are key to our success. Our work model prioritizes in-person collaboration while offering flexibility to support wellbeing, productivity, individual work styles, and life circumstances. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.

    Recruitment fraud is a scheme in which fictitious job opportunities are offered to job seekers typically through online services, such as false websites, or through unsolicited emails claiming to be from the company. These emails may request recipients to provide personal information or to make payments as part of their illegitimate recruiting process. DXC does not make offers of employment via social media networks and DXC never asks for any money or payments from applicants at any point in the recruitment process, nor ask a job seeker to purchase IT or other equipment on our behalf. More information on employment scams is available here.

4375814545,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-mentimeter-4375814545?position=56&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=nxNuoWoQaUpp9jrlA5MjGw%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Mentimeter,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Mentimeter is an engagement tool with a clear goal in mind. To turn presentations into conversations. Through real-time interactivity and clear visualizations, we get people to participate, engage and become more productive. Transforming all those passive meetings, airless classrooms and drawn out trainings into valuable and memorable moments.

We truly believe that you achieve the best results by doing things together. And that successful leaders need to adopt a curious and collaborative mindset in order to get there. So with you at Mentimeter, you’ll be a big part of the ambition to help over 1 billion people listen, learn and work better together.

Modern data platforms are built on events - well-defined, trustworthy signals that describe how users interact with products and systems over time. As a Data Engineer you will help build and improve the data platform that powers analytics, growth, personalization and decision-making across the company.

This role is part of the Data Platform team, where we design and maintain the core data infrastructure that serves Product, Marketing, Growth, and Analytics. You will focus on the systems and infrastructure that supports transforming batch and streaming data into high-quality datasets that downstream systems - from warehouses to activation tools - can rely on.

We are currently in the early stages of building out a new event-driven architecture, and this role will be part of shaping it. That means owning parts of the streaming infrastructure, contributing to foundational decisions about how events are structured and evolved, and having real influence over how data flows across the company for years to come.
This is a hybrid/onsite role located in Stockholm, our guideline is to spend 3 days a week at our office.
What You’ll Do
Design and operate data infrastructure (streaming and batch) and the systems that move data reliably at scale.
Contribute to how events are defined, structured, and evolved across the platform.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and observability across event pipelines.
Ensure GDPR and PII compliance across data flows at scale.
Contribute to data and architecture best practices that may span across the whole organisation.
Who You Are
A few years of experience as a Data Engineer or data-heavy backend engineer.
Solid programming and data skills (e.g. SQL, Python, and/or JVM-based languages).
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines in a modern cloud data stack.
Experience managing infrastructure as code (e.g. Terraform or similar).
A good understanding of data quality, observability, and reliability.
Clear written communication skills in English and comfort working cross-functionally.
Experience with streaming systems or near real-time data infrastructure (e.g. Kafka, Flink, or similar).
📍 Location:
This is a hybrid/on-site role at Mentimeter HQ in Stockholm, where we expect you to be in the office at least 3 days/week.
Please note that we do not offer relocation sponsorship for the position.
Not Required
You don’t have to know Swedish (daily work is carried out in English and the Mentimeter team currently boasts over 50 nationalities!)
What Mentimeter Can Offer

At Mentimeter we can offer a diverse and inclusive work environment supported by smart and driven colleagues. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our colleagues and therefore offer access to a leadership program (including external personal coach) and relevant education to ensure that we continue to be state-of-the-art when it comes to innovating and building Mentimeter. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, working on a beloved product used by more than 300 million people. It’s not all about work though, we also offer a very healthy view on work-life balance.
All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package, including pension contributions. Learn more about our benefits by visiting our Benefits & Perks page
AI and Hiring at Mentimeter

At Mentimeter, we believe AI helps us work smarter - but it never replaces the human assessment, curiosity, and personal connection that define our culture and our hiring. We use AI as a sparring partner: to bounce ideas, bring new perspectives, support structure, and make our work more efficient. But the meaning, decisions, and interactions always come from people.
AI does not screen or decide on candidates.
There is no automated filtering, ranking, or decision-making in our recruitment process. Every application is reviewed by a person.
Hiring teams may use AI to support their work - for example, to structure notes, prepare interview questions, or organize their thinking.

AI strengthens our work, but it does not define it. At Mentimeter, we’re not building an AI-driven hiring process - we’re building a people-first culture, where technology helps us listen, learn, and grow together.
Culture at Mentimeter

At Mentimeter we believe in giving everyone a voice - regardless of who you are. So we build a platform that does just that. Our platform is not only our product but also our organization. A platform where people feel safe, where differences are embraced, a place where you can have fun. We strongly encourage applicants who are people of color, LGBTQ+, women, people with disabilities, and/or formerly incarcerated people, and a college degree is not strictly required. In order to give everyone a voice, we need to be as diverse as our users.
Learn more about our culture by visiting our Culture page.
Review our Privacy Policy for more information.

4390149339,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-noba-bank-group-4390149339?position=57&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=ApWXCe5LlTXUgsSzw5iJtA%3D%3D,Data Engineer,NOBA Bank Group,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Are you looking for a role where your engineering skills create real business value? As a Data Engineer in Bank Norwegian at NOBA Bank Group, you’ll help transform raw data into structured, high-quality assets. You’ll play a key role in modernizing our data landscape, adopting new technologies, and enabling the organization to innovate and grow.
About The Role

The Data & Analytics Engineering department brings together specialists in data infrastructure, analytics, and data management. Together we build a modern and reliable data foundation that supports decision-making across the business.

In this role, you’ll design, develop, and maintain robust data pipelines, partner with teams across the bank, and ensure that business-critical data needs are met. This is a great opportunity to grow your expertise, collaborate broadly, and see the tangible results of your work.
We offer a hybrid working model with two common in-office days. While we value flexibility, we also prioritize collaboration, team-building, and enjoying fikas and after-work gatherings together!
Main Tasks
Design, build, and maintain efficient data pipelines for reliable acquisition, transformation, and integration.
Develop data models that support scalable and well-structured data usage.
Safeguard data quality, security, and compliance throughout the lifecycle.
Collaborate closely with stakeholders such as Risk, Finance, and IT to ensure business needs are met.
Enhance data usage and accessibility by applying modern data engineering practices.
We Are Looking For You Who
Enjoys working with data engineering, solving technical challenges, and building solutions that make data both accessible and reliable.

We believe you are proactive and detail-oriented, with a genuine curiosity for new technologies. You thrive in collaboration, approach challenges with a “can-do” mindset, and enjoy turning complex problems into practical solutions.
For This Role, You’ll Need
Experience working in a similar role within Data Engineering
An academic degree related to the data or software engineering field
Proficiency in ETL processes and data logic, with an understanding of how data is sourced, transformed, and presented to data users.
Proficiency with Microsoft’s cloud-based data stack (SQL, Power BI, Azure, etc.).
Experience with Databricks.
Fluency in English is required, along with fluency in either Swedish or Norwegian.
It's Positive If You Have
Experience with additional BI and programming tools (e.g., dbt, Python).
A background in data roles within the financial sector.
Familiarity with Agile and Scrum methodologies.
Our offer to you
Freedom with responsibility: Flexible working arrangements to suit your work style.
Career development: Opportunities to grow and develop in your role.
Feedback culture: A supportive environment that encourages open communication.
Coaching leadership: Managers who guide and support your career journey.
A caring company culture: We genuinely care about each other!
You’ll also have the chance to work with cutting-edge data technologies and be part of shaping the future of NOBA’s data landscape.
In addition to this, it is important to us that you recognize yourself in our values: we are wholehearted, we collaborate and we take the lead.
Have you heard of NOBAVERSE?

In our NOBAVERSE, we value openness, trust, and diversity. Here, you can influence your own development and have fun along the way to achieving our goals – all in a workplace that’s a little extra bubbly! ✨
Read more about our employee value proposition on our careers page.
Read more about how our recruitment process works in the FAQ.

We care for your privacy and, with reference to the GDPR, would like to ask you not to include any sensitive personal data in your application, for examples information about ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs or information about your health.
Read more about how we handle your personal data here.
If you have any questions or concerns about the position, please contact the responsible recruiter.
We look forward to receiving your application! 🙏✨

4344531994,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-stegra-4344531994?position=58&pageNum=0&refId=bBbp%2F0yjGF0gJj54H4JmRw%3D%3D&trackingId=qZhdPj5BD5uIcxDGwDV2Wg%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Stegra,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hesitate to apply unless they meet every requirement. At Stegra, we’re building an impact-driven, sustainable company - and we need a diverse, inclusive team to make it happen.
If you share our passion but don’t meet every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You might be the perfect fit for this role, or another as we grow.
At Stegra, we’re not just building a plant, we’re proving that sustainable industry is possible. And to do that, we need people like you.

Join a team where safety comes first. Where we support each other, learn as we go, and make space for everyone to grow and contribute. This is your chance to be part of something big - while being closer to nature and leading a balanced, purposeful life.
What You’ll Do

We are building a next-generation steel plant - and data is a core part of how we operate, optimize, and scale. We believe the best data solutions are created close to the business, by teams who understand both the context and the technology. We are now looking for a Data Engineer to join our Digital Domain supporting Finance and HR. In this role, you will work at the intersection of business and technology - helping shape how data is modeled, shared, and used to drive decisions across the organization. You will be part of a cross-functional team where business experts, analysts, and developers collaborate closely.

Together, you will build data products that are reliable, understandable, and designed to be reused across the company - not just for a single use case. At the same time, you will contribute to how we build our data platform and ways of working. We aim to continuously evolve - adapting to new technologies, improving how we design data, and staying aligned with modern best practices. You will also take a leading role in facilitating the data platform roadmap - aligning needs across domains, shaping priorities, and ensuring progress on key initiatives.
Responsibilities:
Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and products that enable Finance and HR to make reliable, data-driven decisions
Work closely with business stakeholders and analysts to translate needs into scalable data models and solutions
Help structure and expose data so it is consistent, understandable, and reusable across domains
Take an active role in improving how we work with data across teams - leading and driving improvements to platform capabilities, standards, and patterns
Enable self-service use of data by making it accessible and usable beyond engineering teams
Make thoughtful trade-offs in how data is designed and delivered - balancing batch vs near-real-time, handling historical data, and shaping metrics and semantics for usability
Define and promote best practices for data quality, testing, documentation, and access control
Explore how modern tooling, including AI-assisted development, can improve how we build, test, and maintain data products
Support and coach colleagues in modern data engineering practices
What You’ll Bring

You enjoy solving real problems and are motivated by building data solutions that are actually used. You are curious about how data platforms should be designed — and how those designs need to evolve as technology and business needs change.
We believe you bring many of the following:
Several years of experience in data engineering or modern data platform development
Experience building end-to-end data solutions (ingestion, storage, transformation, consumption)
Interest in contributing beyond your immediate team — shaping shared ways of working, standards, and platform capabilities
Strong SQL skills and experience modeling data for analytical use
Experience with cloud-based data platforms and infrastructure-as-code
Knowledge of engineering practices such as CI/CD, testing, and code review
Ability to make sound technical decisions without being tied to specific tools or vendors
Interest in areas such as self-service analytics, semantic modeling, temporal data, and data governance
Experience working closely with business stakeholders, analysts, or operational teams
Strong communication skills and ability to explain technical solutions clearly
Nice to have:
Experience with, or interest in, industrial/operational (OT) data and how data from physical processes can be used for analytics and optimization
Benefits At Stegra

At Stegra, you’ll join a company where your work has real impact on the climate, the industry, and future generations. We’re building a culture rooted in safety, trust, and inclusion, where people feel supported to grow and thrive.
Read More About Benefits At Stegra Here.
Here’s what makes this role unique:
Breadth, not silos: You’ll work across multiple technical areas - data, architecture, infrastructure and backend - gaining deep understanding of our digital ecosystem.
Ownership from day one: You’ll support a specific domain and act as a bridge between operations and tech, and drive solutions all the way from need to implementation and iteration.
Close to the action: You’ll collaborate closely with stakeholders, understand their workflows, and ensure your digital solutions create real value in their day-to-day operations.
This is Stegra

Stegra is on a mission to change the global steel industry by producing green hydrogen, iron, and steel - with the goal of eliminating CO₂ emissions. Instead of coal, we use green hydrogen and fossil-free electricity, meaning our primary emissions will be water and heat.

By 2030, our ambition is to produce 5 million tonnes of green steel annually in our fully integrated, digitized, and sustainable plant in Boden, Northern Sweden - currently under construction. But this is just the beginning. Our expertise in green hydrogen will enable us to decarbonize industries beyond steel, paving the way for a cleaner future.
Our Stockholm Office

Our modern Stockholm office is located on Norra Stationsgatan, close to Karolinska University Hospital in central Stockholm. Here, you’ll find a bright, collaborative workspace where cross-functional teams connect, share ideas, and drive progress. Whether you're visiting regularly or working hybrid, you'll be part of a vibrant office culture with easy access to the city’s energy and amenities.

Founded in 2020, Stegra (formerly H2 Green Steel) was created with the purpose to decarbonize hard-to-abate industry. Construction of our first project – an integrated plant producing green hydrogen, green iron and green steel – is well underway in Boden, Sweden. Slashing emissions using green hydrogen and renewable electricity, we’re starting a clean industrial revolution. Steel is just the beginning. Together, we focus on delivering green impact – for our people, customers, investors, society and planet.

Studies show that women and underrepresented groups often hesitate to apply unless they meet every requirement. At Stegra, we’re building an impact-driven, sustainable company - and we need a diverse, inclusive team to make it happen.

If you share our passion but don’t meet every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You might be the perfect fit for this role, or another as we grow.

At Stegra, we’re not just building a plant, we’re proving that sustainable industry is possible. And to do that, we need people like you.

Join a team where safety comes first. Where we support each other, learn as we go, and make space for everyone to grow and contribute. This is your chance to be part of something big - while being closer to nature and leading a balanced, purposeful life.

What You’ll Do

We are building a next-generation steel plant - and data is a core part of how we operate, optimize, and scale. We believe the best data solutions are created close to the business, by teams who understand both the context and the technology. We are now looking for a Data Engineer to join our Digital Domain supporting Finance and HR. In this role, you will work at the intersection of business and technology - helping shape how data is modeled, shared, and used to drive decisions across the organization. You will be part of a cross-functional team where business experts, analysts, and developers collaborate closely.

Together, you will build data products that are reliable, understandable, and designed to be reused across the company - not just for a single use case. At the same time, you will contribute to how we build our data platform and ways of working. We aim to continuously evolve - adapting to new technologies, improving how we design data, and staying aligned with modern best practices. You will also take a leading role in facilitating the data platform roadmap - aligning needs across domains, shaping priorities, and ensuring progress on key initiatives.

Responsibilities:

  • Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and products that enable Finance and HR to make reliable, data-driven decisions
  • Work closely with business stakeholders and analysts to translate needs into scalable data models and solutions
  • Help structure and expose data so it is consistent, understandable, and reusable across domains
  • Take an active role in improving how we work with data across teams - leading and driving improvements to platform capabilities, standards, and patterns
  • Enable self-service use of data by making it accessible and usable beyond engineering teams
  • Make thoughtful trade-offs in how data is designed and delivered - balancing batch vs near-real-time, handling historical data, and shaping metrics and semantics for usability
  • Define and promote best practices for data quality, testing, documentation, and access control
  • Explore how modern tooling, including AI-assisted development, can improve how we build, test, and maintain data products
  • Support and coach colleagues in modern data engineering practices

What You’ll Bring

You enjoy solving real problems and are motivated by building data solutions that are actually used. You are curious about how data platforms should be designed — and how those designs need to evolve as technology and business needs change.

We believe you bring many of the following:

  • Several years of experience in data engineering or modern data platform development
  • Experience building end-to-end data solutions (ingestion, storage, transformation, consumption)
  • Interest in contributing beyond your immediate team — shaping shared ways of working, standards, and platform capabilities
  • Strong SQL skills and experience modeling data for analytical use
  • Experience with cloud-based data platforms and infrastructure-as-code
  • Knowledge of engineering practices such as CI/CD, testing, and code review
  • Ability to make sound technical decisions without being tied to specific tools or vendors
  • Interest in areas such as self-service analytics, semantic modeling, temporal data, and data governance
  • Experience working closely with business stakeholders, analysts, or operational teams
  • Strong communication skills and ability to explain technical solutions clearly

Nice to have:

  • Experience with, or interest in, industrial/operational (OT) data and how data from physical processes can be used for analytics and optimization

Benefits At Stegra

At Stegra, you’ll join a company where your work has real impact on the climate, the industry, and future generations. We’re building a culture rooted in safety, trust, and inclusion, where people feel supported to grow and thrive.

Read More About Benefits At Stegra Here.

Here’s what makes this role unique:

  • Breadth, not silos: You’ll work across multiple technical areas - data, architecture, infrastructure and backend - gaining deep understanding of our digital ecosystem.
  • Ownership from day one: You’ll support a specific domain and act as a bridge between operations and tech, and drive solutions all the way from need to implementation and iteration.
  • Close to the action: You’ll collaborate closely with stakeholders, understand their workflows, and ensure your digital solutions create real value in their day-to-day operations.

This is Stegra

Stegra is on a mission to change the global steel industry by producing green hydrogen, iron, and steel - with the goal of eliminating CO₂ emissions. Instead of coal, we use green hydrogen and fossil-free electricity, meaning our primary emissions will be water and heat.

By 2030, our ambition is to produce 5 million tonnes of green steel annually in our fully integrated, digitized, and sustainable plant in Boden, Northern Sweden - currently under construction. But this is just the beginning. Our expertise in green hydrogen will enable us to decarbonize industries beyond steel, paving the way for a cleaner future.

Our Stockholm Office

Our modern Stockholm office is located on Norra Stationsgatan, close to Karolinska University Hospital in central Stockholm. Here, you’ll find a bright, collaborative workspace where cross-functional teams connect, share ideas, and drive progress. Whether you're visiting regularly or working hybrid, you'll be part of a vibrant office culture with easy access to the city’s energy and amenities.

Founded in 2020, Stegra (formerly H2 Green Steel) was created with the purpose to decarbonize hard-to-abate industry. Construction of our first project – an integrated plant producing green hydrogen, green iron and green steel – is well underway in Boden, Sweden. Slashing emissions using green hydrogen and renewable electricity, we’re starting a clean industrial revolution. Steel is just the beginning. Together, we focus on delivering green impact – for our people, customers, investors, society and planet.

• Performance bonus: up to 10% of base, paid monthly
Location: Dubai, UAE (On-site) with relocation assistance and visa support
About the Role:

If you spend your days running A/B tests, analyzing conversion funnels, and digging into why users drop off before checkout — and you write your own SQL and Python to get those answers — this role is built for you. General analysts and program managers without hands-on technical and experimentation skills won't be a fit.

You'll own Puffy's experimentation program from hypothesis to business recommendation. You pull data from Shopify, GA4, Google Ads, and BigQuery yourself. You design the test, run the statistical analysis, and tell the business what to change and why. Every recommendation you deliver has to be clear enough that marketing, UX, and development can act on it the same week.
The Career Accelerator:

Puffy runs a high volume of experiments across a $1B+ e-commerce platform. You'll be involved in more A/B tests, conversion analyses, and data pipeline builds in your first year than most analysts see in three. You'll work across departments — marketing, UX, development, operations — which means broader context and faster pattern recognition. The pace is demanding, and it rewards people who are genuinely curious and want to sharpen their craft quickly.
What Is In It For You:
This isn't just another analytics role. This is your chance to become a true industry architect.

1. Own the Full Experimentation Cycle — Not Just the Dashboard. Most data analysts inherit someone else's reporting setup and maintain it. Here, you own hypothesis generation, test design, statistical analysis, and the business recommendation that follows. You build your own pipelines across Shopify, GA4, Google Ads, and BigQuery — no waiting in a queue for data, no presenting metrics without a point of view. You still report to the manager, but the experimentation output is yours to drive. Within 12 months, you'll have more hands-on testing reps than analysts accumulate in three years elsewhere.
2. Join the Elite Organization and Partner with the 1%:
🏆 Top 5 Mattress Brand in the USA with Over $1 Billion USD in Sales
🌍 Great Place to Work Certified
💡 Work with Forbes Council Member CEO Gain firsthand exposure to strategic decisions and high-velocity execution that drives a global brand.

3. Experience a World-Class Lifestyle. Dubai offers a tax-free salary, world-class infrastructure, year-round sunshine, and a global lifestyle that most professionals only experience later in their careers. Your entire compensation package lands in your account — no deductions, no surprises.

4. Become the Person Who Knows Why Things Convert. In most companies, analysts report numbers. Here, you're the person who tells the business what to change and why. When a test wins or loses, your analysis is the reason leadership moves. You'll build the instinct to separate signal from noise across segments, devices, and traffic sources — and the communication skills to make non-technical stakeholders act on what you find.
What You'll Need:
3+ years in data analysis, experimentation, or CRO within e-commerce, D2C, or any high-traffic transactional website (fintech, marketplaces, SaaS, banking)
Strong SQL — complex joins, window functions, CTEs across large datasets without relying on pre-built dashboards
Working Python (or R) for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and automation
Proven A/B testing experience — hypothesis design through statistical analysis to business recommendation
column2: solid grasp of sample sizing, significance thresholds, and common pitfalls

column1: Hands-on GA4 experience — event-based tracking, custom explorations, and the ability to tie GA4 data to other sources
Experience integrating ad platform data (Google Ads, Meta) with site analytics to see the full acquisition-to-conversion funnel
Ability to independently connect to and pull from multiple data sources (APIs, warehouses, flat files) without engineering support
Sharp analytical communication — complex findings distilled into concise recommendations for non-technical stakeholders
Proactive and curious — you spot testing opportunities and chase root causes before anyone asks
Good-to-Have:
Experience with BigQuery or AWS data warehousing, including scheduled queries and data modeling
Familiarity with session replay and heatmap tools (FullStory, Hotjar) for qualitative behavioral analysis
Background in Looker, Tableau, or similar dashboarding tools
The Puffy DNA:
We're restless, perpetually hungry, and fast-paced, driven by an unwavering belief that we can outperform any competitor — regardless of their size.
Be the Owner: We don't hire employees
column2: we welcome owners. You're accountable for results, not activity.

column1: Execute with Urgency: We thrive in high-stakes environments by making thoughtful decisions quickly and acting decisively.

Monthly performance bonuses (up to 10%)
Tax-free income

22 working days annual leave + UAE public holidays

UAE work visa sponsorship
International flights & airport transfers
14-day 4-star hotel accommodation
💼 Work Environment:
AI First organization with access to AI-native tool stack
Premium office environment in Dubai Internet City
Learning & development opportunities
International team collaboration (14+ nationalities)

Eligibility for selection to the Leaders Club — an elite recognition program featuring premium wellness memberships, exclusive networking with senior leadership, and annual leadership retreats
Eligibility for nomination as 'We Are' Champion of the Month — monthly recognition with gift vouchers and company-wide celebration for exceptional contributions

End of Service Gratuity per UAE law

Avstämningar av värdepapper, likvider och valutor
Din profil

Har ett genuint intresse för ekonomi och aktiemarknaden
Har goda kunskaper i Excel/Sheets

Talar och skriver flytande svenska och engelska

4430558033,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-at-netent-4430558033?position=6&pageNum=2&refId=pwSoBux2xvPKrq5C%2BJd39g%3D%3D&trackingId=KzuyDsArEGnbDhYaxxaMiw%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst,NetEnt,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Company Description

Evolution RNG is the dedicated slots division of Evolution, a world-leading innovator in online casino solutions. We bring together the industry’s most creative and successful slot brands under one powerhouse umbrella: Nolimit City, NetEnt, Red Tiger, Big Time Gaming, Sneaky Slots and Tap-A-Roo. Our mission is to build scalable, high-impact entertainment for players and operators worldwide while pushing the boundaries of slot mechanics and player engagement. By balancing creative product development with operational excellence, we deliver top-tier gaming content across the globe.
Job Description

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join the Evolution RNG Division in Stockholm. This role will operate at divisional level and be responsible for shaping, delivering and governing key data capabilities that support decision-making, operational effectiveness and risk management across the RNG organization. The successful candidate will be a booster for teams across Commercial, Product, Fraud, Customer Support, Engineering and Operations. This person will be expected to bring structure, ownership and forward-looking thinking to critical data assets, reporting, monitoring routines and governance practices across RNG.
Key Responsibilities
Business Intelligence & Data Analytics
Deliver data-driven insights and analysis to support commercial performance, product development, operational efficiency, and fraud prevention across the RNG division.
Develop, maintain, and enhance reporting solutions and Tableau dashboards, ensuring data accuracy, accessibility, and effective governance.
Translate business requirements into scalable datasets, reporting frameworks, and process improvements that support informed decision-making.
Lead and contribute to data-related initiatives and projects, collaborating with stakeholders across the organization to deliver business value.
Develop and improve fraud monitoring capabilities, identifying anomalies, player exploits, defects, and suspicious behavior across RNG products.
Drive continuous improvements in data quality, governance, monitoring processes, and operational routines.
Fraud Monitoring
Further develop fraud monitoring toolset and data framework across all RNG game providers.
Drive the further advancement of fraud detection capabilities, ensuring that monitoring approaches remain effective as game complexity evolves.
Work towards improving the scope, accuracy and effectiveness of the fraud detection toolset and related monitoring processes.
Help improve monitoring practices, operational routines and data structures that enable earlier detection of anomalies, player exploits, defects and suspicious behaviour.
Qualifications
Several years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, analytics engineering or a comparable senior data role.
Strong SQL capability and proven experience building analytical datasets, reporting layers and decision-support solutions.
Demonstrated experience working with BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, Superset or similar platforms, including ownership of reporting assets and user enablement.
Ability to operate credibly with senior stakeholders and to balance hands-on delivery with broader ownership and prioritization.
Experience working with data governance, data quality improvement and cross-functional collaboration in complex environments.
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail and an ability to work effectively in ambiguous or evolving problem spaces.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and spoken, with the ability to explain complex findings clearly to technical and non-technical audiences.
A proactive, structured and outcome-focused mindset with the ability to independently drive work forward.
Nice to Have
Experience with AWS technologies such as Athena
Experience with data management, data catalogues or operational data tools such as no-code database platforms.
Exposure to fraud analytics, anomaly detection, game monitoring or risk-related analytical domains.
Experience with Python or other scripting languages for automation and advanced analysis.
Experience with version control and development best practices such as Git and CI/CD workflows.
Background in gaming, iGaming or other digitally native, data-rich environments.
Additional Information
Our benefits are
Competitive pension, health and medical insurance
30 paid vacation days
Fitness benefit (Friskvårdsbidrag)
Professional growth
Sport activities, parties, team buildings
This is a 100% on-site position. Based in Stockholm, Sweden.

4431403617,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/arbetsledare-j%C3%A4rnv%C3%A4g-at-nyab-4431403617?position=7&pageNum=2&refId=pwSoBux2xvPKrq5C%2BJd39g%3D%3D&trackingId=A5mLA7YvHie67vLECxpRIw%3D%3D,Arbetsledare Järnväg,NYAB,"Hägersten, Stockholm County, Sweden","NYAB växer med de individer som utmärker sig som entreprenörer med nyfikenhet och kunskap. Med nya spännande projekt inom vårt järnvägssegment behöver vi bli fler för att möta vår växande marknadsandel. Vi håller oss till investeringsprojekt och BEST-K.
Vi söker nu dig som är vass inom BEST-K och vill vara med i vår resa att göra NYAB till en av de större aktörerna inom järnvägssektorn.
Varför ska du jobba på NYAB?
NYAB är ett företag för människor som gillar ansvar, tempo och raka besked.
Här har du kollegor som ställer upp, en ledning som litar på dig och en kultur där man både kan skratta, jobba hårt och vara stolt över det man levererar.
Vi driver projekt med glädje, engagemang och ett öga för detaljer. VI har ingen onödig byråkrati – bara bra människor, schyssta förutsättningar och tydliga mål.

Du kommer in i en snabbfotad arbetsgrupp med högt engagemang och högt tempo. Alla hjälps åt och stöttar varandra. Det finns gott om skratt och god stämning, trots stundtals krävande projekt. Kvalitet och leverans ligger i fokus.
Vad får du göra?
Du är platschefens förlängda arm – den som ser till att planerna blir verklighet.

Du håller ihop laget, planerar, leder och ser till att jobbet blir gjort säkert, rätt och i tid. Du har koll på människor, maskiner, tider, kostnader och ser till att vardagen rullar – även när allt annat vill spåra ur.
Du gillar att jobba med trafikverkets investeringsprojekt och vill bygga nytt.
Du kommer bland annat att:
Leda produktionen och ansvara för laget och underentreprenörer
Planera arbetet tillsammans med platschef
Ha ansvar för din del av ekonomin
Motivera teamet och säkra bra arbetsmiljö
Fixa arbetsberedningar, risker och avvikelser
Se till att vi följer Trafikverkets krav och säkerhet till punkt och pricka
Jobbet innebär resor – vi är där spåren finns. Än så länge är vi verksamma från Östergötland till Kiruna.
Läs mer om några av våra projekt:
Spårbyte sträckorna Tillberga-Kolbäck & Valskog-Frövi
Utbyggnad av dubbelspår mellan Avesta Krylbo och Dalslund
Spårväxel i Hallsberg
Norrbottniabanan mellan Umeå godsbangård och Sundbäck
Är du rätt person för oss?
Vi tänker oss att du:
Har minst 2 års erfarenhet som arbetsledare eller liknande
Har jobbat med järnväg och kan BEST-K
Gillar ansvar och kan ta beslut
Fan bygga tillit och ett sammansvetsat team
Är trygg, pålitlig och tydlig – både mot kunder och kollegorna i teamet
Hanterar siffror och ekonomi utan frustration
Pratar svenska och engelska utan problem
Du är en sån som människor gärna följer. En som tar ansvar, löser problem och håller huvudet kallt.
Om NYAB

NYAB är ett nordiskt infrastruktur- och energibolag -- vi bygger det samhälle vi faktiskt behöver just nu. Järnväg, kraftledningar, vägar, broar, mark och anläggning. Allt det som gör att industrin kan ställa om, att norr och söder hänger ihop och att Sverige fungerar. Vi är aktiva från Norrbotten och Västerbotten ner till Mälardalen, börsnoterade och mitt i en väldigt spännande tillväxtfas.

Hos NYAB ges alla utrymme till att vara sig själva. Att välkomna olikheter och skapa förutsättningar för utveckling, individuellt och kollektivt, bygger en kultur av prestation. Mångfalden av människor, med olika egenskaper och kompetenser, i kombination med höga ambitioner skapar en miljö där individer trivs, utvecklas och presterar.

Genom vår entreprenöriella själ har vi skapat en familjär gemenskap med stark lagkänsla där vi vågar vara modiga och framåtlutade för att hitta nya vägar och möjligheter att utvecklas. På NYAB arbetar du inte ensam, utan tillsammans mot ett gemensamt mål med möjlighet till utveckling och interna karriärvägar. Vi tar tillvara på varje medarbetares kompetens och drivkraft i en miljö med individen i centrum och möjlighet till påverkan i varje enskild roll, med frihet under ansvar.
Ansökan
Ansök till oss med ditt CV eller LinkedIn redan nu!
Rekrytering sker löpande, vi återkommer när vi sett över din ansökan.
Om du har frågor om tjänsten kan du kontakta rekryterande chef Anel Bajramovic, Arbetschef på anel.bajramovic@nyabgroup.com
Vi strävar efter att vara en attraktiv arbetsgivare och ger lika möjligheter för alla att ansöka till våra tjänster.
Start: Enligt överenskommelse
Omfattning: Heltid
Placering: Där projekten finns

    Du är en sån som människor gärna följer. En som tar ansvar, löser problem och håller huvudet kallt.

    Om NYAB

    NYAB är ett nordiskt infrastruktur- och energibolag -- vi bygger det samhälle vi faktiskt behöver just nu. Järnväg, kraftledningar, vägar, broar, mark och anläggning. Allt det som gör att industrin kan ställa om, att norr och söder hänger ihop och att Sverige fungerar. Vi är aktiva från Norrbotten och Västerbotten ner till Mälardalen, börsnoterade och mitt i en väldigt spännande tillväxtfas.

    Hos NYAB ges alla utrymme till att vara sig själva. Att välkomna olikheter och skapa förutsättningar för utveckling, individuellt och kollektivt, bygger en kultur av prestation. Mångfalden av människor, med olika egenskaper och kompetenser, i kombination med höga ambitioner skapar en miljö där individer trivs, utvecklas och presterar.

    Genom vår entreprenöriella själ har vi skapat en familjär gemenskap med stark lagkänsla där vi vågar vara modiga och framåtlutade för att hitta nya vägar och möjligheter att utvecklas. På NYAB arbetar du inte ensam, utan tillsammans mot ett gemensamt mål med möjlighet till utveckling och interna karriärvägar. Vi tar tillvara på varje medarbetares kompetens och drivkraft i en miljö med individen i centrum och möjlighet till påverkan i varje enskild roll, med frihet under ansvar.

    Ansökan

    Ansök till oss med ditt CV eller LinkedIn redan nu!

    Rekrytering sker löpande, vi återkommer när vi sett över din ansökan.

    Om du har frågor om tjänsten kan du kontakta rekryterande chef Anel Bajramovic, Arbetschef på anel.bajramovic@nyabgroup.com

    Vi strävar efter att vara en attraktiv arbetsgivare och ger lika möjligheter för alla att ansöka till våra tjänster.

    Start: Enligt överenskommelse

    Omfattning: Heltid

    Placering: Där projekten finns
