4431877941,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analytics-specialist-at-aarke-4431877941?position=9&pageNum=2&refId=pwSoBux2xvPKrq5C%2BJd39g%3D%3D&trackingId=TnRETah0WUolRYwK%2Fsjc2A%3D%3D,Data & Analytics Specialist,Aarke,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","ABOUT AARKE

Aarke is a Swedish design company redefining everyday kitchen appliances through premium materials, engineering, and timeless design. Founded in 2013, we are on a mission to re(de)fine the home appliances industry — combining Scandinavian design, exceptional quality, and top-tier materials in a category often overlooked by the design community.

Our flagship product, the Aarke Carbonator, has earned a devoted global following with customers in more than 30 countries. As we continue to scale internationally and expand into new product categories, we are looking for sharp, hands-on people to grow with us.
THE POSITION

This is a chance to own Aarke's entire data foundation and use it to help a fast-growing design brand grow. The Data & Analytics Specialist works as close to commercial decisions as to the data itself, and turns a strong data platform into better decisions across the business.

The role owns Aarke's data platform: the BigQuery warehouse, the data model, the pipelines and the Power BI reporting layer the company relies on. It is the source of truth across sales, e-commerce, marketing, operations, finance, product, claims and customer experience, and it gives the whole company reliable numbers to move quickly on. Keeping it accurate, available and trusted is what makes everyone else faster.

Just as important, this is an enabling role across the whole business. A large part of the job is helping Aarke grow and run well: on the commercial side, turning data into insight on sales, channels, conversion, customer lifetime value and retention
column2: and on the operational side, giving Operations, Logistics and Finance the insight they need on inventory, supply, fulfilment, returns, service and cost-to-serve. The role works with the e-commerce, performance marketing and CRM teams as well as the operations, logistics and finance teams so decisions across the company are grounded in real numbers. It suits someone who enjoys sitting between business and technology, wants to keep learning across both, and brings findings to the teams rather than waiting to be asked.

column1: The role owns the platform and makes it easy for others to use. Teams across the company work with the data and run their own tools, while this role holds the model, the quality and the standards everyone relies on. We are looking for someone who is not tied to any one technology: someone who builds well on what we have today, keeps a clear view of what good looks like elsewhere, and is confident and bold enough to bring in better ways of doing things when they will move the business forward. It reports to the COO and works closely with the Online and Commercial teams as well as Operations and Finance.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Data Platform Ownership
• Own the BigQuery medallion warehouse, including architecture, pipelines, data quality and the canonical data model across all business domains.
• Own the Power BI semantic layer and the reporting infrastructure the company builds on.
• Keep the platform correct, available and trusted as the single source of truth, and resolve discrepancies between systems.
• Work with the external data consultancy during a handover period as specialist support, and take over the platform knowledge as the original build team transitions out.
Analytics that Drives Growth and Performance

• Turn data into insight across the whole business, covering both the commercial side (sales and channel performance, conversion, customer lifetime value, repeat purchase, retention and margin) and the operational side (inventory, supply, fulfilment, returns, service levels and cost-to-serve), and bring it to the teams who can act on it.
• Work closely with the Online and e-commerce team on the analytics behind the webshop and marketplaces, including funnel, conversion, channel mix and customer behaviour across markets.
• Support Performance Marketing with the data and reporting they need to measure and improve spend, attribution and channel return.
• Support CRM with the data behind lifecycle, subscription and retention performance.

• Support Operations and Logistics with the data behind inventory health, supply and demand, fulfilment and 3PL performance, returns and cost-to-serve, so the operation can be run and improved on real numbers.
• Provide the data and reporting Finance needs for margin, cost and performance analysis across products and channels.
• Bring findings to the business without being asked. This includes surfacing patterns they should know about, beyond the reports they request.
• Own the cross-domain questions and data that do not obviously belong to any single team.
The Data Platform and Reporting
• Build and maintain the BI models and reporting to definitions set by Finance and the business owners.
• Set the standard for how data is modelled, named and trusted, so the whole company works from the same numbers and can move quickly on them.
• Partner with Finance, who own the metric definitions (net sales, GP2, active subscriber, and so on), while this role builds the models that produce them.

• Work alongside the Online, CRM and Performance Marketing teams, who run their own channel tools such as GA4, Polar and Klaviyo. This role owns how that data flows into the warehouse and ties together, rather than owning the tools themselves. This is a key interface with the work the online team holds today.
Ways of Working
• Work as part of a small data and systems team, clear on who is responsible for what, and ready to help across the edges.
• Partner with the Automation & AI Specialist. This role looks after the data platform and reporting, the other looks after systems and automation.
• Use AI in day-to-day work and build data that is easy for the wider team to query and use.
QUALIFICATIONS
• Strong data engineering capability, comfortable owning a cloud data warehouse (BigQuery or equivalent), pipelines and a medallion-style architecture.
• Strong analytical and commercial sense, able to turn data into decisions rather than only dashboards.
• Hands-on with SQL and a BI or semantic layer (Power BI or equivalent).
• Experience across e-commerce or consumer-goods data, spanning commercial data (sales, marketing) and operational data (inventory, supply chain, logistics, fulfilment), is an advantage.
• AI-fluent in working method, and curious about applying AI to analysis and tooling.
• Fluency in English.
PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES
• Proactive, and shares insight with the business rather than waiting to be asked.
• Takes care of the numbers and wants them accurate and trusted across the company.
• Improvement-minded, and keeps the platform healthy and makes it better over time.
• Collaborative, and owns the platform without gatekeeping it, helping teams get what they need.
• Comfortable taking over and looking after something the original team is moving on from.
• Not tied to any one technology, keeps up with what good looks like elsewhere, and is confident enough to challenge the current setup and try better ways of doing things.
WHY AARKE?

At Aarke you get to work with consumer products that people genuinely love — and contribute to building the paid media engine that helps us grow globally. We are a small, hands-on team where your work has real impact and visibility.
We offer a collaborative environment, strong learning opportunities, and the chance to grow within a fast-scaling international company. Our headquarters is in central Stockholm, Vasastan.
WHAT NOW?
Please submit your application through career.aarke.com/jobs. We review applications continuously.
For questions about the role, please contact Anna Korling (Chief People Officer) at anna@aarke.com.

4423415185,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-quartr-4423415185?position=3&pageNum=5&refId=eGNygtZBy6bOfQA3JKcmBw%3D%3D&trackingId=rbHDgYRSrBFZeP1wElXUlg%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Quartr,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Quartr is a software company serving the world of finance. Every day, leading companies and financial institutions worldwide use our products to research faster and understand deeper.

For too long, markets have been obsessed with numbers. Everyone has access to the same quantitative data, while qualitative insights – the words, the signals, the nuance – have remained mostly in the shadows. We make those insights accessible, searchable, and actionable.

Our products are used daily by some of the most sophisticated teams in finance and tech, including major hedge funds and Magnificent 7 companies, and over a million finance professionals have downloaded our mobile app.
We're backed by some of the smartest minds in our industry and growing fast at a global scale, and we're still at the very beginning of what we expect to be a multi-decade journey.

Now, we are looking for a Data Engineer to join our new Data Catalog team and play a key role in building the data backbone that powers Quartr's financial intelligence. You'll own the systems that keep our data consistent, queryable, and discoverable across an evolving landscape of storage and indexing technologies.
What you'll do
Design, build, and operate production database systems with a focus on reliability, performance, and correctness at scale.
Architect and maintain real-time sync pipelines between primary databases and secondary query stores (e.g., search, vector, graph, analytics).
Own schema versioning and distribution. Packaging, publishing, and evolving database and event schemas across teams.
Evaluate and integrate secondary indexing technologies as the needs of our AI-powered products change.
Collaborate with product, data, and infrastructure teams to ensure data is accurate, consistent, and accessible in near real time.
Who you are
A deep generalist on the data infrastructure side with genuine expertise in how databases behave under real production conditions.
Self-driven and confident, taking full ownership of your work.
Pragmatic but quality-conscious, knowing when to move fast and when to build for the long term.
Based in Sweden with a valid work permit.
Required Skills
Hands-on relational database experience: query optimization, schema design, scaling, and operational concerns. Strong SQL experience is also required.
Proven experience syncing databases in near real time, database-to-database or database-to-query-store (e.g., Typesense, Elasticsearch)
Experience with at least one secondary indexing category: search, vector, graph, or analytics.
Cloud infrastructure experience, preferably AWS.
A plus, but not required
Experience with CDC and event streaming technologies such as Kafka or NATS.
Familiarity with data catalog or data platform tooling, e.g., Confluent, Databricks, Redpanda, or similar.
Python or TypeScript proficiency.
What we offer
Do your life's work: The world's leading finance professionals use Quartr daily. We do fewer things better and hold a very high bar for quality.
Join the foundational team: We're still at the very beginning of a multi-decade journey. You'll have a big influence on product, culture, and how we win.
Meritocracy over hierarchy: You'll join a culture where the best idea wins. We value clear thinking, leave ego at the door, and obsess over the details.
Office culture with flexibility: Join us in one of our offices. We value being together, while also offering the flexibility to work from home when needed.
Compensation & well-being: We offer competitive salaries, benefits, and a generous wellness allowance to support your physical and mental health.
We include occupational pension for all employees in Sweden.

en möjlighet att vara en del av ett professionellt och kompetent arbetslag
varierande arbetsuppgifter, både i grupp och enskilt arbete

Om tjänsten
Klinisk patologi och cancerdiagnostik ingår i Medicinsk Diagnostik Karolinska. Vi finns inom Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset.
Nu erbjuder vi möjligheten att söka en tjänst som undersköterska till vårt histologilaboratorium.

Inom Klinisk patologi och cancerdiagnostik studerar vi vävnad och celler för att utreda och diagnostisera sjukdomar där alla våra medarbetare är en viktig del i kedjan. Till vårt laboratorium kommer prover som prepareras och färgas för att kunna studeras i mikroskop eller digitalt.
Vårt arbete har en avgörande betydelse för att bedöma sjukdomsutveckling och planera behandling för patienten.

Att arbeta som undersköterska hos oss är både stimulerande, omväxlande och utmanande. Du har ett nära samarbete med övriga kollegor inom avdelningen som består av biomedicinska analytiker, läkare, medicinska sekreterare och andra funktioner.

Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer bland annat att innefatta arbete i det histologiska flödet, hantering av vävnadsprover, preparering och materialhantering samt andra laborativa och administrativa arbetsuppgifter.

hantering och registrering av vävnadsprover
arbete inom histologiska processer (preparering, flöden och laboratoriestöd)
arkivering och materialhantering
samarbete med övriga yrkeskategorier inom laboratoriet
andra förekommande arbetsuppgifter på laboratoriet
Tjänsten avser ett vikariat på ett år från 1/9.
Vi söker dig som
är lugn, uppmärksam och tillmötesgående i ditt bemötande, med förmåga att arbeta noggrant och metodiskt i en laborativ miljö
har lätt för att anpassa dig till ändrade omständigheter och kan snabbt ställa om vid förändringar i arbetsflödet
arbetar bra med andra människor och samarbetar smidigt med kollegor för att uppnå gemensamma mål
kommunicerar tydligt och är lyhörd i samarbetet med andra
tar ansvar för dina arbetsuppgifter och bidrar till en god arbetsmiljö.

Krav

Bevis om skyddad yrkestitel för undersköterskor (öppnas i nytt fönster) eller arbetsgivarintyg som styrker att du hade tillsvidareanställning som undersköterska 1 juli 2023 i enlighet med övergångsbestämmelserna (2021:739) i Patientsäkerhetslagen (2010:659) (öppnas i nytt fönster). Du som söker med arbetsgivarintyg ska bifoga betyg från din undersköterskeexamen.
Goda kunskaper i svenska i tal och skrift

Tidigare erfarenhet av laboratoriearbete
Erfarenhet av histologi eller patologi
Om rekryteringsprocessen

För att din ansökan ska bedömas komplett ska den innehålla bevis om skyddad yrkestitel från Socialstyrelsen eller arbetsgivarintyg som styrker tillsvidareanställning som undersköterska 1 juli 2023. Notera att du som söker med arbetsgivarintyg även behöver bifoga betyg från din undersköterskeexamen för att ansökan ska bedömas komplett.

Välkommen med din ansökan – Tillsammans är vi Karolinska!

Inför tillsättning av befattning som innefattar vård av barn och ungdom, kontrolleras den som erbjuds tjänsten mot misstanke- och belastningsregistret. Här kan du läsa mer om rekryteringsprocessen och om vilka kontroller som utförs på Karolinska.
Som anställd på Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset kan du komma att krigsplaceras. Läs mer om vad det innebär här.
Om du är eller tidigare har varit anställd i Region Stockholm kommer vi att ta interna referenser från din nuvarande eller tidigare chef om du blir aktuell för anställning.
För tillsvidaretjänster kan provanställning komma att tillämpas.
Om Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset

Vi på Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset är stolta över att vi är ett av världens främsta universitetssjukhus. Förutom vårt särskilda ansvar för den högspecialiserade vården i Stockholmsregionen är huvuduppdraget att tillsammans med Karolinska Institutet utbilda framtidens vårdanställda och bedriva världsledande forskning samtidigt som vi fortsätter vårt arbete med att utveckla vården för patientens bästa. Vi arbetar inom en rad olika områden och professioner.
Du kan läsa mer om vårt uppdrag inom vård, forskning och utbildning här.
Vår vision - Vi ska bota och lindra imorgon det ingen kan bota och lindra idag.
Läs gärna mer om oss på karolinska.se och följ oss på sociala medier!
Funktion Medicinsk Diagnostik Karolinska

Funktion Medicinsk Diagnostik Karolinska är en sammanslagning av Karolinska Universitetslaboratoriet och Bild och Funktion med laboratorieverksamhet, radiologi, nuklearmedicin, medicinsk strålningsfysik, radiofarmaci och strålsäkerhet inom Karolinska Universitetssjukhuset.
Här kan du läsa mer om Medicinsk Diagnostik Karolinska.
Här kan du läsa mer om Karolinska Universitetslaboratoriet.
Att söka jobb i Region Stockholm
Vi eftersträvar jämställdhet och jämlikhet på vår arbetsplats och ser gärna sökande med olika bakgrund och förutsättningar.

Vi tar endast emot ansökningar via detta system. Ansök genom att klicka på knappen ”Ansök”. Ansökningar per brev eller e-post beaktas inte. Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.

Vårt rekryteringssystem kan inte hantera anonyma ansökningar eller sökande med skyddade personuppgifter. Om du har skyddade personuppgifter ber vi dig att kontakta den kontaktpersonen som finns angiven i annonsen. Din ansökan kommer då att hanteras utanför rekryteringssystemet. Du bör även vara försiktig med vilken information du lämnar i din ansökan och endast ta med information som är relevant för den aktuella befattningen.

Region Stockholm ansvarar för hälso- och sjukvård, kollektivtrafik, regional utveckling och bidrar till kulturlivet. Varje dag, dygnet runt. I landets snabbaste växande region. Tillsammans skapar vi Europas attraktivaste storstadsregion.

Att söka jobb i Region Stockholm

Vi eftersträvar jämställdhet och jämlikhet på vår arbetsplats och ser gärna sökande med olika bakgrund och förutsättningar.

Vi tar endast emot ansökningar via detta system. Ansök genom att klicka på knappen ”Ansök”. Ansökningar per brev eller e-post beaktas inte. Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare av ytterligare jobbannonser.

Vårt rekryteringssystem kan inte hantera anonyma ansökningar eller sökande med skyddade personuppgifter. Om du har skyddade personuppgifter ber vi dig att kontakta den kontaktpersonen som finns angiven i annonsen. Din ansökan kommer då att hanteras utanför rekryteringssystemet. Du bör även vara försiktig med vilken information du lämnar i din ansökan och endast ta med information som är relevant för den aktuella befattningen.

Region Stockholm ansvarar för hälso- och sjukvård, kollektivtrafik, regional utveckling och bidrar till kulturlivet. Varje dag, dygnet runt. I landets snabbaste växande region. Tillsammans skapar vi Europas attraktivaste storstadsregion.

Minst 5 års arbetslivserfarenhet i rollen som Data Engineer, där konsult har erfarenhet av att utveckla organisationsövergripande data- och analysplattformar
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av utveckling av dataflöden, datamodeller eller analyslösningar i Microsoft Fabric och/eller Microsoft Azure Synapse.

Meriterande

4429962831,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-angry-birds-2-at-rovio-entertainment-4429962831?position=6&pageNum=5&refId=eGNygtZBy6bOfQA3JKcmBw%3D%3D&trackingId=iR67GAuAEUnKQ3zJhZG1Pw%3D%3D,"Senior Data Analyst, Angry Birds 2",Rovio Entertainment,"Stockholm, Stockholm County, Sweden","Rovio is part of the SEGA family and world famous for our flagship IP Angry Birds - want to know more about Rovio as an employer? Click here.

Data is at the heart of everything we do at Rovio. It enables us to continually improve our games and provide incredible experiences for the millions of users who play our games every day. All of this data is only valuable if it can be used to guide our day to day operations and the mission of the Data and Analytics craft is to turn that raw data into actionable insights.

We are now looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our flagship Angry Birds 2 game in the Stockholm studio. Coming into this role, you will build an understanding of the game and different business levers and use that knowledge to build new game features, optimising existing ones, structure vague problem statements in collaboration with key stakeholders. You will be working as a part of a live ops pod, supporting it as a dedicated team member.

The team is located in our studio in the centre of Stockholm and works in a hybrid mode (3 days per week onsite). Currently not based in Sweden? No problem at all, we will help you with the relocation and work permit if needed!
You will have impact and fun at work by:
Working with the team members of your pod, participating in the end to end process of new feature development from planning, research and testing to launch and post launch evaluation
Bringing an objective perspective and recommendations to all phases of decision making, leveraging your technical skills to mine raw data for valuable information
Constantly designing and running in-game A/B test experiments, helping the team to find options leading to better game monetization performance and higher audience satisfaction
Creating dashboards and reports to help the game team understand your findings
Proactively working with data exploration and looking for opportunities to improve gameplay experience
Working closely with peer Data Analysts, Product Managers, Designers and Producers to continuously improve how we are interpreting game metrics and making decisions
Coaching and sharing best practices between colleagues in the data craft and whole game team
Experience and skills we are looking for:
High proficiency in SQL
Proficiency in scripting languages such as Python or R
Solid understanding of statistics (hypothesis testing, regression, analysing bias)
Experience in product analysis and experiments design including A/B testing
Excellent presentation and collaboration skills with proven ability to present data and provide actionable recommendations to senior stakeholders
Prior experience as an analyst in mobile games or apps with large audiences
Passion for playing games and understanding what makes a successful feature
You'll work in a hybrid setup with two to three days at the office per week. Kindly send us your CV in English. We look forward to speaking to you!
We welcome applicants to use the name they want to be addressed by in the application form, regardless of whether it is their legal name.

At Rovio, we are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities or conditions in our working environment and job application procedures. We are committed to continuously improving workplace accessibility. If you have any questions before applying, please write to us at recruitment@rovio.com.

4432252521,https://se.linkedin.com/jobs/view/se-manager-at-apple-4432252521?position=7&pageNum=5&refId=eGNygtZBy6bOfQA3JKcmBw%3D%3D&trackingId=xM255wFionxTLfK4jgmlRA%3D%3D,SE-Manager,Apple,"Solna, Stockholm County, Sweden","Summary

Apple Retail is where the best of Apple comes together. We bring our expertise to help people do what they love, delivering an only-at-Apple experience. We believe inclusion is a shared responsibility and we work together to foster a culture where everyone belongs and is inspired to do their best work.

As a Manager at an Apple Store, you lead, coach, and develop a team who delivers exceptional customer experiences. You prioritize actively participating in customer interactions with team members throughout the store to achieve performance goals and business priorities.

A Manager is responsible for driving the performance of key goals within assigned areas of the store, in addition to company priorities. You work alongside and collaborate with others to execute strategy and deliver on Apple’s goals.
Description
Lead a team, developing and empowering each team member to learn, grow, and achieve performance and developmental goals.
Assist with recruiting, training, developing, and retaining a diverse, high-performing team.

Actively participate in Floor Leadership by interacting with team members and customers throughout the store, modeling what good looks like, and making sure that business priorities are met and exceptional customer service is delivered.
Address customer and team member concerns and escalations, and partner with leadership and key business or People partners when appropriate.
Drive business priorities and achieve store performance goals by planning and executing operational strategies within assigned functional areas.
Maintain and uphold company policies and procedures, and protect all company assets, including confidential business, customer, team member, and financial information.
Perform other tasks as needed.
Contribute to an inclusive environment by respecting each other’s differences and having the curiosity to learn.
Demonstrate Apple’s values of inclusion and diversity in daily activities.
Be a role model for inclusive leadership behaviors and build, develop, and retain diverse teams.
Take action to create a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment for all team members.
Minimum Qualifications
You should:

Be available to work a schedule based on business needs that may include nights, weekends, and public holidays in the retail store, and reliably attend work as scheduled, in line with local laws and subject to any approved accommodations.
Be proficient in English and the local language, both written and spoken.
Have experience leading others in retail, sales, or a related field.
Preferred Qualifications
You can:
Exceed goals successfully, and persist in accomplishing objectives despite obstacles and setbacks.
Follow through on commitments and establish mechanisms to encourage others to do the same.
Instill trust within the team and operate with a high level of integrity.
Make timely and sound decisions by asking questions and using analytics, experience, and judgment.
Communicate with excellence, and tailor your communication style to different audiences.
Develop others through mentorship, coaching, and effective feedback.
Provide support and guide others through challenges while remaining calm in a fast-paced and constantly changing retail environment.
Resolve conflict and settle differences in productive ways.

At Apple, we're not all the same. And that's our greatest strength. We draw on the differences in who we are, what we've experienced, and how we think. Because to create products that serve everyone, we believe in including everyone. Therefore, we are committed to treating all applicants fairly and equally. We will work with applicants to make any reasonable accommodations.

At Apple, we believe accessibility is a fundamental human right. You’ll find that idea reflected in everything here — in our culture, our benefits and our digital tools. By welcoming as many perspectives as possible, we help you build a career where you feel like you belong.
Learn about accessibility in Apple’s workplace

As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

KAYAK’s Organic Growth team, within Marketing SEO, uses travel search data to create timely, data-driven stories for media and public relations. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can turn raw search signals into clear, reusable datasets, automated pipelines, and easy-to-use dashboards that support global PR and editorial campaigns.
This role will be required to work from our Copenhagen office at least 3 days per week.
In This Role, You Will
Receive and scope data requests from PR and local media, then deliver clean, press-ready datasets.
Work with PR and communications partners to shape data angles and check feasibility.
Build and maintain automated SQL/Python pipelines and templates for recurring requests.
Create and maintain self‑service dashboards and lightweight tools (e.g., Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau) so teams can quickly explore trends.
Validate data quality and collaborate with Data Engineering to improve underlying models and delivery.
Document methods and make outputs easy for non-technical audiences to use.
Please Apply If You Have
A Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst, marketing analyst, or similar role.
Practical experience working with SQL and extracting/manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience in Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.

Strong analytical thinking and the ability to translate complex data into clear, media-ready insights.
Comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical partners and delivering work on tight timelines.
Nice-to-have
Experience building reusable pipelines or automation for recurring reports.
Experience working with marketing, search, or travel data.
Experience building lightweight self-serve dashboards.
Benefits And Perks
Work from (almost) anywhere for up to 20 days per year
Focus on mental health and well-being:

Company-paid subscription to HeadSpace

No meeting Fridays

Paid volunteer time
Focus on your career growth:
Development Dollars
Leadership development
Access to thousands of on-demand e-learnings
Travel Discounts
Employee Resource Groups

Inclusion

About the Role:

For our client in the financial industry, we are looking for two intermediate Data Analysts to join and support an active Execution Team within a large-scale remediation program. In this analytical and delivery-focused role, you will handle daily customer base corrections, balance complex actuarial calculations within the core system, and execute predefined solutions under a structured project management framework.


  • Leading the daily handling, grouping, and preparation of complex customer data lists for correction.
  • Analysing customer accounts and calculating specific data corrections within the core system according to defined business logic.
  • Proactively guiding data verification, highlighting edge cases, and analysing variations that deviate from mainstream profiles.
  • Facilitating execution tracking and supporting the responsible data expert during high-volume delivery phases.
  • Collaborating closely with team leads, project managers, and data experts to ensure remediation targets stay on schedule for the end-of-year milestone.


Required Skills:

  • Strong generalist data competencies with a proven flair for data analysis and hands-on base corrections.
  • Deep processing expertise utilizing a comprehensive toolkit including advanced Excel and functional SQL knowledge.
  • Proven track record working within a structured, deadline-oriented project management environment.
  • Experience maintaining high adaptability and flexibility while handling varied tasks depending on changing project needs.
  • Professional fluency in Danish (written and spoken) to effectively interact with localized core databases and teams.
  • Professional fluency in English (written and spoken).


Preferred Skills:

  • Recently completed education as an Actuary or a highly similar analytical/mathematical background.
  • Prior experience working on data cleanup, compliance, or remediation initiatives within the financial or insurance sectors.


Recann is supporting a client on a contract Data Analyst requirement for delivery on an international programme.


Recann is acting as an employment business in relation to this vacancy.

4433383082,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-airbus-4433383082?position=4&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=NLOZnTJzwHIUq2OmY9zmWA%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Airbus,Copenhagen Metropolitan Area,"Job Description

Would you like to pair practical tasks with data analytics, finding joy in structuring, providing, and explaining data? Do you have a flair for structure and visual presentations? At Satair, data and analytics today serve as one of our largest levers for accelerating a continuous improvement culture, driving efficiency and competitiveness. We are looking for a new colleague with exceptional analytical skills, curiosity for tools and processes and an appetite to help execute on the results derived.

In this role, you will play a key role in enabling data driven decision making to support our mission within Infrastructure & Operations, supporting critical tasks for IT infrastructure and support. Instead of waiting for outcomes to happen, you will help us actively influence our operating terms. You will work within small, multi-disciplinary teams with a core focus on delivering pragmatic, sustainable solutions directly to and with our technical departments. Crucially, you will not be alone or required to build everything from scratch
column2: you are joining a larger, well-established data mesh organization where you can leverage robust enterprise data foundations and collaborate with a global network of data professionals.

column1: Tasks & Accountabilities
You will contribute to the team through your analysis and technical execution across the following key responsibilities:

ITSM Platform Transformation: Satair is currently changing its ITSM platform and wants to retain high-level data to monitor developing trends across platforms. In this context, you will handle data transfers and work hand-in-hand with functional teams to develop and improve dashboards and reports.

Actionable Diagnosis & Problem Solving: Ad-hoc tasks will occur where you will be expected to analyze complex technical data to help identify potential root causes. You will deconstruct problems and proactively develop actionable solutions through process automation, tools, dashboards, or presentations.

Pragmatic Execution: You will manage your time dynamically so you do not focus on the analysis only, but also actively commence on execution. This includes developing minimum viable products (MVPs) and digital products that directly address functional pain points.

Community Integration: You will become a part of our wider data mesh community to help us understand and use knowledge gained across the business. Stepping into this well-established data mesh ecosystem means you won't be reinventing the wheel - instead, you will network across teams to re-use existing data products and infrastructure . Within this framework, you will act as a focal point driving awareness and adoption of technologies and analytics.

Stakeholder Management: Your communication skills and structural mindset will be essential, as we serve many different stakeholders and must ensure our messages are well received at all ends. You will articulate complex ideas clearly and effectively to diverse audiences.
Your Profile

We are looking for a quick learner who is passionate about their work, possesses an entrepreneurial mindset, and has a strong drive to get things done. You hold a Bachelor's degree or have 2 - 4 years of experience in a similar position with a strong eagerness to combine deep analysis with practical, hands-on work.
Technical Toolbox
You have a demonstrated understanding of data types and can successfully work with and combine structured and unstructured data.
You possess solid experience with databases, data structures, collaboration tools, and scripting languages.
Robust coding abilities (e.g., Python, SQL) and experience working with APIs, algorithms, or data integrations are considered a highly valuable plus.
Experience with or exposure to Palantir Foundry is highly advantageous, as it serves as a central pillar of our data tech stack and analytics ecosystem
You know how to utilize your own technical ""toolbox"" to share data through structured exhaustive lists, diagrams, or pivot tables.
Mindset & Interpersonal Skills
You have a strong ability to work independently, maintain consistency in terms of deliverables, and bring an independent yet cooperating drive to the team.
You excel at building long-term relationships and networking across a large organization to collaborate, share knowledge, and re-use existing solutions.
You understand the unique challenges of different departments, allowing you to empathize with their concerns and tailor your approach to support technology adoption.
Travel in this position can occur but is currently deemed a rare case (e.g., less than 10 days per year).
What does your next challenge look like?
At Satair, we believe in sustainable aviation that allows people to travel and connect across the globe.
We are on a mission to provide world class material solutions that keep aircraft flying and extend their lifespan.
In Satair, you can challenge the status quo, pioneer new ways of working, and generate real value for airlines and suppliers worldwide.
Being part of the Airbus family gives us the opportunity to live a global mindset, working in international teams and collaborating across functions, sites and cultures.
If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow.
Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!
About SATAIR

Satair offers first class material management solutions in the aftermarket business of the aviation industry. We are working to be the most customer-centric organization in providing commercial aviation spares parts to make the world continue flying. We are constantly taking customer experience to new levels by improving how we fulfill and deliver customers’ orders quickly and accurately, anywhere in the world. To meet this goal, Satair is continually striving to innovate and provide best-in-class service levels by pioneering new products and services in a fast growing business environment.

With our global functional organization spanning across 11 different sites and locations worldwide with more than 1.800 people and a turnover of $3.0 billion, you will be part of a truly global family.

Our customers have the ultimate goal to operate their fleet and our Airbus aircraft safely, efficiently in a cost-time-quality effective manner and of course with the expected comfort level for their customers. We at Satair do everything to support our customers in this.
If you want to know more about our business, have a closer look at our website www.SATAIR.com or check out our SATAIR Youtube Channel at www.youtube.com/sataircompany !
What do we offer at SATAIR?

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!
Here is a selection of some of the benefits we offer our employees in Copenhagen:
Competitive remuneration, including short term incentive bonus on all levels
Work/life balance: hybrid working, flex time, maternity/paternity/parental leave, 29 days of holiday + senior and flex days
Personal development: personalized development plans, large portfolio of learning solutions and many internal mobility opportunities, both locally and globally
Health and well-being: Health insurance, in-house canteen with many healthy options
Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company
Please send us your application as soon as possible to get the opportunity to become part of an exceptional global team!
We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.
When applying for the position, you accept our data privacy statement: http://www.SATAIR.com/privacy
This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.
Company
Satair A/S
Contract Type
Permanent
Experience Level
Professional
Job Family
Digital

By submitting your CV or application you are consenting to Airbus using and storing information about you for monitoring purposes relating to your application or future employment. This information will only be used by Airbus.

Airbus is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.

Airbus is, and always has been, committed to equal opportunities for all. As such, we will never ask for any type of monetary exchange in the frame of a recruitment process. Any impersonation of Airbus to do so should be reported to emsom@airbus.com.
At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking.

    We are looking for a quick learner who is passionate about their work, possesses an entrepreneurial mindset, and has a strong drive to get things done. You hold a Bachelor's degree or have 2 - 4 years of experience in a similar position with a strong eagerness to combine deep analysis with practical, hands-on work.

    Technical Toolbox

    • You have a demonstrated understanding of data types and can successfully work with and combine structured and unstructured data.
    • You possess solid experience with databases, data structures, collaboration tools, and scripting languages.
    • Robust coding abilities (e.g., Python, SQL) and experience working with APIs, algorithms, or data integrations are considered a highly valuable plus.
    • Experience with or exposure to Palantir Foundry is highly advantageous, as it serves as a central pillar of our data tech stack and analytics ecosystem
    • You know how to utilize your own technical ""toolbox"" to share data through structured exhaustive lists, diagrams, or pivot tables.

    Mindset &
column6:
Interpersonal Skills

  • You have a strong ability to work independently, maintain consistency in terms of deliverables, and bring an independent yet cooperating drive to the team.
  • You excel at building long-term relationships and networking across a large organization to collaborate, share knowledge, and re-use existing solutions.
  • You understand the unique challenges of different departments, allowing you to empathize with their concerns and tailor your approach to support technology adoption.
  • Travel in this position can occur but is currently deemed a rare case (e.g., less than 10 days per year).

What does your next challenge look like?

At Satair, we believe in sustainable aviation that allows people to travel and connect across the globe.

We are on a mission to provide world class material solutions that keep aircraft flying and extend their lifespan.

In Satair, you can challenge the status quo, pioneer new ways of working, and generate real value for airlines and suppliers worldwide.

Being part of the Airbus family gives us the opportunity to live a global mindset, working in international teams and collaborating across functions, sites and cultures.

If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow.

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!

About SATAIR

Satair offers first class material management solutions in the aftermarket business of the aviation industry. We are working to be the most customer-centric organization in providing commercial aviation spares parts to make the world continue flying. We are constantly taking customer experience to new levels by improving how we fulfill and deliver customers’ orders quickly and accurately, anywhere in the world. To meet this goal, Satair is continually striving to innovate and provide best-in-class service levels by pioneering new products and services in a fast growing business environment.

With our global functional organization spanning across 11 different sites and locations worldwide with more than 1.800 people and a turnover of $3.0 billion, you will be part of a truly global family.

Our customers have the ultimate goal to operate their fleet and our Airbus aircraft safely, efficiently in a cost-time-quality effective manner and of course with the expected comfort level for their customers. We at Satair do everything to support our customers in this.

If you want to know more about our business, have a closer look at our website www.SATAIR.com or check out our SATAIR Youtube Channel at www.youtube.com/sataircompany !

What do we offer at SATAIR?

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!

Here is a selection of some of the benefits we offer our employees in Copenhagen:

  • Competitive remuneration, including short term incentive bonus on all levels
  • Work/life balance: hybrid working, flex time, maternity/paternity/parental leave, 29 days of holiday + senior and flex days
  • Personal development: personalized development plans, large portfolio of learning solutions and many internal mobility opportunities, both locally and globally
  • Health and well-being: Health insurance, in-house canteen with many healthy options

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company

Please send us your application as soon as possible to get the opportunity to become part of an exceptional global team!

We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.

When applying for the position, you accept our data privacy statement: http://www.SATAIR.com/privacy

This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.

Company

Satair A/S

Contract Type

Permanent

Experience Level

Professional

Job Family

Digital

By submitting your CV or application you are consenting to Airbus using and storing information about you for monitoring purposes relating to your application or future employment. This information will only be used by Airbus.

Airbus is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.

Airbus is, and always has been, committed to equal opportunities for all. As such, we will never ask for any type of monetary exchange in the frame of a recruitment process. Any impersonation of Airbus to do so should be reported to emsom@airbus.com.

At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking.

4427331442,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-worker-analytics-insights-enablement-lego-retail-at-the-lego-group-4427331442?position=8&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=%2FKrOGDIxEIDq99EMEJiFdQ%3D%3D,"Student Worker, Analytics & Insights Enablement, LEGO Retail",the LEGO Group,"Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Job Description
Are you ready to start your data and analytics career, and do you get excited by a fast paced global retail environment, then you are the one we are looking for!

This entry level position into a global and known consumer brand organization is a big opportunity. You will get to learn bottom up how the LEGO Retail business works and get exposed to the foundational commercial processes linked to our expanding data environment. Using the newest and strongest analytics tool available and big data methodologies, this role will help make sense of the data through our data lake environment.

This role offers a unique chance to experience retail data and language which you would not normally not be exposed to in a production company like The LEGO Group. This international role will expose you to many different cultures and consumer behaviours, hence, increase your awareness of how important it is to be able to collaborate across borders.

If you are new in the corporate world, want to kickstart your career and you are curious to what it takes to succeed within a global company, this entry level position in The LEGO Group and LEGO Retail is a good place to start. Our journey has just begun, and you will be able to help shape ways of working from the start and place your own unique footprint on the area.
Core Responsibilities

This role will be important for our team and help Retail BI to become a stable and trusted business partner. The person will be our safety net for reporting and ensuring our data quality and integrity, alongside validating report structures and enabling key operational measures and communication.
Number oriented, but no need for extensive IT know-how.
Should be flexible as we work in a global set up across many time zones.
Structure and precision key qualities, helping to ensure consistency & reliability across our data products.
to work across various platforms & multiple key stakeholders.
Adaptable & keen to learn across a variety of platforms & business areas, connecting with multiple key stakeholders.
Play your part in our team succeeding
The department’s key focus is to create and enable self-service data intelligence products for LEGO Retail data consumers and strategic decision makers.

Further, partnering with the analytics & insights commercial partners to craft these data points into crisp and action-oriented business recommendation to support both short term operational optimisation as well as tactical and strategical decision. The team is supporting cross functional in the commercial value chain Marketing, Finance, Operations, Stores and e-Commerce.

LEGO Retail as a standalone business unit was established in 2017 and since then we have established several standard processes and standard reporting tools, and we need help to manage and maintain these plus help with communicating daily and weekly standard reporting to key stakeholders across the business.

This position will become a key enabler to the way we operate our business on a day-to-day basis, which will give great insights to the Retail area in the LEGO Group and to how we operate as a newly start business within the business.
Do you have what it takes?
Current student on second or third year of a university or related schooling.
Bachelor’s degree in economics, Statistics, Business, Engineering or related field or equivalent working experience.
Business-related study background year 3-4 of university or related schooling.
High level of English both written and verbal is a must as there will be communication with stakeholders across the globe (Mainly UK, US and Singapore) both via mails and in meetings.
Nice to have
Global mindset, have spent time outside Denmark is a plus
Knowledge of SQL, Tableau, Power BI or similar is a benefit

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
What’s in it for you?
Here is what you can expect
Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.
Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.
Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.
Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.
Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.
Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.
Just imagine building your dream career.
Then make it real.
Join the LEGO® team today.

4433316845,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-alfa-laval-4433316845?position=9&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=tbYzrTCRroNXXxivkhK4EQ%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Alfa Laval,"Kolding, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Every day, we get opportunities to make a positive impact – on our colleagues, partners, customers and society. Together, we’re pioneering the solutions of the future and unlocking the full potential of precious resources. Trusted to act on initiative, we challenge conventional thinking to develop world-leading technologies that inspire progress in vital areas, including energy, food, water and shipping.

As we push forward, the innovative, open spirit that fuels our 140-year-old start-up culture and rapid growth also drives our personal growth. So, as we shape a more resourceful, less wasteful world, we build our careers too.
About The Job

As a Data Analyst, you’ll provide data analytics and support for business and process data, working with large datasets (including product master data in PLM and ERP systems). You’ll be part of a cross-functional R&D team (60+ colleagues), collaborating closely with manufacturing, distribution, and product management teams.
Responsibilities Include
Data analysis and exploration
Collaborate with cross functional teams to understand the data
Acquire, preprocess, and clean data from various sources to ensure quality and reliability
Analyze business and process data to identify trends and patterns impacting operations
Create informative dashboards and visualizations (primarily in Excel, Power BI).
Stay current with industry trends and best practices
What You Bring
Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Engineering, or similar, or equivalent experience.
Strong skills in Excel (required) and Power BI (nice to have).
Understanding of PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) and product master data management.
Experience in data governance and data quality management.
Language skills: English and Danish (preferred)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent organizational and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Ability to manage and drive changes in a global, multi-site manufacturing environment.
Effective communication and training abilities.
Who You Are
Systematic, detail-oriented, and collaborative. You thrive in diverse teams, balance independence with teamwork, and contribute positively to our culture.
You have a keen eye for detail, strong data discipline, and the ability to understand and manage complex data structures across multiple platforms.
We are conducting a continuous review of the applications received. We do not accept applications via email, they will be deleted, due to General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
Please send your application and resume, no later than 18th of July 2026. We look forward to hearing from you soon!

We care about diversity, inclusion, and equity in our recruitment processes. We also believe behavioral traits can provide important insights into a candidate's fit to a role. To help us achieve this we apply Pymetrics assessments, and upon application you will be invited to play the assessment games.

4426362394,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-skatteguiden-4426362394?position=10&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=Pxq8O%2FlpQ0pW0tDrCTAp%2Fw%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Skatteguiden,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed. Over én million danskere bruger Skatteguiden til at forstå og optimere deres skat. Vi automatiserer skatteovervågning, finder fradrag, som folk ikke vidste de havde, og giver brugerne indsigt til bedre økonomiske beslutninger — direkte forbundet til SKAT. Vi er ~50 mennesker i København, med en 4,7-stjernet rating og én mission: gør finansiel indsigt tilgængelig for alle, ikke kun dem der har råd til en revisor.
Rollen:

Vi har brug for en analytiker, der kan gå i dybden med den skattedata, der driver vores produkter. Du bliver den i datateamet, der forstår skattedata bedst — og en central sparringspartner for de produktteams, der bygger vores skatteberegnings- og fradragsprodukter. Du behøver ikke kende dansk skattelovgivning fra dag ét. Du skal være skarp nok på data til at lære det hurtigt.
Hvad du kommer til at arbejde med
Modellere og dokumentere skattedata i dbt på Snowflake
Udvikle og validere prædiktive modeller — dokumenteret klar til engineering handover
Designe og køre simuleringer med live data og konstruerede scenarier
Vedligeholde skattedatamodeller i takt med regulatoriske ændringer
Bidrage til observability og datakvalitet med et datadrevet perspektiv
Hvad du bringer
Stærkt SQL-fundament og erfaring med kompleks datamodellering
Erfaring med vores data stack: Snowflake, dbt, Lightdash, Evidence eller tilsvarende tooling.
Solid erfaring med prædiktiv modellering og modelvalidering
Analytisk skarp med øje for edge cases og korrekthed
Trives i et tværfunktionelt produktteam — ikke en ren engineering-rolle
Erfaring med Lightdash. Python.
Godt at have kendskab til dansk skattelovgivning, finans eller revision.
Taler flydende dansk
Hvad vi tilbyder
En teknisk dyb rolle i et komplekst og meningsfuldt domæne
Muligheden for at blive organisationens foretrukne ekspert på skattedata
Et stærkt data team med høje faglige standarder og moderne tech stack
Office first i København — fem min fra Rådhuspladsen, frokost hos Claus Meyer, aftensmad hvis dagen er lang
Praktisk Start: Hurtigst muligt
Rapporterer til: Head of Data & Operations Planning
Løn: Kompetitiv og erfaringsbaseret
Our Lean Hiring process
Screening call med Christian Payne, 30 min (online)
45 minutters møde med hiring manager og product manager (onsite)
1 times case session med hiring manager og teammedlem, efterfulgt af kort snak med Nikolai (CEO), 30 min (onsite)

4425457595,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-quantitative-analyst-at-copenhagen-energy-trading-4425457595?position=11&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=VRkeNcvgg33nhra4t%2Btmbw%3D%3D,Student Quantitative Analyst,Copenhagen Energy Trading,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Student Quantitative Analyst
Join our team at Copenhagen Energy Trading

Do you want to be part of a fast-growing energy trading company with high ambitions, a quantitative mindset, and plenty of room for personal and professional development? Are you passionate about using data to understand complex markets, mathematical modelling, and statistical analysis?
Then take a look at this exciting opportunity to develop your skills at Copenhagen Energy Trading – a young and fast-growing energy trading company based in Copenhagen.
What You’ll Be Doing

You'll join our Tech team, where we build tools and models that support every part of the trading process – from data pipelines and forecasting models to trading dashboards and automated strategies. Everything is built in Python, and quantitative analysis plays a central role in how we understand and operate in power markets.
As a Student Quantitative Analyst, you'll:
Support the team with analysis that feeds directly into trading decisions
Work with real models and market data
Improve your statistical and analytical skills by applying them to real problems
Learn about power markets and how quantitative methods enable smarter trading
Take on responsibility from day one, with guidance from an experienced team

You'll be part of an open and collaborative culture where honesty and transparency shape how we work and grow together. We believe in giving you autonomy, flexibility, and trust, and if things go well, this could lead to a full-time position after graduation.
What we offer
A student position (15–20 hours per week) with flexible working hours to ensure that you can balance work and studies without compromise
Work alongside experienced and ambitious teammates who set a high bar for quality, and learn what professional quantitative analysis looks like in the real world
The opportunity to build your influence and leave your fingerprint on the way we work in a young and fast-growing company
A steep learning curve in a fast-moving, quantitatively driven environment
Competitive pay, team events, and a super friendly vibe
A potential path to a full-time role after graduation
What We're Looking For
Currently studying a bachelor's or master's degree in Mathematics, Physics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
Strong foundations in statistics, probability, and mathematical modelling
Familiarity with Python or R for quantitative analysis
Experience with time series analysis, regression, or optimisation is a plus
Interest in energy markets, trading, or solving complex quantitative problems
A proactive mindset and eagerness to learn from a fast-paced team
Fluent in English (written and spoken)
Bonus: Experience with financial markets, algorithmic trading, or building personal quant projects
Who We Are
Copenhagen Energy Trading A/S was founded in 2021 and is part of Copenhagen Energy Group.

We specialize in short-term power trading across Europe and North America. Technology is at the heart of our operations, supported by a team of top-tier talent. Driven by data and technology, we use deep insights into electricity markets to create discretionary and algorithmic trading strategies that efficiently move power to where it's needed most.

We believe in a culture of collaborative growth and do not have a system of bureaucratic policies or rigid hierarchy. We support a strong sense of team spirit, surrounded by a like-minded team and an easy-going work culture.
Interested?

All applicants are welcome. At Copenhagen Energy Trading, we believe that diversity, equality, and inclusion are fundamental in the creation of an inspirational workplace that enables us to be innovative and creative. We interview candidates continuously and may select the right candidate prior to the expiry of the job ad. So please do not hesitate to apply.
Application Deadline: 29 juni 2026

To apply, submit your cover letter, CV and current grades merged into one document. For more information, you are welcome to contact xx or our Recruitment Manager, Anna Schumann, at as@copenhagen-energy.com.

4415885283,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-og-excel-analytiker-at-greenpeas-a-s-4415885283?position=12&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=wvVVJYNoi%2BoHq6ZrrkNjvw%3D%3D,Data- og Excel-analytiker,GreenPeas A/S,"Ringe, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Data- og Excel-analytiker søgesGreen Peas A/S
Er du en sand ørn til Excel, og motiveres du af at dykke ned i data, spotte mønstre og sikre 100% korrekte registreringer? Så er det måske dig, vi søger til vores team hos Green Peas A/S!

Vi leder efter en dygtig og detaljeorienteret profil, der arbejder analytisk, sikkert og fokuseret med datahåndtering og registrering i Excel. Stillingen er som udgangspunkt sæsonarbejde, men for den rette person er der gode muligheder for varigt arbejde.
Dine primære arbejdsopgaver:
Analysere, behandle og strukturere operationelle data.
Sikre præcis, systematisk og fejlfri registrering af korrekte data i Excel.
Identificere eventuelle uoverensstemmelser i datagrundlaget og proaktivt kvalitetssikre disse.
Hvem er du?
Stærk Excel-bruger: Du mestrer Excel og trives med at arbejde analytisk og struktureret med store mængder information.
Analytisk arbejdende: Du har en naturlig logisk sans, arbejder metodisk og formår at bevare overblikket over komplekse data.
Sikker og fokuseret: Du arbejder med stor nøjagtighed, har skarpt fokus på detaljen og går op i, at tingene er 100% korrekte hver gang.
Sproglige kvalifikationer: Du taler og skriver ubesværet på både dansk og engelsk.
Praktisk information:
Opstart: Snarest muligt.
Ansættelsesform: Som udgangspunkt sæsonarbejde – med mulighed for varigt arbejde for den rette person!
Løn: Efter kvalifikationer.
Er du den, vi leder efter?
Vi holder samtaler løbende og besætter stillingen, så snart vi har fundet den rette kandidat. Tøv derfor ikke med at række ud med det samme!
Ansøg via direkte besked, eller send din ansøgning til Peter eller Nicholai på: 👉psj@greenpeas.dk eller nst@greenpeas.dk
Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

4425974755,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-data-engineer-at-nordic-power-trading-4425974755?position=15&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=X8cqHB9AlXDOfKfR7jKh0A%3D%3D,Student Data Engineer,Nordic Power Trading,"Kolding, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","🔌 Student Data Engineer – Nordic Power Trading
📍 Hybrid (Kolding office + remote) | ⏱ Part-time | 💰 200 DKK/hour
We're scaling our data infrastructure to support systematic trading in European power markets — and we're looking for a student data engineer to own a real piece of it.
About the Role

This is a hands-on engineering role, not a setup-and-document job. You'll work at the core of our data stack, building and maintaining the pipelines that feed our trading models and analysis workflows — from raw market data ingestion to clean, structured output for the quant team.
You'll own real parts of the infrastructure from day one and be expected to deliver code that runs in production.
What you'll be doing:
• Building and maintaining Python-based data pipelines for time-series market data
• Integrating with external market data APIs (energy exchanges, transmission operators, forecast providers)
• Structuring and managing large datasets in DuckDB, Parquet, and cloud storage (GCS)
• Designing and iterating on data schemas for energy market data — actuals, forecasts, capacities, and auction results
• Automating recurring data collection, validation, and reporting tasks
• Supporting the quant team with clean, reliable data for analysis and model development
Who We're Looking For
• Enrolled in Computer Science (5th or 6th semester) or holds a bachelor's in CS
• Grade average of at least 10 on the Danish 7-point scale
• Solid Python skills and comfortable working in a real codebase with Git
• Experience with APIs and structured or time-series data
Experience with energy markets, financial data, or cloud infrastructure is a plus — but curiosity and engineering discipline matter more.
What We Offer
✅ Real ownership over live production systems from day one
✅ Direct collaboration with a quant team in systematic energy trading
✅ Full-time hours available during holidays and summer
✅ Possibility of a permanent position after graduation
✅ Low-bureaucracy environment where good work gets noticed
📩 Interested? Send us a message or apply directly. We review applications on a rolling basis.

You'll own real parts of the infrastructure from day one and be expected to deliver code that runs in production.


What you'll be doing:

• Building and maintaining Python-based data pipelines for time-series market data

• Integrating with external market data APIs (energy exchanges, transmission operators, forecast providers)

• Structuring and managing large datasets in DuckDB, Parquet, and cloud storage (GCS)

• Designing and iterating on data schemas for energy market data — actuals, forecasts, capacities, and auction results

• Automating recurring data collection, validation, and reporting tasks

• Supporting the quant team with clean, reliable data for analysis and model development


Who We're Looking For

• Enrolled in Computer Science (5th or 6th semester) or holds a bachelor's in CS

• Grade average of at least 10 on the Danish 7-point scale

• Solid Python skills and comfortable working in a real codebase with Git

• Experience with APIs and structured or time-series data

Experience with energy markets, financial data, or cloud infrastructure is a plus — but curiosity and engineering discipline matter more.


What We Offer

✅ Real ownership over live production systems from day one

✅ Direct collaboration with a quant team in systematic energy trading

✅ Full-time hours available during holidays and summer

✅ Possibility of a permanent position after graduation

✅ Low-bureaucracy environment where good work gets noticed


📩 Interested? Send us a message or apply directly. We review applications on a rolling basis.

4432667016,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/business-intelligence-specialist-at-boozt-4432667016?position=16&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=A82Y7YixflXrCvdc3s4hzw%3D%3D,Business Intelligence Specialist,Boozt,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","THE OPPORTUNITY
As the leading Nordic online department store - 1,600+ brands, 14 markets, one of the world's most automated fulfilment centres - data powers every decision we make at Boozt.
We're hiring two new colleagues across our BI function: one in our Logistics Reporting & Analytics (Warehouse & operations) and in our Commercial Business Partner Team.
Different teams, different challenges, one thing in common: your data insights help shape smarter decisions and turn them into real business impact.
Logistics Reporting & Analytics: Early Career
Operations-focused

Learn the business from the ground up. Every order that reaches a customer is the result of an efficient supply chain, a distribution network, and a customer service operation working in sync. You'll sit close to the warehouse floor, fulfilment, returns, and last mile, turning operational data into dashboards and analyses that help teams move faster and smarter.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Build, develop, and maintain QlikCloud apps end to end - from data load and modelling to the dashboards teams use every day
Write and optimise load scripts, build data models, and keep apps fast and reliable as data volumes grow
Run ad hoc analysis in Qlik to answer operational questions quickly when they come up
Turn requirements from distribution, supply chain, and customer service teams into well-built, reusable reporting
Maintain and improve our existing Qlik reporting - refactoring, fixing, and extending what's already there
Work with our data and engineering teams on data quality and the pipelines that feed Qlik (BigQuery)
YOUR PROFILE
2> years in a BI, analytics data role - internships, thesis work, and student jobs count
Comfortable with SQL and Qlik scripting / data modelling, or genuinely motivated to get there fast
Hands-on with QlikCloud, or able to learn it quickly (experience with Power BI, Tableau, or Looker transfers well
column2: we use Qlik with BigQuery)

column1: You move from ""here's the data"" to ""here's what we should build""
Curious about how the business actually works, not just the numbers it produces
Commercial Business Partner Team: Mid-level
Engineering-focused

Work with large and complex datasets to build the data foundation behind key commercial decisions. You'll design and maintain the data models, pipelines, and BigQuery architecture that our commercial teams rely on to understand performance, set priorities, and identify growth opportunities. Your work powers high-impact commercial decisions.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Design and maintain the BigQuery data infrastructure powering commercial reporting
Develop Qlik Sense scripts and maintain dashboards and lead our migration to Qlik Cloud
Integrate data from multiple source systems using SQL, including one-off data pulls when needed
Document data models, calculations, and mappings so the work stays transparent and maintainable
Partner with analysts and business partners to turn business requirements into robust data products
YOUR PROFILE
2+< years building BI solutions end to end: data models, pipelines, KPIs, dashboards
Strong SQL and scripting skills, with hands-on experience integrating data from multiple source systems
Proactive and driving, you push work forward without waiting to be told
Comfortable translating non-technical requirements into technical solutions
Bachelor's in computer science, information systems, or a related field
column2: fluent English

column1: Proven Qlik experience is a plus

BOOZT PERKS
Great personal and internal career development opportunities
A culture built on our values of trust, freedom, and responsibility
Driven and passionate international colleagues
A generous employee discount
Healthy and delicious breakfast and lunch, top-quality coffee, and fresh fruit available all day
In-house gym, onsite masseuse, and medical doctor
An amazing HQ in central Copenhagen – the iconic, energy-efficient Krystallen building, recognized for its architectural design and sustainability. Enjoy breathtaking city views and much more!

4426649044,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-worker-data-analytics-sql-snowflake-at-siemens-energy-4426649044?position=17&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=UBfNHEEhEsxEKtoPwdayFw%3D%3D,Student Worker - Data Analytics & SQL (Snowflake),Siemens Energy,"Brande, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","A Snapshot of Your Day

Join our dynamic Procurement & Logistics finance team as a Student Worker, where you will play a crucial role in ensuring the seamless processing and analysis of financial and operational data. Imagine leveraging your SQL skills within the Snowflake platform to extract and transform large datasets from SAP, preparing them for insightful analysis. You will tackle data inconsistencies, optimize processes, and support the development of data models, all while working independently on technical tasks that drive our mission forward. Your contributions will not only enhance our efficiency but also empower our finance team to make informed decisions.
How You’ll Make An Impact
You will develop SQL queries in Snowflake supported by Cortex AI tool to extract and transform data, ensuring it meets the needs of the finance and controlling teams.
Combine and structure large datasets, troubleshooting inconsistencies and implementing corrective actions in data pipelines.
Optimize and automate data extraction and transformation processes to enhance efficiency and reliability.
Support the development and maintenance of data models for reporting and analytical purposes, responding to ad-hoc data requests from stakeholders.
What You Bring
Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s program in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, IT, Mathematics, or a similar technical field.
Demonstrated experience with SQL is mandatory, with prior experience in data platforms like Snowflake being a strong advantage.
Strong foundation in data transformation (ETL/ELT) and data modeling, along with database management skills.
Proficiency in Python and Power BI is a plus.
A keen analytical mindset, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently on technical tasks.
About The Team

You will be part of an international, highly engaged team of experienced finance professionals, mainly based in Brande but with satellites in the USA, Mexico, Brazil, and Germany. The team works closely with the supply chain business, combining finance and supply chain data to support collaboration. You will work alongside team members who have many years of experience with the data and will be mentored by a controller with experience in SQL and beginner/discovery experience with Snowflake.
Who is Siemens Gamesa?

Siemens Gamesa is part of Siemens Energy, a global leader in energy technology with a rich legacy of innovation spanning over 150 years. Together, we are committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. As a leading player in the wind industry and manufacturer of wind turbines, we are passionate about driving the energy transition and providing innovative solutions that meet the growing energy demand of the global community. At Siemens Gamesa, we are constantly seeking talented individuals to join our team and support our focus on energy transformation.
Our Commitment to Diversity

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by multiple nationalities. We celebrate character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.
Rewards/Benefits
Become a part of our mission for sustainability: Clean energy for generations to come
A global team of diverse colleagues who share passion for renewable energy
Trust and empowerment to make your own ideas reality
Personal and professional development to grow internally within our organization
Employer-funded pension

Siemens Gamesa is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a work environment that is free from discrimination and where employees are treated with dignity and respect. Employment at Siemens Gamesa is based solely on an individual's merit and qualifications, which are directly related to job competence. Siemens Gamesa does not discriminate against any employee or job applicant on the basis of race, ethnicity, nationality, ancestry, genetic information, citizenship, religion, age, gender, gender identity/expression, sexual orientation, pregnancy, marital status, disability or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, rules or regulations. We adhere to these principles in all aspects of employment, including recruiting, hiring, training, compensation, promotion and benefits.
Application
Don’t hesitate – apply in English via Apply button, id nr 296960 no later than 2026-07-05.
Ongoing selection is applied, the role might be filled before last application date.
