4431657891,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-scientist-at-trustpilot-4431657891?position=36&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=5ybAP1VGcADm%2BqdEx%2FIpFg%3D%3D,Data Scientist,Trustpilot,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

A core value proposition of Trustpilot is Trust. We work hard to ensure that our platform has genuine content from real people with real experiences. That means eliminating fake reviews and acting on signs of suspicious behavior to protect our platform from fraudulent activity. Our Trust Applied AI team’s mission is to use advanced modeling to safeguard the integrity of online reviews, and we have several cross-functional teams devoted to Trust where Applied AI is integral to achieving our goals.

We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our Trust team. You and your team will work to develop, deploy, and maintain innovative models at scale, alongside a cross-functional team of software developers, product managers, ML engineers, and designers. You will have the opportunity to collaborate widely across the business with our Technology and Product teams. You will also work closely with stakeholders in Legal and Platform Integrity.

To succeed in this role, you should have proven experience in developing and deploying ML models, ideally using graph data. You need a strong technical foundation and hands-on experience in all stages of data preparation, exploration, and modeling. An adaptable mindset and understanding of the interface between Applied AI and engineering are essential as you will be working in cross-disciplinary teams. Experience with deploying solutions into production is expected, as you will be responsible for implementing end-to-end solutions—from data preprocessing to serving predictions.
What you will do:
Be hands-on in delivering the Applied AI / Data Science component of key strategic projects in Trust, from building and fine-tuning language models to sequence modeling, graph-based models, etc.
Develop, maintain, and deploy production-ready ML models, with tooling support from ML engineers
Engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and contribute to the Trust roadmap
Seize the chance to meaningfully influence our business through the latest advancements in Applied AI and keep Trustpilot on the edge of innovation when it comes to fake review detection

The opportunity to work with market-standard data engineering and deployment technologies including Google Agent Platform tools and Google BigQuery, and with leading Data Science tools and emerging technologies for model building and deployment
Opportunities to develop your career in a friendly, diverse, international team and workplace
Who you are:

Experience with analytical and quantitative problem-solving using advanced statistical techniques and machine learning methods. Particularly relevant experience: high-precision classification focus, graph neural networks, graph algorithms used within machine learning, fine tuning of large language models, working with large label sets with varying label quality, and adversarial machine learning.
Experience in building and deploying production-ready ML models at scale, and solid data engineering skills, ideally in GCP
Ability in Python and SQL for data manipulation, modeling, and scripting
Experience in working with large datasets, ideally from tech platforms, e-commerce, or SaaS-type businesses. Knowledge of behavioral data, detecting misbehavior and/or fraud at scale is a big plus
Knowledge of data pipelining and prior experience with cloud-based ML model deployments
Great communication skills - both with technical colleagues and with business stakeholders
2-3 years or more of technical experience in a Data Science role, preferably in the technology sector or in a technical consultancy
What’s in it for you:
A range of flexible working options to dedicate time to what matters to you
Competitive compensation package + bonus
25 days holiday per year, with an extra 5 days holiday allocated after your 1 year anniversary (prorated)
Two (paid) volunteering days a year to spend your time giving back to the causes that matter to you and your community
Rich learning and development opportunities are supported through the Trustpilot Academy and Blinkist
Comprehensive health package, pension, and full access to Headspace, a popular mindfulness app to promote positive mental health
Paid parental leave

Central office with  a laid-back vibe and constant buzz of different languages being spoken everywhere you go It’s complete with a coffee bar, canteen, and table tennis and has a wide variety of refreshments available - you can opt-in for breakfast and lunch at an affordable price
Regular opportunities to connect and get to know your fellow Trusties, including company-wide celebrations and events, ERG activities, and team socials
Opportunity to join the Trustpilot Social Club for a small monthly fee and attend regular social and leisure activities like go-karting, cooking classes, and escape rooms
Employee discounts to restaurants and shops
Still not sure?

We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences along with how you will contribute to our working culture. Even if you don’t feel you meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!
About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas.

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.
Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

Position:
Quantitative development & optimisation:

Build and maintain flexibility trading algorithms for renewable, hybrid and BESS assets, maximising revenue across energy, ancillary services, and capacity markets in Denmark (first, then Germany, then expanding to wider Europe.
Design, back-test, implement in code and continuously improve trading strategies for intraday, imbalance, and frequency response markets.
Build revenue stacking models that quantify and optimise the interplay between co-located revenue streams, including hybrid wind/solar-plus-storage configurations.

Be responsible and accountable for the realized performance of our flexibility portfolio
There is a relocation package, but you must have experience in the above.

4366681691,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/register-in-our-candidate-data-base-at-veo-technologies-4366681691?position=39&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=VQBpa77f3E1XNh5lWwLgDQ%3D%3D,Register in our candidate data base,Veo Technologies,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Veo is a global leader in AI-based sports camera technology. Our innovative, fully automatic camera solution enables sports teams to record matches and training sessions without a camera operator. We’re democratizing the world of sports by granting video analysis for teams on all levels—a privilege that used to be only for the few. More than 50,000 clubs in 90+ countries record their games every week.
Growing as fast as we do in Veo means that every day is different, exciting, and challenging, both on the front line and in the back office.
But that’s not the most remarkable thing about us.
The coolest thing is our people. We’ve attracted some of the brightest minds in the industry. They are the reason we can create a great product and do it while enjoying ourselves.
Does your dream job at Veo not appear on the list, then sign up in our candidate data base and we will get in contact with you if the right opportunity arises for you.

Our Copenhagen office mirrors our innovative technology – with an indoor ball court, rooftop terrace, and well-equipped gym. We have created a facility that supports connecting disciplines across the business, facilitates creative thinking, and gives the space to engage with colleagues, our global partners, and the entire Veo community.
Values we hold at Veo:
Dare - We take initiative, stay curious, and challenge the status quo in our work.
Own it - We drive impact by identifying opportunities, taking responsibility, and confidently executing.
Level up - We embrace growth, continuously learning and shaping our skills.
Play as a team - We collaborate across a diverse organization, leveraging shared insights for stronger
outcomes.
Customer Centric - We deliver value by having close partnerships and a deep understanding of our customers (internally and externally)

We value diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, even if your experience doesn’t completely match every qualification listed. What matters most is your creativity, potential, and ability to make meaningful contributions.

4425938182,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-scientist-genai-at-danske-bank-4425938182?position=42&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=LhDtYgNTycb%2FOSBMW8aQ%2Bg%3D%3D,Senior data scientist GenAI,Danske Bank,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Job Description
Senior data scientist GenAI

Are you passionate about cutting-edge AI and language models? Do you want to shape the future of how personal customers interact with Danske Bank? We are looking for a Senior Data Scientist with deep expertise in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Generative AI (GenAI) to join our Conversational AI unit within the Personal Customers AI Centre of Excellence.
Who Are We

The Personal Customers AI Centre of Excellence is a diverse team of data scientists, ML engineers and MLOps engineers. Our Conversational AI unit builds adviser and customer-facing GenAI-powered solutions and stays at the forefront of technology and research to create real value for customers.
What You Will Do

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will lead the development of language-driven solutions for our Conversational AI unit, enhancing the AI Assistant with actionable flows, building RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems connected to internal data and co-designing multi-agent sets that empower customers with self-service capabilities.
Your key responsibilities will include:

Designing, developing and optimising advanced NLP/GenAI solutions using LLMs, including auxiliary tools such as NER, offensive speech detection, information masking and summarisation on multi-turn conversational input.
Expanding and implementing RAG pipelines that integrate vector databases and embedding models for structured and unstructured data and making them work effectively in a conversational setting.

Collaborating with ML engineers, data engineers and product teams to integrate and deploy models into production environments using Python, core ML libraries, Docker, Kubernetes and cloud platforms (AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP, Databricks).

Driving experimentation, A/B testing and model evaluation strategies using metrics such as BLEU, ROUGE, F1, exact match, faithfulness and hallucination detection, and continuously improving latency, accuracy and LLM judges.

Staying current with the latest research and industry trends in LLMs, transformers and prompt engineering, identifying areas for improvement in existing solutions, and mentoring junior team members while contributing to best practices in model development and MLOps.
Your Skills And Experience
5+ years of experience in data science or machine learning with a strong focus on NLP, and solid knowledge of transformers, tokenisation, embeddings and attention mechanisms.
Hands-on experience with GenAI frameworks and tooling such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI APIs and Hugging Face, plus strong Python skills and familiarity with PyTorch, TensorFlow and scikit-learn.

Proven experience building and deploying RAG architectures and vector-based retrieval (FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, OpenSearch) in production, working with data and services in AWS and Databricks, and deploying models using Docker, Kubernetes and cloud platforms.
Strong understanding of evaluation metrics for NLP tasks and excellent problem-solving and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex topics to different stakeholders.

It is a plus if you have experience with prompt engineering and instruction-tuned models, fine-tuning domain-specific LLMs, responsible AI principles and model risk management, multi-agent orchestration frameworks (for example LangGraph or AWS Agent Core), and Databricks for processing and model serving. Experience from domains such as finance, healthcare or customer service chatbots is also beneficial.
Why join us?

You will work on cutting-edge GenAI projects with real-world impact for millions of customers in a department with a solid track record of scaled solutions. You will join a collaborative, cross-functional team with room for autonomy and a flexible working environment, and you will work in our new headquarters with great facilities.
Interested?
Apply now or reach out to Ledian Selimaj – LEDS@danskebank.dk for more information. First interviews will start in June, so do not hold back.

We are looking for a Senior Data Scientist with deep expertise in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Generative AI (GenAI) to join our Conversational AI unit within the Personal Customers AI Centre of Excellence.

Who Are We

The Personal Customers AI Centre of Excellence is a diverse team of data scientists, ML engineers and MLOps engineers. Our Conversational AI unit builds adviser and customer-facing GenAI-powered solutions and stays at the forefront of technology and research to create real value for customers.

What You Will Do

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will lead the development of language-driven solutions for our Conversational AI unit, enhancing the AI Assistant with actionable flows, building RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems connected to internal data and co-designing multi-agent sets that empower customers with self-service capabilities.

Your key responsibilities will include:

  • Designing, developing and optimising advanced NLP/GenAI solutions using LLMs, including auxiliary tools such as NER, offensive speech detection, information masking and summarisation on multi-turn conversational input.
  • Expanding and implementing RAG pipelines that integrate vector databases and embedding models for structured and unstructured data and making them work effectively in a conversational setting.
  • Collaborating with ML engineers, data engineers and product teams to integrate and deploy models into production environments using Python, core ML libraries, Docker, Kubernetes and cloud platforms (AWS Bedrock, Azure, GCP, Databricks).
  • Driving experimentation, A/B testing and model evaluation strategies using metrics such as BLEU, ROUGE, F1, exact match, faithfulness and hallucination detection, and continuously improving latency, accuracy and LLM judges.
  • Staying current with the latest research and industry trends in LLMs, transformers and prompt engineering, identifying areas for improvement in existing solutions, and mentoring junior team members while contributing to best practices in model development and MLOps.

Your Skills And Experience

  • 5+ years of experience in data science or machine learning with a strong focus on NLP, and solid knowledge of transformers, tokenisation, embeddings and attention mechanisms.
  • Hands-on experience with GenAI frameworks and tooling such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI APIs and Hugging Face, plus strong Python skills and familiarity with PyTorch, TensorFlow and scikit-learn.
  • Proven experience building and deploying RAG architectures and vector-based retrieval (FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, OpenSearch) in production, working with data and services in AWS and Databricks, and deploying models using Docker, Kubernetes and cloud platforms.
  • Strong understanding of evaluation metrics for NLP tasks and excellent problem-solving and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex topics to different stakeholders.

It is a plus if you have experience with prompt engineering and instruction-tuned models, fine-tuning domain-specific LLMs, responsible AI principles and model risk management, multi-agent orchestration frameworks (for example LangGraph or AWS Agent Core), and Databricks for processing and model serving. Experience from domains such as finance, healthcare or customer service chatbots is also beneficial.

Why join us?

You will work on cutting-edge GenAI projects with real-world impact for millions of customers in a department with a solid track record of scaled solutions. You will join a collaborative, cross-functional team with room for autonomy and a flexible working environment, and you will work in our new headquarters with great facilities.

Interested?

Apply now or reach out to Ledian Selimaj – LEDS@danskebank.dk for more information. First interviews will start in June, so do not hold back.

4431690546,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/staff-analytics-engineer-1-year-fixed-term-contract-at-usercentrics-4431690546?position=44&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=C7hUUbkiWw2WC9ZpP2BRFg%3D%3D,Staff Analytics Engineer (1 Year Fixed-Term Contract),Usercentrics,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","SHAPE THE FUTURE OF PRIVACY WITH USERCENTRICS
Hybrid position - 2/3 days in the office
1 year fixed-term contract

Usercentrics is a global leader in the field of consent management platforms (CMP). We follow our company vision to build a world where user privacy enables a thriving digital ecosystem. Our mission is to provide privacy solutions that empower organizations to embrace data privacy while building trust with their users through freedom of choice. With our product, businesses can collect, manage and document user consents on websites and apps, and achieve full compliance in accordance with global privacy regulations while facilitating high consent rates and building trust with their customers.

We're looking for an experienced Staff Analytics Engineer to own the design and delivery of scalable data models and data products across our data warehouse, semantic layer, and reporting layer. Reporting to the Director of Analytics, you will enable critical decision making across the business, from marketing and product to commercial operations.

This is a senior individual contributor role with technical leadership responsibilities. You will not manage a team initially, but you will set standards and mentor others. This role may evolve into a people management position as the team scales.
Your Tasks
Drive the development and expansion of our Data Warehouse and BI solutions, with a focus on simplicity, scalability, and reliability, ensuring they grow sustainably
Own and evolve the Analytics Engineering strategy, including architectural decisions, modeling standards, and the long term roadmap.
Collaborate with Data Analysts, Data Scientists, and backend engineers to deliver trusted, well modeled, analysis-ready datasets.
Apply software engineering best practices across the analytics codebase, including version control, code reviews, automated testing, lineage, and continuous integration.
Evaluate, select, and deploy tools and frameworks to strengthen data quality, governance, and reliability.
Partner closely with business stakeholders to design and scale the semantic layer to support diverse and evolving business needs, enabling and accelerating self service analytics.
Act as a go to person for Data Analysts and Data Scientists, providing guidance to improve pipelines and remove inefficiencies, redundancies, and technical bottlenecks.
Mentor and support other team members as the team scales, contributing to capability building, onboarding, and continuous knowledge sharing.
Bring strategic thinking and innovative ideas to help shape a growing team and deliver meaningful business impact at scale.
Requirements
5+ years of relevant experience in a comparable role, designing and scaling complex data models for analytics and data science.
A unique combination of technical expertise, business acumen, and communication skills, enabling you to persuade and influence data product stakeholders.
Proficiency with dimensional modeling (star schema) and medallion architecture, with the ability to design and develop curated data marts.
Strong coding skills in SQL and proficiency in DBT, orchestration tools, and engineering best practices.
Experience with cloud data warehouses (BigQuery preferred) and semantic layers such as MetricFlow, LookML, Omni, or Cube
Curiosity, strong problem solving skills, and a proactive, creative mindset.
Excellent organizational and communication skills, both verbal and written.

Don't meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and people of colour are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Usercentrics we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.
Benefits
Why join Usercentrics?
Joining Usercentrics means becoming part of a fast-growing, diverse and international team of tech enthusiasts and entrepreneurially-minded who build our success story together
Company culture is important to us - we strive to continuously develop a positive, vibrant and inspiring environment that enables everyone to thrive both personally and professionally
Get involved! We have plenty of initiatives and love to see people from all department enthusiastically participating and shaping our future together in different cross-department projects
Your work-life balance is important to us too - we offer flexible working hours, hybrid working and the possibility of workcations (in accordance with our company policy)
We always remember to have fun along the way, both in our day-to-day work and at our regular team events on site in our offices in Munich, Copenhagen, Odense, Lisbon and Prague or online

4429590582,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-spirii-4429590582?position=45&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=5cp73F6Cx%2F3vHcYN8UASSQ%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Spirii,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.
Your Responsibilities
Pipeline & Ingestion
Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.
Data Platform & Infrastructure
Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.
Data Quality & Governance
Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.
Collaboration & Stakeholders
Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.
About You
You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.
Qualifications
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred
column2: BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).

column1: Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.
Nice to Have
Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.
Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.
Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.
The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.
About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company

Key Responsibilities
You will build and lead a high-performing global Business & Data Analysis chapter, ensuring alignment between business needs, data capabilities, and enterprise standards.

Own and drive standards, methods, and best practices for business and data analysis
Lead and develop analysts, strengthening quality, consistency, and impact across teams
Ensure high-quality requirements, mapping, and documentation across domains through formed frameworks
Drive end-to-end clarity from strategy through to delivery execution
Support complex analysis and stakeholder alignment across business and IT, while strengthening collaboration in order to unblock delivery when needed
Ensure alignment and reuse of capabilities across platforms, reducing duplication. Contribute hands-on when required to unblock teams and move delivery forward
Validate and challenge requirements to support domain and enterprise data strategies
Provide leadership and guidance on business transformation and process optimization efforts
Build and grow a strong global analyst community through mentoring, capability development, and knowledge sharing
You will also collaborate closely with architects, SMEs, and data analysts to ensure cohesive, end-to-end solutions, and provide guidance on business transformation and process optimisation.
To succeed in this role, you bring:
7+ years of experience in business analysis and/or data/process mapping, ideally within logistics, supply chain, or enterprise data platforms
Demonstrated experience leading a chapter, guild, or community of practice — including people leadership, setting standards, and ensuring learnings across teams

A structured and pragmatic approach to business and data analysis, including requirement management, process and data mapping, and documentation aligned with enterprise standards and target operating models
Proven ability to define and apply standards, methods, templates, and best practices to ensure consistent and high-quality analysis deliverables
Strong capabilities in requirement management, including creating clarity, ensuring quality, and maintaining traceability from business need through to delivery
Hands-on experience with process mapping and data mapping (e.g. source-to-target flows and data lineage)
Experience enabling scalable and reusable solutions through structured analysis, documentation, and governance
Ability to bridge business and technical stakeholders, facilitate workshops, and translate complexity into clear, actionable deliverables
Experience working in product-oriented environments applying Agile frameworks (e.g. Scrum, Kanban, SAFe)
Proven stakeholder management and communication skills in complex global organizations

Experience driving cross-domain alignment, dependency management, enterprise-wide standardization, and collaboration across architecture, engineering, QA, and governance functions in enterprise delivery environments
Experience working with distributed teams across multiple time zones
Experience working with Data Products within an Event Driven Data Mesh is a benefit
Want to know more and apply?
We accept applications until 5 July 2026, but we screen candidates on an ongoing basis, so we recommend applying early. No cover letter needed

4432953977,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/analyst-student-assistant-energy-management-business-analytics-at-copenhagen-infrastructure-partners-4432953977?position=50&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=Bfcye1ZvjThDvElcUedMPA%3D%3D,"Analyst (Student Assistant), Energy Management (Business Analytics)",Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Do you want to join a global fund manager in energy infrastructure investments? If so, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP) is currently looking for an Analyst to join our Energy Management team based in Copenhagen.
Team

As an Analyst you will work in our Hyperscaler CoE & Business Development team within Energy Management in CIP. This team brings deep knowledge across both mature and emerging renewable energy markets and technologies. The team plays an important role in supporting CIP throughout the full lifecycle of renewable energy investments, including contributing insights and analytics during key transaction processes and supporting the commercial offtake and risk exposure of the Funds and their projects. The team’s work helps ensure that energy market dynamics are well reflected in investment decisions, supporting value creation and effective risk management across the portfolio.
Role

This is an opportunity to work at the intersection of renewable energy markets, analytics, and technology in a market-facing team with broad exposure across the CIP platform. You will work on real business problems with direct relevance to how CIP identifies opportunities, supports commercial decision-making, and builds scalable tools and processes across the platform. Rather than supporting from the sidelines, you will take real ownership of workstreams and contribute to projects that are used by stakeholders across the business.

For the right candidate, this role offers a steep learning curve, meaningful responsibility, and close collaboration with experienced colleagues and senior stakeholders, in one of the most important sectors at the heart of the energy transition.
The position is part-time and based in our headquarters in NordØ. The workload will vary depending on ongoing projects, with flexibility to structure your time according to your schedule.
Responsibilities will consist of but are not limited to:
Support commercial decision-making through building and maintaining Power BI dashboards and reporting tools,
Structure and manage datasets for market intelligence and pipeline tracking,
Support development of matching logic between projects and potential offtake opportunities,
Assist in preparation of materials for internal decision-making and stakeholder dialogues,
Contribute to system design discussions and documentation,
Manage smaller workstreams within larger initiatives.
Role development opportunities will include but are not limited to:
End-to-end delivery responsibility for smaller internal business development and analytics projects,
Direct collaboration with senior leaders and stakeholders across the CIP platform,
Opportunities to present data-, AI- and technology-focused business development and improvement ideas to senior leaders across CIP Platform Solutions,
Ownership of recurring market intelligence, pipeline tracking, and decision-support reporting across the platform.
You

We are looking for Copenhagen-based students finishing their bachelor’s or in the early stage of their Master’s. Relevant educational focuses include business analytics, AI, data science, software/technology, or similar quantitative fields from DTU, ITU, KU, CBS, or equivalent institutions.

You should have a solid technical foundation, be comfortable working directly with data and digital tools, and be motivated by building structured and automated solutions to real business problems. We value this alongside commercial curiosity, structured thinking, and an interest in renewable energy.
We look for the following in a candidate:
Structured, detail-oriented, and able to work from problem statement to practical solution,
Good communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to engage both business and technical stakeholders,
Able to translate business needs into analytical, technical, and product-like solutions,
Comfortable managing their own work and taking ownership of building and improving tools,
Solid academic and/or practical experience within relevant data, analytics, or technology fields.
Strong candidates will also bring hands-on technology and data skills:
Experienced within data structuring and data modelling, with the ability to turn messy inputs into usable datasets and scalable logic,
Experience building solutions yourself in tools such as Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, Excel, SQL, Python, or similar technologies,
Ability and motivation to develop working prototypes, internal tools, automations, and decision-support products,
Solid understanding of how data flows across systems, including data capture, transformation, logic, reporting, and user-facing applications,
A solid understanding of the role and practical deployment opportunities of AI in tools, systems, and business applications,
Curiosity around system design, product thinking, and how technology can be used to solve real commercial problems in a scalable way.

Please note that we do not offer a remote working opportunity, thus you must be a Danish resident or an upcoming student enrolled in a Danish university to be eligible for this role. Students on exchange in Denmark are not considered eligible for this position.
The process

Please apply online as soon as possible as applications will be processed on an ongoing basis. For questions related to the role, please reach out to our Talent Acquisition team at recruitment@cipfs.com. We do not include CV's and Cover Letters sent by email.

As we continue to improve our candidate experience, diversity and avoid unconscious bias, please avoid having a photo in your CV. We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality for all applicants.

Please be aware that we will not accept speculative CV submissions for any of our roles from recruitment agencies, and any unsolicited candidate submissions will be exempt from any payment expectations and ownership.
Building value that matters

Founded in 2012, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners P/S (CIP) today is the world’s largest dedicated fund manager within greenfield renewable energy investments and a global leader in offshore wind. To date, CIP has developed and constructed more than +50 greenfield renewable projects around the world. CIP is developing a greenfield project portfolio of ~160GW total, and ~16 GW is in operation or under construction. CIP manages 13 funds and has to date raised approximately EUR ~35 billion for investments in energy and associated infrastructure from more than ~200 international institutional investors.

CIP has a multinational team of ~500 full-time employees with extensive experience and knowledge within the fields of renewable energy project development and regulated infrastructure. In addition, CIP has a broad range of competencies within corporate finance, mergers & acquisitions, engineering, construction, project development, and project management.

CIP has built a prominent investment team capable of originating proprietary investment opportunities and executing projects and investments based on the team’s industrial background, large industrial network, and extensive experience in structuring, executing, and managing investments within energy infrastructure. Headquartered in Copenhagen, CIP also operates from key locations across the globe, including offices in Hamburg, Munich, London, Utrecht, New York, Tokyo, Melbourne, Luxembourg, Kuala Lumpur, Seoul and Singapore. In addition, CIP has 17 local project offices globally.
For more information, visit www.cip.com.

4429322297,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-worker-ai-explorer-at-panzerglass%C2%AE-4429322297?position=52&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=O0t1SIcI5uwXzHer087v8g%3D%3D,"Student Worker, AI Explorer",PanzerGlass®,"Hinnerup, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","Would you like to work on real AI projects from day one? Are you a technically curious student with a passion for building things that actually work? Do you thrive when given the freedom to experiment and the responsibility to deliver? Then you might be one of our new AI Explorers.

PanzerGlass is globally recognised as one of the leading brands in screen protection. To support our next wave of digital transformation, we are building a new team, AI Explorers, based at our HQ in Hinnerup and reporting directly to the COO. As part of this team, you'll be hands-on from the start, exploring, building, testing, and deploying practical AI and automation tools that have an immediate impact on how we work.

You will work with different stakeholders across the global organisation and engage in AI projects defined by management. Your tasks will vary, as you will work with specific AI projects or tools on a rotation basis, typically for 2-3 months, depending on need, before taking on a new project.
Key responsibilities
You will take real ownership of AI and automation projects across the business. Your responsibilities will include:
Designing and developing AI-driven tools and automations
Building prototypes and Minimum Viable Products (MVPs) to test new ideas quickly
Integrating AI solutions into existing systems and workflows
Collaborating with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into working solutions
Continuously improving and maintaining implemented solutions
Exploring new technologies, Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), and frameworks relevant to AI
Your profile
You're currently studying towards a bachelor's or master's degree in a technical field. You don't just read about AI, you build with it.
To succeed in this role, you will need:
A genuine interest in AI, automation, and emerging technologies
The ability to work independently and turn ideas into working solutions
A problem-solving mindset and strong attention to detail
It's a plus if you have:
Experience with machine learning or AI frameworks
Familiarity with APIs and system integrations
Knowledge of cloud platforms or automation tools
Previous personal or academic projects involving AI
We offer
A company with best-in-class employee survey results, listed among the best workplaces in Denmark and Europe
An opportunity to work on real AI projects with immediate business impact
The freedom to experiment and build solutions from scratch
Impact on business operation - your work will be seen and felt
A supporting team that values learning and development opportunities
Professional and highly skilled colleagues who set the bar high
Application
We screen and interview candidates on an ongoing basis, and we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.
Due to GDPR regulations, we are unable to accept applications sent by email. Please submit your application on our Careers Page.
Start date: As soon as possible.

Would you like to work on real AI projects from day one? Are you a technically curious student with a passion for building things that actually work? Do you thrive when given the freedom to experiment and the responsibility to deliver? Then you might be one of our new AI Explorers.


PanzerGlass is globally recognised as one of the leading brands in screen protection. To support our next wave of digital transformation, we are building a new team, AI Explorers, based at our HQ in Hinnerup and reporting directly to the COO. As part of this team, you'll be hands-on from the start, exploring, building, testing, and deploying practical AI and automation tools that have an immediate impact on how we work.


You will work with different stakeholders across the global organisation and engage in AI projects defined by management. Your tasks will vary, as you will work with specific AI projects or tools on a rotation basis, typically for 2-3 months, depending on need, before taking on a new project.


Key responsibilities

You will take real ownership of AI and automation projects across the business. Your responsibilities will include:

  • Designing and developing AI-driven tools and automations
  • Building prototypes and Minimum Viable Products (MVPs) to test new ideas quickly
  • Integrating AI solutions into existing systems and workflows
  • Collaborating with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into working solutions
  • Continuously improving and maintaining implemented solutions
  • Exploring new technologies, Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), and frameworks relevant to AI


Your profile

You're currently studying towards a bachelor's or master's degree in a technical field. You don't just read about AI, you build with it.


To succeed in this role, you will need:


  • A genuine interest in AI, automation, and emerging technologies
  • The ability to work independently and turn ideas into working solutions
  • A problem-solving mindset and strong attention to detail


It's a plus if you have:


  • Experience with machine learning or AI frameworks
  • Familiarity with APIs and system integrations
  • Knowledge of cloud platforms or automation tools
  • Previous personal or academic projects involving AI


We offer

  • A company with best-in-class employee survey results, listed among the best workplaces in Denmark and Europe
  • An opportunity to work on real AI projects with immediate business impact
  • The freedom to experiment and build solutions from scratch
  • Impact on business operation - your work will be seen and felt
  • A supporting team that values learning and development opportunities
  • Professional and highly skilled colleagues who set the bar high


Application

We screen and interview candidates on an ongoing basis, and we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.


Due to GDPR regulations, we are unable to accept applications sent by email. Please submit your application on our Careers Page.


Start date: As soon as possible.

4432554387,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-research-assistant-part-time-at-yougov-4432554387?position=54&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=cdmqbQNJmqQYI0KE5bqhMA%3D%3D,Student Research Assistant (part-time),YouGov,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","YouGov is an international online research data and analytics technology group. Our innovative solutions help the world’s most recognised brands, media owners and agencies to plan, activate and track their marketing activities better. With operations in the UK, the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, India and Asia Pacific, we have one of the world's largest research networks. At the core of our platform is an ever-growing source of connected consumer data that has developed daily over our 20 years of operation. We call it living data. All of our products and services draw upon this detailed understanding of our 20+ million registered panel members to deliver accurate, actionable consumer insights. Job description You will be supporting fast-turnaround research projects on many different topics, which is a part of YouGov’s Client Service offering.

As Student Research Assistant, you will support Research Executives on various tasks. The role relies on excellent communication skills in Norwegian, Danish and English. You’ll be working closely with other YouGov teams as well as directly with clients to design and deliver research which is in line with the brief.
Your primary responsibility will be:
Proof-reading of client-based questionnaires.
Checking and adjustment of questionnaire scripting instructions.
Scripting of surveys in YouGov’s own self-service tool.
Checking of questionnaire test links before launch.
Data output checking and delivery for clients.
Supporting on custom report preparations.
These are the essentials you’ll need to get an interview:
Danish, Norwegian and English in writing/speaking.
Working proficiency in the basic MS package.
Strong attention to details and the ability to deliver results and meet deadlines.
Problem-solving, analytical and numerical skills.

This is a part-time, paid vacancy of maximum 20 hours per week for student candidates. It is hybrid working arrangement of 40% in office, 60% remote working. The salary is DKK150 per hour, with a pay review after 6 months.
Why join YouGov?
Join our global team to help us achieve our social mission: to make millions of people’s opinions heard for the benefit of our local, national, and international communities.

Understanding diversity of opinion requires diversity of background. Although our global panel of millions of people worldwide powers our research, our biggest asset is our people. If our research is to be truly representative of what the world thinks, we need people from all walks of life to be part of the team to bring their perspective to the work we do.
Life at YouGov

We are driven by a set of shared values. We are fast, fearless, and innovative. We work diligently to get it right. We are guided by accuracy, ethics, and proven methodologies. We respect and trust each other, bringing these values into everything that we do.

We strive to provide YouGovers with best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellbeing. We want our employees to have a sense of belonging and uniqueness in a supportive workplace, so they can bring their full selves to work.
Equal Opportunity Employer

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity or expression, and sexual orientation), parental status, national origin, marital status, age, disability, genetic information, HIV status, political affiliation, socioeconomic background, veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law or in line with our responsibilities as a fair and ethic employer. All employment decisions are made based on occupational qualifications, merit, and business need.
Data Privacy
To find out how we collect and use your personal data when you apply for a role at YouGov, please read our privacy notice at https://jobs.yougov.com/privacy

4433085755,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/ai-data-professionals-kommende-projekter-at-etteplan-4433085755?position=55&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=81JfPLdeds%2BA058QvqcjXQ%3D%3D,AI & Data Professionals - Kommende Projekter,Etteplan,"Herlev, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Freelance Opportunities

Vi samarbejder løbende med erfarne freelancekonsulenter på tværs af en bred vifte af ingeniør- og teknologiprojekter. Hvis du er interesseret i fremtidige freelanceopgaver hos Etteplan, vil vi meget gerne lære dig at kende. Send os dit CV, så kontakter vi dig, når vi ser en mulighed, der matcher din erfaring og dine kompetencer.
Om Etteplan
Etteplan er en international ingeniør- og teknologivirksomhed, som hjælper industrielle virksomheder med at designe, udvikle og forbedre produkter, systemer og digitale løsninger.
I Danmark er vi i gang med at udvide vores tilstedeværelse og opbygger et netværk af erfarne AI & Data Professionals, som er interesserede i at arbejde med os på kommende projekter.

Vores kunder arbejder med kunstig intelligens, data og digitalisering på mange forskellige måder – fra machine learning og data engineering til generativ AI og intelligente produkter på tværs af brancher som produktion, energi, life science og industriel teknologi.
Om rollen
Vi søger profiler, der ønsker at være med til at udvikle intelligente løsninger baseret på data og kunstig intelligens.
Afhængigt af projektet kan du arbejde med machine learning, generativ AI, data engineering, analytics eller udvikling af AI-løsninger, der understøtter digitale produkter og forretningsprocesser.
Du vil typisk samarbejde med software engineers, data specialister, domæneeksperter og forretningsinteressenter om at omsætte data og teknologi til løsninger, der skaber reel værdi.
Typiske teknologier og kompetenceområder
Programming & AI Development
Python, Java, C#, C++ eller tilsvarende programmeringssprog.
Machine Learning & Data Science
Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Science, statistiske modeller og predictive analytics.
Generative AI & LLMs
Large Language Models (LLMs), Generative AI, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), AI-agenter og lignende løsninger.
Cloud & MLOps
Azure, AWS, Google Cloud Platform, MLOps, CI/CD og cloud-baserede AI-platforme.
Data Engineering & Analytics
Data pipelines, ETL, dataplatforme, analytics og databehandling.
Du behøver ikke at have erfaring med alle teknologierne ovenfor. Vi arbejder med forskellige kunder og projekter, så den konkrete teknologistak afhænger af opgaven.
Hvem vi typisk leder efter
Vi er som udgangspunkt interesserede i profiler, der har:
erfaring med ét eller flere områder inden for AI, machine learning eller data
erfaring med at udvikle eller implementere intelligente løsninger
evnen til at omsætte komplekse problemstillinger til praktiske løsninger
interesse for nye teknologier og kontinuerlig læring
erfaring med tværfagligt samarbejde mellem teknik og forretning
Derfor søger vi profilen
Vi arbejder løbende på nye projekter i Danmark og vil gerne være i dialog med erfarne AI & Data Professionals.
Selvom der ikke nødvendigvis er et konkret projekt lige nu, tager vi ofte fat i kandidater fra vores netværk, når nye muligheder opstår.

Vi er altid interesserede i at komme i dialog med dygtige specialister. Selvom vi ikke nødvendigvis har et projekt, der matcher din profil i dag, vil vi meget gerne lære dig at kende og kontakte dig, når den rette mulighed opstår.
Hvad sker der, når du søger
Vi gennemgår ansøgninger løbende.
Hvis din profil matcher et kommende projekt, kontakter vi dig for en uforpligtende dialog om mulighederne.
Din profil kan også blive en del af vores konsulentnetværk til fremtidige projekter.

4423024072,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-ai-applications-at-bestseller-4423024072?position=56&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=vt%2F4ibERpTiY8KsDN12%2BdQ%3D%3D,Data Engineer - AI Applications,BESTSELLER,"Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","Join our innovative team at BESTSELLER as we leverage cutting-edge AI and data-driven solutions to transform the retail and e-commerce experience. We are looking for a Data Engineer who is passionate about building and operating the data products and platforms that power data-driven decisions, and eager to grow their craft in a fast-moving environment.
This role places you at the heart of our Appretio platform, our commitment to revolutionizing merchandising through data-driven solutions.
YOUR TEAM
You will become part of Merchandising Solutions, a department of more than 20 data, AI and technology specialists working to transform how merchandising decisions are made across BESTSELLER.

You will join a product team of ten highly skilled colleagues, including data scientists, engineers and application developers. Together, we build solutions that help our business make better decisions through data and AI. We work closely, share knowledge openly and value curiosity, ownership and continuous learning.
YOUR NEW ROLE

As a Data Engineer, you will help build and evolve the robust, scalable data pipelines and cloud-based infrastructure that power Appretio. Working closely with merchandisers, data scientists, engineers, and application developers, you will contribute to a data platform that is efficient, reliable, and ready to support cutting-edge AI initiatives. You will learn and apply data engineering best practices, take ownership of well-defined areas, and gradually grow into more senior responsibilities over time.
You will be supported through hands-on mentorship from experienced engineers, clear ownership of key areas, and ample opportunities to develop your skills and expertise.
Your Benefits
Joining BESTSELLER means becoming part of a collaborative and ambitious environment where technology creates tangible business impact.
You can expect:
The opportunity to build solutions used across multiple international fashion brands
Close collaboration with experienced engineers and data specialists
A culture focused on learning, knowledge sharing and continuous improvement
Professional development opportunities, including training, certification and new responsibilities as you grow
The freedom and responsibility to influence how we build our data platforms and products
YOUR PROFILE

You are proactive, structured and motivated by building reliable data solutions. You enjoy solving technical challenges, learning from others and continuously improving your craft. At the same time, you understand the value of collaboration and can communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
We imagine that you:
Hold a degree in Computer Science, Engineering or a related field, or bring equivalent practical experience
Have hands-on experience within data engineering and exposure to production data platforms
Have working knowledge of cloud environments such as AWS or Azure, data integration tools and modern data warehouse or lakehouse architectures
Possess solid SQL and Python skills and experience building data pipelines
Have experience with Git and CI/CD practices
Are interested in streaming and real-time data technologies such as Kafka or Spark Streaming
Are eager to learn, take responsibility and continue developing your technical expertise
It is considered an advantage if you also have:
An understanding of the trade-offs between transactional, analytical and distributed databases
Experience with multi-tenant database environments
Experience or interest in FastAPI, ORMs and related backend technologies
Experience optimising databases and pipelines for analytics-driven applications
An interest in data contracts, data products and data governance
WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

If you have any questions about the position, please feel free to contact Director, Data & Tech Accelerator, Emil Hartvig Diekema, at +45 29 62 82 03. Please upload your CV as soon as possible and no later than 28 June 2026. We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting once we have found the right match.
Location: Your primary workplace will be at our office in Aarhus.

At BESTSELLER, we are more than 15,000 people working for over 20 fashion brands such as JACK & JONES, SELECTED, VERO MODA, ONLY, PIECES, VILA, OBJECT, MAMALICIOUS, NAME IT, NOISY MAY and Y.A.S. At BESTSELLER E-Commerce, we are an international team of 170 people from more than 40 nationalities with the purpose to drive all e-commerce functions at BESTSELLER. With our office in the heart of Amsterdam as well as locations in Poland and Denmark, we are supporting all fashion brands of the BESTSELLER family and sell online in 15 countries around the world. We work in an environment where fashion meets technology. Challenging ourselves to offer the best experience to our customers and stakeholders and to work with the latest technologies is what drives us. As a team, we value autonomy and flexibility. We thrive in a flat organisation, where everyone has the chance to grow and learn. For more information, please go to www.bestseller.com.

4424191816,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/analytics-engineer-at-vestas-4424191816?position=58&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=HnqYFvGjyqaA0HOB3rQi9A%3D%3D,Analytics Engineer,Vestas,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Join Vestas and help turn data into powerful business insights. As part of our Reporting & Analytics team, you will build scalable data models and dashboards using modern cloud platforms, driving smarter decisions across a global organization.
Finance > Group Financial Performance > Reporting & Analytics CoE

The Reporting & Analytics (R&A) Centre of Excellence is a global function in Finance responsible for enabling data-driven performance management and decision-making across Vestas. The CoE brings together business-facing analytics, governance and definitions, analytics engineering, and operations into a single, integrated operating model. Its purpose is to create a scalable, standardised, and high-quality analytics environment that delivers “one version of the truth” across Vestas. The R&A CoE works closely with Finance, Commercial, Operations, and Digital to translate business needs into analytics products, while ensuring consistency in data definitions, efficient delivery, and modern engineering practices. By combining subject matter knowledge, technical capability, and operational excellence, the CoE plays a critical role in improving performance transparency, accelerating decision-making, and reducing manual reporting across the enterprise.
Responsibilities

In this role, you will contribute to the development of analytical solutions, including presentation layers with data visualizations, semantic layers, and core analytical data models that support business decision-making. As part of the Analytics Engineering pillar, you will play a key role in building and delivering scalable analytics products.
Design and develop analytical solutions, including dashboards, semantic layers, and data models.

Collaborate closely with Product Owners and Business Analysts to translate business requirements into effective analytics solutions. Work cross-functionally with domain experts, governance teams, and other stakeholders to ensure alignment and consistency.
Contribute to the design and implementation of analytics data architecture.
Develop and enhance the core layers of analytical solutions (data models, semantic and presentation layers).
Maintain, optimize, and continuously improve existing data models and analytics solutions.
Support the rollout of analytics solutions, including validation, testing, documentation, and training.
Drive performance optimization and efficient maintenance of analytics products.
Qualifications
Bachelor’s in Computer Science or a related field, with a preference for specialisation in data analytics or data modelling.
3–5+ years of relevant experience in designing and building analytical solutions
Interest in understanding business processes and domain concepts. Demonstrated ability to translate business requirements into effective analytical solutions.
Hands-on experience with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Microsoft Fabric)
Expertise in data modelling and proficiency in SQL
Experience with data visualization tools, particularly Power BI
Exposure to predictive modelling is considered an advantage
Competencies
Ability to approach problems logically, interpret data effectively, and derive meaningful insights
Proactive in identifying challenges and delivering practical, data-driven solutions
Eagerness to learn, adapt, and explore new tools, technologies, and business areas
High level of accuracy and precision in data handling and solution development
Team player with the ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams
Clear and structured communication, with the ability to explain complex concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Openness to feedback and a drive to continuously enhance skills, processes, and outcomes
What We Offer

You will gain exposure to a global working environment, collaborating with diverse teams across regions, while working on large-scale, cloud-based analytical platforms. The role offers good opportunities for continuous learning and professional development, supported by access to modern tools and technologies within a forward-looking analytics ecosystem.
Additional Information
Primary work location is Copenhagen, Denmark
We do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Please be advised to apply on or before 4th July 2026.
Our commitment to a fair hiring.

At Vestas, we evaluate all candidates solely based on their professional experience, education, and relevant skills. To support a fair recruitment process, we kindly ask that you remove any photos, dates of birth or graduation, gender pronouns, marital status, or other personal information not relevant to the role before submitting your CV/resume. Your CV/resume should focus on your professional and educational background, along with the necessary contact details (email and phone number). We train our hiring teams in inclusive evaluation and regularly review process outcomes to ensure fairness.
DEIB Statement

At Vestas, we recognize the value of diversity, equity, and inclusion in driving innovation and success. We strongly encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply, particularly those who may hesitate due to their identity or feel they do not meet every criterion. As our CEO states, ""Expertise and talent come in many forms, and a diverse workforce enhances our ability to think differently and solve the complex challenges of our industry"". Your unique perspective is what will help us powering the solution for a sustainable, green energy future.
BEWARE – RECRUITMENT FRAUD
It has come to our attention that there are a number of fraudulent emails from people pretending to work for Vestas. Read more via this link, https://www.vestas.com/en/careers/our-recruitment-process
About Vestas
Vestas is the energy industry’s global partner on sustainable energy solutions. We are specialised in designing, manufacturing, installing, and servicing wind turbines, both onshore and offshore.

Across the globe, we have installed more wind power than anyone else. We consider ourselves pioneers within the industry, as we continuously aim to design new solutions and technologies to create a more sustainable future for all of us. With more than 185 GW of wind power installed worldwide and 40+ years of experience in wind energy, we have an unmatched track record demonstrating our expertise within the field.
With 30,000 employees globally, we are a diverse team united by a common goal: to power the solution – today, tomorrow, and far into the future.

Vestas promotes a diverse workforce which embraces all social identities and is free of any discrimination. We commit to create and sustain an environment that acknowledges and harvests different experiences, skills, and perspectives. We also aim to give everyone equal access to opportunity.

To learn more about our company and life at Vestas, we invite you to visit our website at www.vestas.com and follow us on our social media channels. We also encourage you to join our Talent Universe to receive notifications on new and relevant postings.

4429543117,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-lind-capital-4429543117?position=60&pageNum=0&refId=UbdSZWjZ%2F3fZumGCn0qyWQ%3D%3D&trackingId=wtqI4tKQiGpxZpLqCgpCpw%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Lind Capital,"Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","DATA ENGINEER
Build the data and AI foundation behind real trading and risk decisions, on a genuinely modern data stack.
Who we are

Lind Capital is a proprietary trading company focused on the financial markets. We combine market expertise, cutting-edge technology, data-driven decisions, and quantitative models to execute the best possible trades.

We bring together talented people from diverse backgrounds in an informal work environment focused on creating results together. We empower our team to make decisions and prefer direct collaboration over bureaucracy.
Our diverse team of 25+ are based in Aarhus, Denmark.
The role
Data and AI are central to Lind Capital’s success and key to our future growth, and we are looking to add to our data team that currently consists of six Data and ML Engineers.

We run a modern data stack centered on Python (incl. Polars), Airflow, Parquet and Apache Iceberg, with Athena, Postgres, Kafka, and more in the mix. In this role, you’ll turn structured and unstructured data into trusted, well-modeled datasets that power production applications, analytics, and reporting — while helping us expand reliability, performance, and capabilities across our Data Lakehouse.

You won't just feed data to AI applications — you'll help build them. You'll work in a team that actively experiments with AI across both agentic engineering and agentic workflows — from AI-assisted development to the internal AI tooling we develop ourselves.
Key responsibilities
Collaborate closely with trading and risk professionals to define data needs and translate them into durable datasets and interfaces.
Design, build, and operate batch and streaming pipelines in our cloud-based Data Lakehouse.
Use AI-assisted development tools fluently and help build the data foundations and internal tooling that let AI and ML systems work reliably and safely against our data.
Implement data quality checks and improve pipeline observability (monitoring, alerting, and fast debugging when things break).
What we offer
A genuinely modern data stack where the data products and AI tools you build visibly drive live investment and risk decisions — real impact, at a firm small enough that it shows.
Close mentorship from six experienced Data and ML Engineers, with the support and code reviews to help you level up quickly.
A culture grounded in quantitative thinking, curiosity, and experimentation.
A dynamic environment that balances a disciplined approach to data pipelines with the freedom to innovate.
An inclusive, caring, and psychologically safe workplace where diverse perspectives, ideas, and approaches lead to high performance.
What we are looking for
Some hands-on experience with Data or Software Engineering — through work, internships, or your own projects. We care more about how you think than years on a CV.
Strong Python engineering skills (e.g. clean code, tests, packaging).
Solid SQL, and a working understanding of data stores for both transactional and analytical workloads.
Comfort with Git and good engineering habits — testing, CI/CD, and code review.
Genuine interest in working with AI tools day-to-day — both using them to ship faster and thinking critically about where they help and where they don’t.
A strong communicator and team player who thrives in a cross-functional team.
We value potential over perfect match, and we encourage you to apply even if you don't meet all the above. We consider candidates across experience levels.
Location
We support flexible working arrangements
column2: however, success in this role depends on close collaboration, which works best when colleagues are physically together. You should therefore expect to work most of the time from our Aarhus office. Our working language is English, and we welcome applications from non-Danish speakers.

Start date: As soon as possible.
To apply, please submit your CV, grade transcript, cover letter, and other relevant documents via the link below.
Applications will be processed on a rolling basis.

  • Implement data quality checks and improve pipeline observability (monitoring, alerting, and fast debugging when things break).


What we offer

  • A genuinely modern data stack where the data products and AI tools you build visibly drive live investment and risk decisions — real impact, at a firm small enough that it shows.
  • Close mentorship from six experienced Data and ML Engineers, with the support and code reviews to help you level up quickly.
  • A culture grounded in quantitative thinking, curiosity, and experimentation.
  • A dynamic environment that balances a disciplined approach to data pipelines with the freedom to innovate.
  • An inclusive, caring, and psychologically safe workplace where diverse perspectives, ideas, and approaches lead to high performance.


What we are looking for

  • Some hands-on experience with Data or Software Engineering — through work, internships, or your own projects. We care more about how you think than years on a CV.
  • Strong Python engineering skills (e.g. clean code, tests, packaging).
  • Solid SQL, and a working understanding of data stores for both transactional and analytical workloads.
  • Comfort with Git and good engineering habits — testing, CI/CD, and code review.
  • Genuine interest in working with AI tools day-to-day — both using them to ship faster and thinking critically about where they help and where they don’t.
  • A strong communicator and team player who thrives in a cross-functional team.


We value potential over perfect match, and we encourage you to apply even if you don't meet all the above. We consider candidates across experience levels.


Location

We support flexible working arrangements
column2:
however, success in this role depends on close collaboration, which works best when colleagues are physically together. You should therefore expect to work most of the time from our Aarhus office. Our working language is English, and we welcome applications from non-Danish speakers.


Benefits

  • Professional development and clear career growth opportunities.
  • Competitive compensation and employee shares.
  • Good maternity/paternity terms.
  • Modern office environment with a healthy canteen and snacks.
  • Visa support (if needed).


The practical stuff

Start date: As soon as possible.


To apply, please submit your CV, grade transcript, cover letter, and other relevant documents via the link below.


Applications will be processed on a rolling basis.
