4431690546,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/staff-analytics-engineer-1-year-fixed-term-contract-at-usercentrics-4431690546?position=4&pageNum=2&refId=%2FZQoS9jqf7x9Vo%2FcHAkL7A%3D%3D&trackingId=f3%2FLfPb5fCTKdbzDvmiisw%3D%3D,Staff Analytics Engineer (1 Year Fixed-Term Contract),Usercentrics,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","SHAPE THE FUTURE OF PRIVACY WITH USERCENTRICS
Hybrid position - 2/3 days in the office
1 year fixed-term contract

Usercentrics is a global leader in the field of consent management platforms (CMP). We follow our company vision to build a world where user privacy enables a thriving digital ecosystem. Our mission is to provide privacy solutions that empower organizations to embrace data privacy while building trust with their users through freedom of choice. With our product, businesses can collect, manage and document user consents on websites and apps, and achieve full compliance in accordance with global privacy regulations while facilitating high consent rates and building trust with their customers.

We're looking for an experienced Staff Analytics Engineer to own the design and delivery of scalable data models and data products across our data warehouse, semantic layer, and reporting layer. Reporting to the Director of Analytics, you will enable critical decision making across the business, from marketing and product to commercial operations.

This is a senior individual contributor role with technical leadership responsibilities. You will not manage a team initially, but you will set standards and mentor others. This role may evolve into a people management position as the team scales.
Your Tasks
Drive the development and expansion of our Data Warehouse and BI solutions, with a focus on simplicity, scalability, and reliability, ensuring they grow sustainably
Own and evolve the Analytics Engineering strategy, including architectural decisions, modeling standards, and the long term roadmap.
Collaborate with Data Analysts, Data Scientists, and backend engineers to deliver trusted, well modeled, analysis-ready datasets.
Apply software engineering best practices across the analytics codebase, including version control, code reviews, automated testing, lineage, and continuous integration.
Evaluate, select, and deploy tools and frameworks to strengthen data quality, governance, and reliability.
Partner closely with business stakeholders to design and scale the semantic layer to support diverse and evolving business needs, enabling and accelerating self service analytics.
Act as a go to person for Data Analysts and Data Scientists, providing guidance to improve pipelines and remove inefficiencies, redundancies, and technical bottlenecks.
Mentor and support other team members as the team scales, contributing to capability building, onboarding, and continuous knowledge sharing.
Bring strategic thinking and innovative ideas to help shape a growing team and deliver meaningful business impact at scale.
Requirements
5+ years of relevant experience in a comparable role, designing and scaling complex data models for analytics and data science.
A unique combination of technical expertise, business acumen, and communication skills, enabling you to persuade and influence data product stakeholders.
Proficiency with dimensional modeling (star schema) and medallion architecture, with the ability to design and develop curated data marts.
Strong coding skills in SQL and proficiency in DBT, orchestration tools, and engineering best practices.
Experience with cloud data warehouses (BigQuery preferred) and semantic layers such as MetricFlow, LookML, Omni, or Cube
Curiosity, strong problem solving skills, and a proactive, creative mindset.
Excellent organizational and communication skills, both verbal and written.

Don't meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and people of colour are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Usercentrics we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.
Benefits
Why join Usercentrics?
Joining Usercentrics means becoming part of a fast-growing, diverse and international team of tech enthusiasts and entrepreneurially-minded who build our success story together
Company culture is important to us - we strive to continuously develop a positive, vibrant and inspiring environment that enables everyone to thrive both personally and professionally
Get involved! We have plenty of initiatives and love to see people from all department enthusiastically participating and shaping our future together in different cross-department projects
Your work-life balance is important to us too - we offer flexible working hours, hybrid working and the possibility of workcations (in accordance with our company policy)
We always remember to have fun along the way, both in our day-to-day work and at our regular team events on site in our offices in Munich, Copenhagen, Odense, Lisbon and Prague or online

4432211465,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/ai-research-engineer-multi-modal-vision-at-jobgether-4432211465?position=6&pageNum=2&refId=%2FZQoS9jqf7x9Vo%2FcHAkL7A%3D%3D&trackingId=pKOJjcIb%2Bx0mA6vSNFCLZg%3D%3D,AI Research Engineer (Multi-Modal & Vision),Jobgether,Denmark,"This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for an AI Research Engineer (Multi-Modal & Vision) based in Denmark.

This is an exciting opportunity for a research-focused AI engineer to contribute to the development of advanced multimodal systems that combine vision and language capabilities. The role covers the full AI model lifecycle, from dataset creation and training pipeline development to model evaluation, optimization, and deployment. Working within a highly skilled and collaborative team, you will help build scalable AI solutions designed for real-world production environments. The position offers significant ownership, direct impact on cutting-edge research initiatives, and the opportunity to apply state-of-the-art techniques to solve complex challenges. Ideal candidates are passionate about advancing multimodal AI while maintaining a strong engineering mindset focused on measurable outcomes and practical deployment.
Accountabilities
Conduct end-to-end research and development of vision-language models, including training, evaluation, optimization, and deployment activities.
Design and implement advanced post-training methodologies such as supervised fine-tuning, knowledge distillation, and reinforcement learning from human feedback.
Build, curate, filter, and maintain high-quality multimodal datasets tailored to domain-specific applications.
Improve model efficiency and scalability through optimization, compression, and adaptation techniques suitable for resource-constrained environments.
Develop benchmarking systems and evaluation frameworks to assess model quality, robustness, and real-world performance.
Build and maintain distributed training workflows across GPU infrastructure while identifying and resolving performance bottlenecks.
Contribute to open-source AI ecosystems by leveraging and enhancing models, datasets, and development tools.
Monitor emerging research in multimodal learning and vision-language systems, translating relevant advancements into practical improvements.
Collaborate on research publications and contribute to scientific advancements through conference or journal submissions when appropriate.
Requirements
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field
column2: Master's or PhD preferred.

column1: Strong hands-on experience working with multimodal AI systems, particularly vision-language models.
Proven expertise in supervised fine-tuning, knowledge distillation, reinforcement learning from feedback, and other post-training optimization techniques.
Experience with parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches and distributed training frameworks.
Demonstrated success improving model performance on industry-standard benchmarks or production use cases.
Strong understanding of model optimization techniques for deployment in resource-constrained environments.
Experience building scalable machine learning pipelines and training workflows on GPU infrastructure.
Proven contributions to open-source multimodal AI projects through platforms such as GitHub or Hugging Face.
Research background supported by publications in leading AI conferences or journals is highly desirable.
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and the ability to balance research innovation with production-oriented engineering practices.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively within distributed, cross-functional teams.
Benefits
Competitive salary package aligned with experience and expertise.
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge multimodal AI and vision-language research projects.
Fully remote work environment with global collaboration opportunities.
Exposure to large-scale AI infrastructure and advanced machine learning technologies.
High degree of autonomy, ownership, and impact on product and research outcomes.
Collaboration with experienced researchers, engineers, and AI specialists.
Professional growth opportunities through research, innovation, and publication support.
Flexible working arrangements that support work-life balance.
Dynamic, fast-paced environment focused on innovation and continuous learning.
How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

4432223167,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/souschef-fuldtid-odder-at-lidl-danmark-4432223167?position=7&pageNum=2&refId=%2FZQoS9jqf7x9Vo%2FcHAkL7A%3D%3D&trackingId=qDg80ksFLIceTcmcGanbDQ%3D%3D,Souschef - Fuldtid - Odder,Lidl Danmark,"Odder, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","Introduktion
Kan du motivere branchens bedste kolleger og bidrage til, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar? Så er du måske vores nye souschef.

Hos Lidl bliver du en del af Europas største dagligvarekæde med 150+ butikker i Danmark. Sammen er vi drevet af et fælles mål om at blive danskernes førstevalg, og på #teamlidl kommer du med på en succesfuld rejse – på et hold med høje ambitioner til fælles. Vi har brug for stærke ledere, der giver vores medarbejdere retning og overskud, så vi sammen skaber rammerne for landets bedste indkøbsoplevelse.

Som souschef tager du aktiv del i det daglige arbejde i butikken, og får medansvar for butikkens drift og resultater. Hos os får du ansvar lige fra første arbejdsdag og bliver samtidig en del af et ambitiøst karriereudviklingsforløb. Som Europas største dagligvarekæde har vi nemlig høje ambitioner til fælles – i verden og i hverdagen, med grænseløse muligheder og holdånd i en klasse for sig.

Som souschef i Lidl får du en ansvarsfuld rolle med spændende udfordringer og en konkurrencedygtig lønpakke. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter, så du har forudsætningerne for at opnå succes i din nye stilling.
Om jobbet

Som souschef er du med til at drive butikken og lede medarbejderne. Du har medansvar for, at butikken lever op til kundernes forventninger, så de bliver ved med at handle hos os. Ud over at bestille varer, fylde op og ekspedere kunderne ved kassen, får du også følgende opgaver, som du løser i tæt samarbejde med butikschefen:
Planlægning af ressourcer og prioritering af arbejdsopgaver
Oplæring af medarbejdere, så de efterlever Lidls værdier og butikskoncepter
Ledelse af medarbejdere så de lykkes i deres job
Sørge for, at vores butikskoncepter overholdes
Ansvar for butikken når butikschefen ikke er til stede
Om dig

Du er en empatisk og stærk ledertype, der har nemt ved motivere dine medarbejdere. Du bliver respekteret for din faglighed, og du sætter en ære i at tage del i arbejdsopgaverne i den daglige drift, og være med til at sikre, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar.
Du er godt rustet til jobbet, hvis du:
Har brancheerfaring og gerne erfaring fra lignende stilling
Har gennemført et lederkursus eller er motiveret for at udvikle dig som leder
Har en venlig og professionel fremtoning
Inspirerer dine medarbejdere til at følge dit gode eksempel
Har overblik og fokus på detaljen
Ser butikken som et team og ikke er bleg for at give en hånd med, når en kollega har brug for hjælp
Har god forståelse for arbejdsprocesser, procedurer og koncepter
Vi tilbyder
Som souschef i Lidl får du:
En udfordrende rolle, hvor tingene går stærkt
Verdensklasse sammenhold
En konkurrencedygtig lønpakke på 26.000 – 32.500 kr. brutto om måneden
Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
Løn og vilkår i henhold til gældende overenskomst med HK
En grundig oplæring i vores arbejdsgange og koncepter
Adgang til skræddersyede kurser, der udvikler dig både personligt og fagligt
Mulighed for sundhedsordning
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.
Vil du med på #teamlidl?
Sådan søger du stillingen:
Klik på ”Søg nu”
Udfyld formularen
Vedhæft dit motiverede CV
Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.
Vores rekrutteringsproces består af en telefonsamtale og en fysisk samtale i butikken.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidlvest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0535, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.
Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!

Welcome to Engine Lifecycle Management

You will join the Lifecycle Processes and Digital Applications department within the functional area Engine Lifecycle Management. We are a part of a unified Engineering Two-Stroke organisation with the clear ambition to reflect our role as the crankshaft of the MAN ES Engine lifecycle, responsible for the overall end-to-end processes covering the full engine lifecycle.

Alongside our colleagues within the functional area, we ensure production process validation and quality in the supply chain, and safe and compliant market introduction of marine products through an efficient approval process.

We collaborate with a wide range of departments within Engineering, R&D and our after-sales organization PrimeServ. The working atmosphere is relaxed and all are committed to succeeding as a team by being supportive, flexible and sharing knowledge and experience. There is space for creativity and new ideas.
Are you a student ready to apply your theoretical knowledge in practice?

We offer you the opportunity to connect your education with valuable practical experience in a large international company, where diversity and inclusion drive innovation, enabling us to create cutting-edge energy solutions.

At Everllence we have a Student Network created by students for students. Its purpose is to connect students across Denmark, fostering strong academic and social relationships across various departments. This network also offers an opportunity to expand academic connections, potentially paving the way for a future career at Everllence.
In 2027, we will consolidate our Zealand locations into one large site in Trekroner, Roskilde.
Build your future with us
Please apply before the 1st og July 2026. Interviews will be held during the application period.
Questions? You are welcome to contact Benoit Rauturier by email benoit.rauturier@everllence.com

We aim for a fair and inclusive recruitment process. Regardless of gender, age, religion, disability, or ethnic background, we encourage you to apply. We embrace a global mindset and intercultural environment to ensure a collaborative and inclusive workplace. If you need support, such as assistive tools for dyslexia, we can assist you.

4433085626,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-science-student-assistant-at-sea-impact-4433085626?position=2&pageNum=5&refId=XnvZZ5D2wqgFumyZ4tUtEA%3D%3D&trackingId=%2FpO1ZYuU0Th6PvJo%2BzeNxQ%3D%3D,Data Science Student Assistant,Sea Impact,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Do you want to contribute to the growth of a unique offshore wind data intelligence company within the Renewable Energy industry? Sea Impact is looking for a Data Science Student Assistant to support software development and push the boundaries for different datasets.
Innovative Market Intelligence based on Unique Big Data Analysis for a Growing Offshore Wind Industry

At Sea Impact, we specialise in market intelligence for the offshore wind industry. We have developed a revolutionary market intelligence platform that provides key benchmarks for the design, installation, and maintenance of offshore wind farms. Our analyses are based on an innovative methodology of analysing vessel positions for market intelligence. We work with some of the world’s leading offshore wind developers, turbine OEMs and consultancies.
Help Us Create Unique Market Intelligence for Offshore Wind

Based in our Copenhagen office, you will support developing data-driven solutions by analysing offshore wind activity and combining our unique offshore wind data installation data and any publicly available data about the market development.
Your key responsibilities will include:

Data Storage, Integration and Management: Develop and maintain scalable and efficient data pipelines on Azure, while ensuring data integrity and spatial accuracy. Handle interfaces between our cloud-based PostgreSQL databases and Power BI and React-based front ends.
Predictive Analysis: Leverage ML-based solutions to develop new modules for our market intelligence platform, including computer vision and forecasting models.
Data Analysis: Utilize tools such as Power BI and QGIS to analyze geospatial data and extract valuable insights.
Ad-hoc tasks: provide support for ad-hoc tasks such as developing custom reports.
Support the IT development of Sea Impact and have the chance to shape and form it.
As it is a role in a small company you will have plenty of room to influence your position and take on new and exciting tasks as we grow.
Solution-Driven Data Scientist with a Strong Drive

At Sea Impact, we know success comes in many forms. What matters most is your mindset, curiosity, and drive to make an impact. The following qualities and experiences will help you succeed in this role:
You are currently studying towards a bachelor’s degree or have recently started a master’s program within Engineering, Computer Science, or similar
You are proficient in Python and SQL
Have some experience with databases or in developing ML-based solutions
Have previously worked with some of these tools: QGIS, PostgreSQL, Claude Code, Codex, Microsoft Azure, Power BI
You are excited about being part of establishing a new organisation and creating industry-leading market intelligence for offshore wind
Have a proactive and entrepreneurial attitude and thrive in a fast-paced environment, you will enjoy “building something bigger together”

Furthermore, you must have strong communication skills and function well in a collaborative environment. You are structured, proactive, and you are ready to learn and take on new challenges. As Sea Impact is a global company, you must have strong English communication skills.
Experience the Highest Degree of Involvement

A unique and challenging opportunity, where you will be part of forming the future growth of Sea Impact and success towards our clients. You will have the opportunity to play a central role in the development of Sea Impact and have chance to drive the development of market intelligence. Alongside a small team of dedicated problem-solvers, you are given the chance to learn and challenge yourself, while playing a vital part in the continued success of Sea Impact. Above that, you will join an ambitious team that is dedicated to delivering the highest quality, utilizing the diverse expertise and skillset we all bring.
Our Commitment to Inclusion and Fair Hiring

We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace drives innovation, creativity, and stronger results. At Sea Impact, we are committed to fair and transparent hiring processes, welcoming candidates from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. Our hiring decisions focus on what each person brings to the role and our team, and we are committed to ensuring transparent salary structures, with pay decisions based on clear, objective, and gender neutral criteria.
How to Apply

We look forward to receiving your application. Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis, and we reserve the right to close the recruitment process once the right candidate has been found. Please apply directly through our website. If you have any questions or would like to know more about the position, please contact People & Development at recruitment@peak-wind.com.

Innovative Market Intelligence based on Unique Big Data Analysis for a Growing Offshore Wind Industry

At Sea Impact, we specialise in market intelligence for the offshore wind industry. We have developed a revolutionary market intelligence platform that provides key benchmarks for the design, installation, and maintenance of offshore wind farms. Our analyses are based on an innovative methodology of analysing vessel positions for market intelligence. We work with some of the world’s leading offshore wind developers, turbine OEMs and consultancies.

Based in our Copenhagen office, you will support developing data-driven solutions by analysing offshore wind activity and combining our unique offshore wind data installation data and any publicly available data about the market development.

  • Data Analysis: Utilize tools such as Power BI and QGIS to analyze geospatial data and extract valuable insights.
  • Ad-hoc tasks: provide support for ad-hoc tasks such as developing custom reports.
  • Support the IT development of Sea Impact and have the chance to shape and form it.


As it is a role in a small company you will have plenty of room to influence your position and take on new and exciting tasks as we grow.


Solution-Driven Data Scientist with a Strong Drive

At Sea Impact, we know success comes in many forms. What matters most is your mindset, curiosity, and drive to make an impact. The following qualities and experiences will help you succeed in this role:

  • You are currently studying towards a bachelor’s degree or have recently started a master’s program within Engineering, Computer Science, or similar
  • You are proficient in Python and SQL
  • Have some experience with databases or in developing ML-based solutions
  • Have previously worked with some of these tools: QGIS, PostgreSQL, Claude Code, Codex, Microsoft Azure, Power BI
  • You are excited about being part of establishing a new organisation and creating industry-leading market intelligence for offshore wind
  • Have a proactive and entrepreneurial attitude and thrive in a fast-paced environment, you will enjoy “building something bigger together”


Furthermore, you must have strong communication skills and function well in a collaborative environment. You are structured, proactive, and you are ready to learn and take on new challenges. As Sea Impact is a global company, you must have strong English communication skills.


Experience the Highest Degree of Involvement

A unique and challenging opportunity, where you will be part of forming the future growth of Sea Impact and success towards our clients. You will have the opportunity to play a central role in the development of Sea Impact and have chance to drive the development of market intelligence. Alongside a small team of dedicated problem-solvers, you are given the chance to learn and challenge yourself, while playing a vital part in the continued success of Sea Impact. Above that, you will join an ambitious team that is dedicated to delivering the highest quality, utilizing the diverse expertise and skillset we all bring.


Our Commitment to Inclusion and Fair Hiring

We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace drives innovation, creativity, and stronger results. At Sea Impact, we are committed to fair and transparent hiring processes, welcoming candidates from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. How to Apply

4430987188,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/quantitative-researcher-at-venture-up-4430987188?position=3&pageNum=5&refId=XnvZZ5D2wqgFumyZ4tUtEA%3D%3D&trackingId=MmsoPMlru29GccaOiEcHAg%3D%3D,Quantitative Researcher,Venture Up,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Quantitative Researcher

Are you a quantitatively minded problem-solver who enjoys turning complex financial data into robust, systematic strategies? Do you combine strong mathematical thinking with practical programming skills — and have you already applied them in a financial markets context?

A leading quantitative investment team is looking for a Quantitative Researcher to help design, develop, and improve equity statistical arbitrage strategies. This role sits at the intersection of research and development — requiring both strong quantitative reasoning and the ability to build reliable research tools and models in code.
This is an opportunity to work in a collaborative, intellectually rigorous environment where ideas are tested quickly and engineering quality matters.
What You'll Do:
📊 Develop Quantitative Strategies – Research and implement systematic equity statistical arbitrage models using large financial datasets.
🧪 Experiment and Iterate – Design research experiments, evaluate signals, and refine strategies through rigorous analysis and testing.
💻 Build Research Tools – Write clean, efficient Python code to process data, develop models, and support the research pipeline.
📈 Work with Financial Data – Analyse complex datasets to identify patterns, inefficiencies, and opportunities in financial markets — particularly fixed income and equity instruments.
🤝 Collaborate with Engineers and Researchers – Work closely with a small team of technically strong colleagues to translate ideas into production-ready strategies.
The Team:

You'll join a lean quantitative research group focused on systematic trading strategies. The environment is highly technical but pragmatic — ideas are judged by evidence, and researchers are expected to both design models and build the tools needed to test them. The team values people who combine theoretical insight with the ability to implement their ideas in code.
What They're Looking For:
Strong quantitative background (e.g. Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, or similar)
Experience applying quantitative or ML methods to financial data — whether in systematic trading, derivatives, risk modelling, or a related area of financial markets
Strong Python skills and experience working with large datasets
Evidence of practical software development experience
Ability to move between theory and implementation
This is not a role where financial markets knowledge can be learned on the job — some prior exposure to financial instruments or trading environments is expected
You may come from industry or academia, but should be able to point to concrete experience working with financial data or within a financial markets context.
What They Offer:
Highly competitive compensation with strong bonus potential
Pension, healthcare, and comprehensive benefits
A collaborative environment with direct impact on live trading strategies
The opportunity to work on challenging quantitative problems with high-quality data and infrastructure
💰 Base salary is 60-90 Danish Krona per month (€120K – €150K equivalent per year) and benefits include bonus

4430495152,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-data-engineer-at-implement-consulting-group-4430495152?position=4&pageNum=5&refId=XnvZZ5D2wqgFumyZ4tUtEA%3D%3D&trackingId=UPfH7ghWNhGYKDCfgeIkCg%3D%3D,Student Data Engineer,Implement Consulting Group,"Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Do you enjoy working with data – building reliable foundations and turning complex information into clear insights? At Implement Consulting Group, we're looking for a Student Data Engineer to join our internal team and help develop our data platforms and analytics capabilities further.
What you’ll do
It's a hands-on opportunity for a curious, motivated student who wants to work with data, systems, and business challenges – and to grow into data engineering alongside experienced colleagues.
You’ll support the development and maintenance of our internal data foundation – helping ensure that data across Implement is structured, reliable, and accessible for analysis and decision-making.
Your work will include, but not be limited to:
Transforming and preparing data for analytics and reporting using SQL and Python
Helping develop, maintain, and optimise our data pipelines
Contributing to data modelling and structuring of datasets
Working with tools such as Power BI and modern data platforms
Collaborating with colleagues across teams to understand data needs and ensure quality
The role sits at the intersection of data engineering and analytics
, working with data from multiple source systems. You’ll work closely with experienced data engineers and consultants in a team where learning, sparring, and quality come first.
What you’ll bring
We’re looking for someone who is structured, curious, and motivated to learn. You take ownership of your tasks, ask questions when needed, and enjoy working with data.
You bring:
A solid foundation in SQL, including querying, transforming, and validating data
Experience with Power BI or similar analytics tools
A basic understanding of ETL/ELT processes
Familiarity with data modelling concepts, such as star schemas, fact tables, and dimension tables
A willingness to learn new tools and technologies quickly
Basic experience with Python for data ingestion and/or processing
It is also an advantage if you have:
Experience with cloud data platforms such as Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, or Snowflake
Knowledge of data orchestration tools such as Azure Data Factory, Fabric Pipelines, Airflow, or dbt
Experience with Git or version control tools
A basic understanding of APIs, JSON, and data integration
What we offer

At Implement, you’ll be part of a collaborative and ambitious environment where we take both learning and quality seriously. We believe in combining freedom with ownership, collaboration without hierarchy, and a strong focus on learning and long-term impact.
You can expect:
Hands-on experience with data engineering in practice
Work with modern data platforms and tools
Support from experienced colleagues who are invested in your development
Responsibility early on — with the right support and sparring
The opportunity to grow your role over time
How to apply
Please submit your application ASAP, as we review applications and conduct interviews on an ongoing basis.
Apply now and take the next step in your data journey with Implement.
Make your next move matter
About us

Implement Consulting Group (founded 1996) is an international management consulting firm with over 1,800 employees. Headquartered in Copenhagen, we have 12 offices across the Nordics, the DACH region and the United States, making us one of the largest European consultancies. We support organisations in delivering sustainable strategic, commercial, and operational transformations. To help our clients succeed with their most critical change initiatives, we aim to attract, develop, and promote the best talent by creating a unique, collaborative learning environment where people can follow their passion, shape their own careers, and choose their own leaders. and choose their own leaders.

Do you enjoy working with data – building reliable foundations and turning complex information into clear insights? At Implement Consulting Group, we're looking for a Student Data Engineer to join our internal team and help develop our data platforms and analytics capabilities further.

What you’ll do

It's a hands-on opportunity for a curious, motivated student who wants to work with data, systems, and business challenges – and to grow into data engineering alongside experienced colleagues.

You’ll support the development and maintenance of our internal data foundation – helping ensure that data across Implement is structured, reliable, and accessible for analysis and decision-making.

Your work will include, but not be limited to:

  • Transforming and preparing data for analytics and reporting using SQL and Python
  • Helping develop, maintain, and optimise our data pipelines
  • Contributing to data modelling and structuring of datasets
  • Working with tools such as Power BI and modern data platforms
  • Collaborating with colleagues across teams to understand data needs and ensure quality

The role sits at the intersection of data engineering and analytics
, working with data from multiple source systems. You’ll work closely with experienced data engineers and consultants in a team where learning, sparring, and quality come first.

What you’ll bring

We’re looking for someone who is structured, curious, and motivated to learn. You take ownership of your tasks, ask questions when needed, and enjoy working with data.

You bring:

  • A solid foundation in SQL, including querying, transforming, and validating data
  • Experience with Power BI or similar analytics tools
  • A basic understanding of ETL/ELT processes
  • Familiarity with data modelling concepts, such as star schemas, fact tables, and dimension tables
  • A willingness to learn new tools and technologies quickly
  • Basic experience with Python for data ingestion and/or processing

It is also an advantage if you have:

  • Experience with cloud data platforms such as Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, or Snowflake
  • Knowledge of data orchestration tools such as Azure Data Factory, Fabric Pipelines, Airflow, or dbt
  • Experience with Git or version control tools
  • A basic understanding of APIs, JSON, and data integration

What we offer

At Implement, you’ll be part of a collaborative and ambitious environment where we take both learning and quality seriously. We believe in combining freedom with ownership, collaboration without hierarchy, and a strong focus on learning and long-term impact.

You can expect:

  • Hands-on experience with data engineering in practice
  • Work with modern data platforms and tools
  • Support from experienced colleagues who are invested in your development
  • Responsibility early on — with the right support and sparring
  • The opportunity to grow your role over time

How to apply

Please submit your application ASAP, as we review applications and conduct interviews on an ongoing basis.

Apply now and take the next step in your data journey with Implement.



Make your next move matter

About us

Implement Consulting Group (founded 1996) is an international management consulting firm with over 1,800 employees. Headquartered in Copenhagen, we have 12 offices across the Nordics, the DACH region and the United States, making us one of the largest European consultancies. We support organisations in delivering sustainable strategic, commercial, and operational transformations. To help our clients succeed with their most critical change initiatives, we aim to attract, develop, and promote the best talent by creating a unique, collaborative learning environment where people can follow their passion, shape their own careers, and choose their own leaders. and choose their own leaders.

As the leading Nordic online department store - 1,600+ brands, 14 markets, one of the world's most automated fulfilment centres - data powers every decision we make at Boozt.
We're hiring two new colleagues across our BI function: one in our Logistics Reporting & Analytics (Warehouse & operations) and in our Commercial Business Partner Team.
Different teams, different challenges, one thing in common: your data insights help shape smarter decisions and turn them into real business impact.
Logistics Reporting & Analytics: Early Career
Operations-focused

Learn the business from the ground up. Every order that reaches a customer is the result of an efficient supply chain, a distribution network, and a customer service operation working in sync. You'll sit close to the warehouse floor, fulfilment, returns, and last mile, turning operational data into dashboards and analyses that help teams move faster and smarter.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Build, develop, and maintain QlikCloud apps end to end - from data load and modelling to the dashboards teams use every day
Write and optimise load scripts, build data models, and keep apps fast and reliable as data volumes grow
Run ad hoc analysis in Qlik to answer operational questions quickly when they come up
Turn requirements from distribution, supply chain, and customer service teams into well-built, reusable reporting
Maintain and improve our existing Qlik reporting - refactoring, fixing, and extending what's already there
Work with our data and engineering teams on data quality and the pipelines that feed Qlik (BigQuery)
YOUR PROFILE
2> years in a BI, analytics data role - internships, thesis work, and student jobs count
Comfortable with SQL and Qlik scripting / data modelling, or genuinely motivated to get there fast
Hands-on with QlikCloud, or able to learn it quickly (experience with Power BI, Tableau, or Looker transfers well

Commercial Business Partner Team: Mid-level
Engineering-focused

Work with large and complex datasets to build the data foundation behind key commercial decisions. You'll design and maintain the data models, pipelines, and BigQuery architecture that our commercial teams rely on to understand performance, set priorities, and identify growth opportunities. Your work powers high-impact commercial decisions.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Design and maintain the BigQuery data infrastructure powering commercial reporting
Develop Qlik Sense scripts and maintain dashboards and lead our migration to Qlik Cloud
Integrate data from multiple source systems using SQL, including one-off data pulls when needed
Document data models, calculations, and mappings so the work stays transparent and maintainable
Partner with analysts and business partners to turn business requirements into robust data products
YOUR PROFILE
2+< years building BI solutions end to end: data models, pipelines, KPIs, dashboards
Strong SQL and scripting skills, with hands-on experience integrating data from multiple source systems
Proactive and driving, you push work forward without waiting to be told
Comfortable translating non-technical requirements into technical solutions
Bachelor's in computer science, information systems, or a related field
column2: fluent English

4432271449,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/receptionist-afl%C3%B8sere-at-hages-badehotel-4432271449?position=8&pageNum=5&refId=XnvZZ5D2wqgFumyZ4tUtEA%3D%3D&trackingId=ubjuNQ2B%2BbzKEyiuUCsM0Q%3D%3D,Receptionist afløsere,Hages Badehotel,"Lolland Municipality, Region Zealand, Denmark","Afløsere til Hages Badehotel Reception
Brænder du for service, er energien høj og elsker du at arbejde selvstændigt?
Hages Badehotel leder efter en smilende, ansvarsbevidst og top serviceminded afløser til receptionen. Der er tale om en stilling som ferieafløser med mulighed for forlængelse efter ferieperioden.
Du skal være imødekommende, smilende og have lyst til at gøre en forskel for vores skønne gæster. Du vil stå alene i receptionen på vagterne, så det er vigtigt at du kan lide at arbejde selvstændigt.
Vi har en flad organisation og højt til loftet. Du har derfor muligheden for at træffe beslutninger direkte i samspillet med gæsten i receptionen, som vi mener giver den bedste service.
Arbejdsopgaver:
At yde imødekommende, glad og jordnær service til vores gæster
Check ind / Check out af vores gæster
Reservationer per mail
Telefonbetjening
Administration ved ankomst gennemgang, afrejse gennemgang og tildeling af værelser
Opsalg af værelsesopgraderinger
Salg fra vores bar
Rammerne:
Ansættelse hurtigst muligt
Ferieafløser med mulighed for efterfølgende forlængelse
26-35 timer pr. uge
Arbejdstid i tidsrummet kl. 06.15-23.30 dog oftest indenfor tidsrummet 14-45-23.30
Mulighed for arbejde hver anden weekend
Profil og egenskaber:
Du brænder for service
Du har gerne erfaring fra et servicefag
Du forstår dansk og taler et af de nordiske sprog (dansk/svensk/norsk) på et avanceret niveau samt behersker engelsk i skrift og tale – også på et avanceret niveau.
Du har flair for IT – vi benytter Office pakken og Mews hotelsystem
Du har en god energi og en positiv indstilling – samtidig med at du er proaktiv og tager ansvar for din arbejdsplads
Ansøgning:
Der bliver indkaldt til samtaler løbende. Stillingen er til tiltrædelse snarest muligt.
Vi glæder os meget til at høre fra dig.

4432223760,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/sekretariatsleder-at-fuldt-hus-4432223760?position=9&pageNum=5&refId=XnvZZ5D2wqgFumyZ4tUtEA%3D%3D&trackingId=%2FSC1KeUXoHZ4En%2FvvH5cjA%3D%3D,Sekretariatsleder,Fuldt Hus,Copenhagen Metropolitan Area,"Vil du åbne kulturlivets fællesskaber for flere?

I Fuldt Hus tror vi på, at kulturoplevelser kan gøre en reel forskel. Vi arbejder for et kulturliv med plads til flere ved at bygge bro mellem kulturinstitutioner og mennesker i udsatte positioner. Vi samarbejder med mere end 30 kulturinstitutioner over hele landet og formidler gratis billetter til mere end 150 modtagerorganisationer.

Nu søger vi en sekretariatsleder, der vil stå i spidsen for vores lille sekretariat og drive udviklingen i tæt samspil med bestyrelsen og vores samarbejdspartnere. Det er en stilling for dig, der motiveres af at skabe meningsfulde forbindelser mellem mennesker, kultur og samfund — arbejder struktureret og som trives med både ansvar og relations opbyggende arbejde.

Som sekretariatsleder vil du få mulighed for at sætte retning og præge udviklingen i en lille, men landsdækkende forening. Du vil få ansvar for den daglige ledelse og for at omsætte strategi til praksis. Samtidig er du bindeled mellem bestyrelse, medarbejdere og eksterne samarbejdspartnere. Stillingen kræver, at du både kan skabe overblik, sikre drift og bidrage til organisationens videre udvikling.
Dine opgaver
Du vil bl.a.:
sikre drift og udvikling i tråd med strategien og at Fuldt Hus’ digitale billetplatform fungerer godt og understøtter kerneopgaven
opbygge og vedligeholde samarbejde samt bidrage til kommunikation og partnerskaber
have ansvar for budget og økonomi, herunder arbejde med fundraising, medlems- og støttebidrag
lede og udvikle sekretariatets arbejde herunder varetage personaleledelse og koordinering med eksterne leverandører
betjene bestyrelsen og sikre fremdrift i organisationen
Vi forestiller os, at du
har erfaring fra en NGO, forening eller kulturorganisation
arbejder selvstændigt, struktureret og kan skabe stærke relationer og samarbejder
har blik for processer og økonomi samt har stærke IT-kompetencer
er omstillingsparat og kan holde bolde i luften og samtidig bevare overblikket
trives i en lille organisation med både strategi og hands-on opgaver og motiveres af at gøre kulturlivet mere tilgængeligt
har relevant uddannelsesbaggrund og/eller har erhvervserfaring, som kan bidrage positivt ind i rollen. Erfaring med ledelse, projektledelse, fundraising eller bestyrelsesarbejde er en fordel.
Løn og vilkår

Stillingen er på 30 timer ugentligt placeret i tidsrummet fra kl. 08.00 til kl. 17.00. Arbejdsplads på hjemmeadresse eller i kontorfællesskab efter aftale. Der kan forekomme arrangementer, hvor arbejdstiden varierer fra ovenstående, hvorfor det forventes, at du er fleksibel.
Du vil referere til forperson for bestyrelsen, Sara Kudsk-Iversen.

Lønnen udgør 35.000 kr. pr. måned. Hertil kommer feriepenge og pensionstillæg på 15%. Vi tilbyder en stilling med høj grad af indflydelse, fleksibilitet og mening. Stillingen er indtil videre tidsbegrænset til medio 2028 og med ansættelse pr. 1.oktober 2026.
Ansøgning
Send din ansøgning og CV til ansoegning@fuldthus.dk senest den 7. august 2026.
Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte Inger Andersen på mail inan@fuldthus.dk i uge 29 og 32 eller Sara Kudsk-Iversen på mail saku@fuldthus.dk i uge 30.
1.samtaler forventes at blive afholdt den 13. august om eftermiddagen og 2. samtaler afholdes forventeligt den 17. august ml. 16 og 19.
Om Fuldt Hus

I Fuldt Hus tror vi på, at kulturoplevelser kan gøre forskel i menneskers liv. Gennem kultur får mennesker mulighed for at være en del af noget sammen med andre – at opleve nærvær, genkendelse og nye perspektiver på sig selv og verden. Det styrker trivsel, skaber tilhørsforhold og giver en konkret oplevelse af at høre til i samfundets fællesskaber.

Fuldt Hus arbejder for et kulturliv med plads til flere. Vi bygger bro mellem kulturinstitutioner og sociale aktører og formidler adgang til teatre, museer, koncerter og andre kulturoplevelser for mennesker i udsatte positioner. På den måde gør vi det muligt for flere at tage del i kulturlivets fællesskaber – og vi hjælper samtidig kulturinstitutioner med at omsætte ambitioner om mangfoldighed, social bæredygtighed og publikumsudvikling til konkret handling.
Læs mere på www.fuldthus.dk

Nu søger vi en sekretariatsleder, der vil stå i spidsen for vores lille sekretariat og drive udviklingen i tæt samspil med bestyrelsen og vores samarbejdspartnere. Det er en stilling for dig, der motiveres af at skabe meningsfulde forbindelser mellem mennesker, kultur og samfund — arbejder struktureret og som trives med både ansvar og relations opbyggende arbejde.

Som sekretariatsleder vil du få mulighed for at sætte retning og præge udviklingen i en lille, men landsdækkende forening. Du vil få ansvar for den daglige ledelse og for at omsætte strategi til praksis. Samtidig er du bindeled mellem bestyrelse, medarbejdere og eksterne samarbejdspartnere. Stillingen kræver, at du både kan skabe overblik, sikre drift og bidrage til organisationens videre udvikling.


Dine opgaver

Du vil bl.a.:

  • sikre drift og udvikling i tråd med strategien og at Fuldt Hus’ digitale billetplatform fungerer godt og understøtter kerneopgaven
  • opbygge og vedligeholde samarbejde samt bidrage til kommunikation og partnerskaber
  • have ansvar for budget og økonomi, herunder arbejde med fundraising, medlems- og støttebidrag
  • lede og udvikle sekretariatets arbejde herunder varetage personaleledelse og koordinering med eksterne leverandører
  • betjene bestyrelsen og sikre fremdrift i organisationen


Vi forestiller os, at du

  • har erfaring fra en NGO, forening eller kulturorganisation
  • arbejder selvstændigt, struktureret og kan skabe stærke relationer og samarbejder
column2:
• har blik for processer og økonomi samt har stærke IT-kompetencer
  • er omstillingsparat og kan holde bolde i luften og samtidig bevare overblikket
  • trives i en lille organisation med både strategi og hands-on opgaver og motiveres af at gøre kulturlivet mere tilgængeligt
  • har relevant uddannelsesbaggrund og/eller har erhvervserfaring, som kan bidrage positivt ind i rollen. Erfaring med ledelse, projektledelse, fundraising eller bestyrelsesarbejde er en fordel.


    • Løn og vilkår

    Stillingen er på 30 timer ugentligt placeret i tidsrummet fra kl. 08.00 til kl. 17.00. Arbejdsplads på hjemmeadresse eller i kontorfællesskab efter aftale. Der kan forekomme arrangementer, hvor arbejdstiden varierer fra ovenstående, hvorfor det forventes, at du er fleksibel.
column3:
Du vil referere til forperson for bestyrelsen, Sara Kudsk-Iversen.

Lønnen udgør 35.000 kr. pr. måned. Hertil kommer feriepenge og pensionstillæg på 15%. Vi tilbyder en stilling med høj grad af indflydelse, fleksibilitet og mening. Stillingen er indtil videre tidsbegrænset til medio 2028 og med ansættelse pr. 1.oktober 2026.


Ansøgning

Send din ansøgning og CV til ansoegning@fuldthus.dk senest den 7. august 2026.

Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte Inger Andersen på mail inan@fuldthus.dk i uge 29 og 32 eller Sara Kudsk-Iversen på mail saku@fuldthus.dk i uge 30.

1.samtaler forventes at blive afholdt den 13. august om eftermiddagen og 2. samtaler afholdes forventeligt den 17. august ml. 16 og 19.


Om Fuldt Hus

I Fuldt Hus tror vi på, at kulturoplevelser kan gøre forskel i menneskers liv. Gennem kultur får mennesker mulighed for at være en del af noget sammen med andre – at opleve nærvær, genkendelse og nye perspektiver på sig selv og verden. Det styrker trivsel, skaber tilhørsforhold og giver en konkret oplevelse af at høre til i samfundets fællesskaber.

Fuldt Hus arbejder for et kulturliv med plads til flere. Vi bygger bro mellem kulturinstitutioner og sociale aktører og formidler adgang til teatre, museer, koncerter og andre kulturoplevelser for mennesker i udsatte positioner. På den måde gør vi det muligt for flere at tage del i kulturlivets fællesskaber – og vi hjælper samtidig kulturinstitutioner med at omsætte ambitioner om mangfoldighed, social bæredygtighed og publikumsudvikling til konkret handling.

Læs mere på www.fuldthus.dk

4432405670,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/kirkesanger-at-folkekirken-4432405670?position=10&pageNum=5&refId=XnvZZ5D2wqgFumyZ4tUtEA%3D%3D&trackingId=FD%2BrQF%2FGiZYRHRx43YDVZQ%3D%3D,Kirkesanger,Folkekirken,Greater Odense Area,"Rødding, Lem, Lihme og Vejby Kirker søger en kirkesanger med tiltrædelse fra 1.august 2026. Opgaverne er først og fremmest i forbindelse med vores gudstjenester og kirkelige handlinger.
Den pågældende ledige stilling er på 4 timer pr. uge i gennemsnit, - med forventeligt merarbejde. Vi forventer at du har kendskab til folkekirken og er positiv over for det, vi står for.
Kirkesangerne Varetager Følgende Opgaver
Bede ind- og udgangsbøn.
Synge for ved gudstjenester og øvrige kirkelige handlinger, som f.eks. vielser, begravelser/bisættelser og dåb.
Indgå i et samarbejde med præster, organister og kirketjenere om de kirkelige arbejdsopgaver.
Vi forventer, at du:
God til at synge – og gerne en smule erfaring, fx fra korsang
Har stemme og frimodighed til at synge for
Er villig til at synge salmer/sange uden for salmebogen (bl.a. nye salmetillæg, rytmiske sange og Højskolesangbogen)
Kan indgå ved kirkelige handlinger og tilpasse dig de forskellige stemninger, afhængig af anledningens karakter
Er villig til at indgå i samarbejde med den øvrige medarbejdergruppe
Har kendskab til Folkekirken og er positiv over for det, vi står for
Løn Og Ansættelsesvilkår

Ansættelse af en kirkesanger er omfattet af Fællesoverenskomsten mellem Skatteministeriet og CO10 – Centralorganisationen af 2010 og organisationsaftale mellem Kirkeministeriet og CO10 for kirkefunktionærer med kerneopgaver som henholdsvis kordegn, sognemedhjælper/kirke- og kulturmedarbejdere eller kirkemusiker og det tilhørende protokollat for kirkemusikere.
Årslønnen aftales indenfor intervallet 327.436 kr. – 466.596 kr. (nutidskroner).
Der er rådighedsforpligtelse til stillingen. Rådighedstillægget udgør årligt kr. 27.118,53 (nutidskroner).

OK tillæg på kr. 1.061,76 (nutidskroner) pr. år. Løn, rådighedstillæg samt OK-tillæg kvoteres i overensstemmelse med stillingens ansættelsesbrøk (4/37). Der er tilknyttet pensionsordning til stillingen.
For yderligere spørgsmål vedrørende stillingen kan der rettes henvendelse til formand Dorthe Foged Stampe på tlf. 30529954 og til sognepræst Anne Røndal på tlf. 21745334.
Ansøgning sendes senest fredag den 26. juni 2026 kl. 12.00 til Menighedsrådet
på mail: 8549fortrolig@sogn.dk
Ansættelsessamtale og prøvesyngning forventes i uge 27

4427331442,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-worker-analytics-insights-enablement-lego-retail-at-the-lego-group-4427331442?position=1&pageNum=7&refId=F1jAcUdztnqPkn1cWrC8%2Bg%3D%3D&trackingId=WPaV4cHNpu0N6f%2F%2B4TxHmw%3D%3D,"Student Worker, Analytics & Insights Enablement, LEGO Retail",the LEGO Group,"Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark","Job Description
Are you ready to start your data and analytics career, and do you get excited by a fast paced global retail environment, then you are the one we are looking for!

This entry level position into a global and known consumer brand organization is a big opportunity. You will get to learn bottom up how the LEGO Retail business works and get exposed to the foundational commercial processes linked to our expanding data environment. Using the newest and strongest analytics tool available and big data methodologies, this role will help make sense of the data through our data lake environment.

This role offers a unique chance to experience retail data and language which you would not normally not be exposed to in a production company like The LEGO Group. This international role will expose you to many different cultures and consumer behaviours, hence, increase your awareness of how important it is to be able to collaborate across borders.

If you are new in the corporate world, want to kickstart your career and you are curious to what it takes to succeed within a global company, this entry level position in The LEGO Group and LEGO Retail is a good place to start. Our journey has just begun, and you will be able to help shape ways of working from the start and place your own unique footprint on the area.
Core Responsibilities

This role will be important for our team and help Retail BI to become a stable and trusted business partner. The person will be our safety net for reporting and ensuring our data quality and integrity, alongside validating report structures and enabling key operational measures and communication.
Number oriented, but no need for extensive IT know-how.
Should be flexible as we work in a global set up across many time zones.
Structure and precision key qualities, helping to ensure consistency & reliability across our data products.
to work across various platforms & multiple key stakeholders.
Adaptable & keen to learn across a variety of platforms & business areas, connecting with multiple key stakeholders.
Play your part in our team succeeding
The department’s key focus is to create and enable self-service data intelligence products for LEGO Retail data consumers and strategic decision makers.

Further, partnering with the analytics & insights commercial partners to craft these data points into crisp and action-oriented business recommendation to support both short term operational optimisation as well as tactical and strategical decision. The team is supporting cross functional in the commercial value chain Marketing, Finance, Operations, Stores and e-Commerce.

LEGO Retail as a standalone business unit was established in 2017 and since then we have established several standard processes and standard reporting tools, and we need help to manage and maintain these plus help with communicating daily and weekly standard reporting to key stakeholders across the business.

This position will become a key enabler to the way we operate our business on a day-to-day basis, which will give great insights to the Retail area in the LEGO Group and to how we operate as a newly start business within the business.
Do you have what it takes?
Current student on second or third year of a university or related schooling.
Bachelor’s degree in economics, Statistics, Business, Engineering or related field or equivalent working experience.
Business-related study background year 3-4 of university or related schooling.
High level of English both written and verbal is a must as there will be communication with stakeholders across the globe (Mainly UK, US and Singapore) both via mails and in meetings.
Nice to have
Global mindset, have spent time outside Denmark is a plus
Knowledge of SQL, Tableau, Power BI or similar is a benefit

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
What’s in it for you?
Here is what you can expect
Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.
Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.
Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.
Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.
Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.
Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.
Just imagine building your dream career.
Then make it real.
Join the LEGO® team today.

4420609144,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-assistant-portfolio-management-team-at-novo-nordisk-foundation-4420609144?position=3&pageNum=7&refId=F1jAcUdztnqPkn1cWrC8%2Bg%3D%3D&trackingId=wlJgdqqiD24HZkHrzD3D2g%3D%3D,"Student Assistant, Portfolio Management Team",Novo Nordisk Foundation,"Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Do you want to work in a purpose-driven environment, helping the Novo Nordisk Foundation achieve its goals to drive societal impact? We are looking for a student assistant to join our Portfolio Management Team in the Impact & Portfolio department.

In the Portfolio Management Team, you will contribute to portfolio management processes in collaboration with other departments across the Foundation. You will work with data and support analytical work that feeds directly into decisions made by senior leadership and the Portfolio Board.
We are reviewing candidates on an ongoing basis and will close the advert when the right candidate is found.
What You Will Do
You will support the team with tasks including:
Updating and maintaining the project database
Preparing slides and visual materials for leadership and board presentations
Data processing, analytics, and quality assurance on portfolio data
Supporting meeting preparation
Ad hoc analytical tasks across various projects
Requirements
We are looking for a candidate who:
Is currently enrolled in or has recently completed a bachelor's degree in data science, engineering, natural sciences, business administration, or a related field
Has strong data literacy and is comfortable working with structured data, understanding what the numbers mean and spotting when something looks off
Is a Super user in the MS Office package and particularly in Excel and PowerPoint
Has strong verbal and written communication skills in English
Skills within graphic design and data analytics is considered positive
Nice to have:
Experience with data visualisation tools or techniques
Interest in the scientific or philanthropic environment
We imagine that people would describe you as:
Flexible and meets deadlines
Thorough, systematic, and detail-oriented

On important projects that benefit society
With key actors within the areas of support
As part of a dedicated team that is passionate about creating value for society
Application and Information
We encourage you to send your application as soon as possible as we are reviewing candidates on an ongoing basis.

About The Novo Nordisk Foundation

Established in Denmark in 1924, the Novo Nordisk Foundation is an enterprise foundation with philanthropic objectives. The vision of the Foundation is to improve people's health and the sustainability of society and the planet. The Foundation's mission is to progress research and innovation in the prevention and treatment of cardiometabolic and infectious diseases, to apply and advance regenerative medicine, and to advance knowledge and solutions that support a green transformation of society.

4426824155,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/it-supporter-at-airbus-4426824155?position=5&pageNum=7&refId=F1jAcUdztnqPkn1cWrC8%2Bg%3D%3D&trackingId=F3fOZuc6HyHugaHlJ%2Fhoxg%3D%3D,IT Supporter,Airbus,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Job Description
Would you like to support a global business that operates non-stop, requires skilled colleagues and close attention? If yes, keep reading!

One of the most important aspects within IT is IT Support – we help our colleagues to do what they’re best at, by resolving the IT issues they face. If you’ve got the skills, mindset and the right experience, continue reading!

As an IT Supporter in Satair, you will join a team situated in Denmark, Singapore, China, Germany, and the US. We provide IT Support to our colleagues in the business on a 24/7 basis, by following the sun. As we are the first step into IT, guiding colleagues and leading them to our experts is also a discipline we practice. Therefore, this role entails on-call and weekend on-call shifts to provide our colleagues with seamless IT Support around the clock. In addition to the on-call shifts, you will also be a part of a rotating shift schedule, where you will be needed at work from 06:00 to 14:30 and an afternoon shift from 09:00 - 17:30

Next year we’re planning to move our Warehouse operations to a different location, and as part of this move we need the IT Supporter role to move with it. You are therefore expected to be moving with the warehouse employees to the new warehouse located in Greve.
Your daily tasks will mainly consist of 2 areas (but is not limited to): Resolving IT Incidents and completing Service Requests.
What The Job Is …

Your main responsibility is to support our colleagues in the business when we face IT issues or are given a Service Request. As you’re the first point of contact, the ambition to provide good customer service is a necessity and a timely resolution is a must.
Central Aspects Of The Job
Supporting your colleagues with IT Incidents and Service Requests
Drive and coordinate Major Incidents to ensure that we inform our colleagues (internally/externally) and get the Major Incident at hand resolved
Assist our colleagues in Infrastructure & Operations on tasks where hands are needed
Purchase equipment and ensure asset management is done according to our processes
Ensure that IAM activities are done with proper attention and care
Capture and share knowledge through written Knowledge Articles
Work closely with the different Service Desks within the organization of Airbus
What do you offer?

We’re looking for a candidate who likes the IT Support aspect in terms of helping users and making sure that our colleague got what was needed. A skill needed for the job is the ability to park your current task if an urgent issue arises and very importantly, remember to get back to where you left off. Due to the nature of IT Support, a certain part of our work comes with a level of unpredictability.

In our company, the aspect of IT Support spans wide from a technical perspective. General knowledge about IT is therefore a necessity in terms of Microsoft Operating Systems, Active Directory e.g. troubleshooting on DNS, Networking and general application understanding.

Your communication skills are important and that means you can engage in a dialogue with a skilled technician but also a colleague that has less understanding of IT than yourself. This will be needed in verbal and written form. On the written aspect, structure and an eye for detail is a plus.
We strive towards applying the principles of ITIL in our ITSM tool and you therefore need to be aware of the basics of ITIL Foundation.
As a person, your default answer to something unknown should not be ,, I don’t know…’’ but rather ,, I’ll investigate and return!’’. In some cases, patience is required and you will need it.
What are we looking for? How much experience? What type of experience?
Broad understanding of IT (hardware, software, network, communication)
A good understanding of the following will be an advantage:
Network topics such as DHCP, and DNS
Printer troubleshooting and Print Management
Ideally, experience with IT Asset Life Cycle Management (CMDB)
Ideally, experience with writing documentation, as this experience will be required when writing Knowledge Articles
ITIL Foundation certified
Bachelor or 1+ years experience within IT Support
Fluent in English both written and verbally
Written skills that demonstrates structure
Can offer valid references to attest your skills/personality
What does your next challenge look like?
At SATAIR, we believe in sustainable aviation that allows people to travel and connect across the globe.
We are on a mission to provide world class material solutions that keep aircraft flying and extend their lifespan.
In Satair, you can challenge the status quo, pioneer new ways of working, and generate real value for airlines and suppliers worldwide.
Being part of the Airbus family gives us the opportunity to live a global mindset, working in international and diverse teams and collaborating across functions, sites and cultures.

If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow, making an impact on aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.
Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!
About SATAIR

Satair is an Airbus Services company offering first-class material management solutions in the aviation aftermarket industry. We take pride in delivering an excellent customer experience around the clock, driving innovation, and contributing to aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

With our global functional organisation spanning 12 sites and locations worldwide, and a team of over 2,000 people supporting a $3.9 billion turnover, you will be part of a truly global company that continues to grow year after year.
Welcome to the Satair family!
If you want to know more about our business, have a closer look at our website www.SATAIR.com or check out our SATAIR Youtube Channel at www.youtube.com/sataircompany !
What do we offer at SATAIR?

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!
Here is a selection of some of the benefits we offer our employees in Copenhagen:
Financial Benefits: Competitive salary, Employee Stock Ownership Program, Success sharing, Health insurance, Group accident insurance, Employee discounts to local businesses
Employee Health and Wellbeing: On-site gym, App-based health & wellbeing platform, Amazing canteen at a subsided price, Massage scheme

Learning and Development: Airbus Leadership University, Large number of e-learning and training offers for personal and professional development, Promotion of professional development (e.g. postgraduate studies, technician training), Possibility of mentoring, Gain international experience
Work-life blend and life phase support: Possibility to work remotely, 4 care days, Senior days, Working from home policy
Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company
Please send us your CV and application as soon as possible to get the opportunity to become part of an exceptional global team!
We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.
When applying for the position, you accept our data privacy statement: http://www.SATAIR.com/privacy
This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.
Company
Satair A/S
Contract Type
Permanent
Experience Level
Entry Level
Job Family
Digital

By submitting your CV or application you are consenting to Airbus using and storing information about you for monitoring purposes relating to your application or future employment. This information will only be used by Airbus.

Airbus is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.

Airbus is, and always has been, committed to equal opportunities for all. As such, we will never ask for any type of monetary exchange in the frame of a recruitment process. Any impersonation of Airbus to do so should be reported to emsom@airbus.com.
At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking.

4428132871,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/listening-bar-brewery-internship-at-kombucha-inc-4428132871?position=6&pageNum=7&refId=F1jAcUdztnqPkn1cWrC8%2Bg%3D%3D&trackingId=P9fK6wGdSakExVemwvCRrw%3D%3D,Listening Bar & Brewery Internship,Kombucha Inc.,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Internship: Help Build and Run Our New Listening Bar Concept 6–12 months | Copenhagen | €700/month + possible paid shifts
The Opportunity
We’re building something new in the heart of Kødbyen — a small, ambitious listening bar located right next to the Kombucha Inc. brewery.

The bar is already open, but we’re still shaping the space, the concept, the routines, the events, the atmosphere, and the way people experience it. That means there is a lot to build — and we’re looking for a hands-on intern who wants to be part of that journey from the inside.

If you’re curious about hospitality, music, events, branding, community-building, social media, and what it takes to turn a small bar into a living concept, this is your chance to get involved in almost every part of the process.
This internship is especially relevant for a student who needs a practical internship as part of their study programme.
Your Impact
Building the Bar & Concept
Be part of developing the bar’s identity, atmosphere, routines, and guest experience.
Help shape how the bar looks, feels, communicates, and operates day to day.
Support the team in turning ideas into practical systems, events, and experiences.
Contribute to building a place that feels welcoming, creative, and alive.
Music, Events & Community
Help plan, coordinate, and run events at the bar.
Support everything from listening sessions and DJ nights to tastings, community gatherings, and creative collaborations.
Assist with booking, planning, communication, setup, execution, and follow-up.
Bring ideas for new events, music formats, partnerships, and ways to activate the space.
Operations & Running the Place
Take part in the daily running of the bar, including opening routines, service preparation, stock, and general coordination.
Help improve workflows, systems, and routines as the bar develops.
Work closely with the team to make sure the place runs smoothly, while still leaving room for experimentation and creativity.
There may also be opportunities to take paid shifts in the bar alongside the internship.
Social Media & Communication
Help build the bar’s voice and presence on social media.
Create content around the space, events, drinks, music, people, and behind-the-scenes moments.
Support with posts, stories, event communication, newsletters, and simple campaigns.
Help us tell the story of a new listening bar being built in real time.
Business & Concept Development
Support the development of new ideas for drinks, events, collaborations, and guest experiences.
Research trends, local culture, relevant brands, potential partners, music communities, and new opportunities.
Help us understand what works, what people respond to, and how the bar can grow.
What’s In It For You
A front-row seat to building a listening bar and hospitality concept from the ground up.
Hands-on experience across events, operations, branding, social media, music programming, and community-building.
A startup environment where your ideas can become real quickly.
The chance to work closely with a small, creative, and ambitious team.
Experience that is directly relevant if you’re interested in hospitality, entrepreneurship, culture, events, branding, music, or food and beverage.
A monthly internship compensation of €700.
Possible paid shifts in the bar, depending on availability and fit.
Who We’re Looking For
Someone curious, hands-on, and excited about building things.
A student looking for a 6–12 month internship as part of their study programme.
Someone interested in hospitality, music, events, social media, culture, branding, or entrepreneurship.
A person who enjoys working with people and creating good experiences.
Someone organized enough to help make things happen, but flexible enough to work in a place that is still being built.
A collaborative mindset — we’re a small team, and we work closely together.
You don’t need to know everything already. But you should be ready to learn, take initiative, and roll up your sleeves.
How To Jump In
No need for a lengthy application. Just send us:
A short video introducing yourself, or
Your CV, and examples of relevant past work, projects, events, content, or ideas.
Let us know why you want to be part of building the listening bar — and what you would love to help create.
Note: This is an internship with a monthly compensation of €700. There may also be the possibility of taking paid shifts in the bar.

A front-row seat to building a listening bar and hospitality concept from the ground up.

Hands-on experience across events, operations, branding, social media, music programming, and community-building.

A startup environment where your ideas can become real quickly.

The chance to work closely with a small, creative, and ambitious team.

Experience that is directly relevant if you’re interested in hospitality, entrepreneurship, culture, events, branding, music, or food and beverage.

A monthly internship compensation of €700.

Possible paid shifts in the bar, depending on availability and fit.

Who We’re Looking For

Someone curious, hands-on, and excited about building things.

A student looking for a 6–12 month internship as part of their study programme.

Someone interested in hospitality, music, events, social media, culture, branding, or entrepreneurship.

A person who enjoys working with people and creating good experiences.

Someone organized enough to help make things happen, but flexible enough to work in a place that is still being built.

A collaborative mindset — we’re a small team, and we work closely together.

You don’t need to know everything already. But you should be ready to learn, take initiative, and roll up your sleeves.

How To Jump In

No need for a lengthy application. Just send us:

A short video introducing yourself, or

Your CV, and examples of relevant past work, projects, events, content, or ideas.

Let us know why you want to be part of building the listening bar — and what you would love to help create.

Note: This is an internship with a monthly compensation of €700. There may also be the possibility of taking paid shifts in the bar.
